kk.title: Ағылшында бір ғана «болашақ шақ» жоқ
kk.html: Болашақ бірнеше құрылыммен беріледі, таңдау сөйлеуші оқиғаны қалай көретініне байланысты: will — болжам, сол сәттегі шешім, ұсыныс, уәде; going to — ниет пен айғаққа негізделген болжам; present continuous — бекітілген жоспар; present simple — кесте.

ru.title: will vs going to
ru.html: I think it'll rain later (прогноз/мнение → will). Look at those clouds — it's going to rain (прогноз по признакам → going to). The phone's ringing. — I'll get it (решение сейчас → will). We're going to paint the house (заранее решённый план → going to).

kk.title: will vs going to
kk.html: I think it'll rain later (болжам/пікір → will). Look at those clouds — it's going to rain (қазіргі айғақтан болжам → going to). The phone's ringing. — I'll get it (сол сәттегі шешім → will). We're going to paint the house (алдын ала шешілген жоспар → going to).

ru.title: present continuous & present simple
ru.html: Present continuous = договорённость, часто с другим человеком, со временем/местом: I'm meeting Sam at six. Present simple = расписание (установлено учреждением): The train leaves at nine. Сравни: I'm seeing the dentist tomorrow (моя договорённость) vs The surgery opens at eight (его расписание).

kk.title: present continuous & present simple
kk.html: Present continuous = бекітілген жеке жоспар, жиі басқа адаммен, уақыт/орынмен: I'm meeting Sam at six. Present simple = кесте (мекеме бекіткен): The train leaves at nine. Салыстыр: I'm seeing the dentist tomorrow (менің жоспарым) vs The surgery opens at eight (оның кестесі).

ru.title: going to vs present continuous
ru.html: Оба — планы, но present continuous — более определённая договорённость (детали решены), going to может выражать ещё не устроенное намерение: I'm going to learn Italian (намерение) vs I'm starting Italian classes on Monday (устроено). С глаголами движения (go, come, leave) предпочтителен present continuous: We're going to Spain (не going to go).

kk.title: going to vs present continuous
kk.html: Екеуі де болашақ жоспар, бірақ present continuous нақтырақ бекітілген (егжей-тегжей шешілген), going to әлі реттелмеген ниетті білдіре алады: I'm going to learn Italian (ниет) vs I'm starting Italian classes on Monday (бекітілген). Қозғалыс етістіктерімен (go, come, leave) present continuous қолайлы: We're going to Spain (going to go емес).

ru.title: После when/if/as soon as — present, не will
ru.html: В придаточных времени и условия о будущем — present, не will: I'll call you when I arrive (не when I will arrive). If it rains, we'll stay in. Это касается when, as soon as, before, after, until, if, unless.

kk.title: when/if/as soon as-тен кейін — present, will емес
kk.html: Болашақ туралы уақыт және шарт бағыныңқыларында present қолданылады, will емес: I'll call you when I arrive (when I will arrive емес). If it rains, we'll stay in. Бұл when, as soon as, before, after, until, if, unless-ке қатысты.

ru.title: Регистр и shall
ru.html: shall сохраняется в основном в предложениях (I/we): Shall we go? Shall I help? и в формальных/юридических правилах (The tenant shall pay…). Для обычных прогнозов стандарт — will. be going to чуть более разговорно; will и present-формы — во всех регистрах.

kk.title: Регистр және shall
kk.html: shall негізінен ұсыныста (I/we) сақталады: Shall we go? Shall I help? және ресми/заңды ережелерде (The tenant shall pay…). Кәдімгі болжамда will стандарт. be going to сәл бейресми/ауызша; will мен present формалар барлық регистрде.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ I will meet Sam tomorrow at six (договорённость) → ✓ I'm meeting Sam… ✗ When I will finish → ✓ When I finish. ✗ Look — it will rain! (признаки сейчас) → ✓ it's going to rain!

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ I will meet Sam tomorrow at six (бекітілген) → ✓ I'm meeting Sam… ✗ When I will finish → ✓ When I finish. ✗ Look — it will rain! (қазіргі айғақ) → ✓ it's going to rain!

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• will = прогноз / решение сейчас / предложение / обещание.
  • going to = намерение и прогноз по признакам.
  • present continuous = договорённость; present simple = расписание.
  • После when/if/as soon as → present, не will.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• will = болжам / сол сәттегі шешім / ұсыныс / уәде.
  • going to = ниет пен айғаққа негізделген болжам.
  • present continuous = бекітілген жоспар; present simple = кесте.
  • when/if/as soon as-тен кейін → present, will емес.

activities.typeLabel: Which future form does the meaning need?
activities.prompt: Match each idea to the most natural future form.

items.t: I predict our team will win.
Those clouds — it's going to pour.
I'm having lunch with Ana at one.
— Someone's at the door. — I'll go.
We're going to renovate the kitchen.

activities.why: will = prediction/instant decision; going to = intention or evidence; present continuous = fixed arrangement; present simple = timetable.

items.s: I'm meeting the client tomorrow at ten.
The museum opens at nine on Sundays.
I will meet you tomorrow at ten. (already arranged)
When I will get home, I'll text you.

activities.why: a fixed arrangement → present continuous; time clauses → present tense (when I get home).
a prediction from what you can see now → 're going to knock over.

activities.before: I'll let you know as soon as I
activities.why: after ‘as soon as’ use the present, not will → hear.

activities.before: We can't come on Saturday — we
activities.why: a settled personal arrangement → present continuous 're visiting.
a scheduled event fixed by organisers → present simple.

activities.q: “— We've run out of milk. — Oh, ___ some on my way home.”
why: a present arrangement wasn't made in advance here.
Correct — a decision made at the moment of speaking → I'll get.
the present simple can't express this spontaneous decision.

activities.q: “I ___ get fit this year — in fact, I ___ the gym at seven tomorrow with a trainer.”
why: reversed: the vague resolution is the intention, the booked session the arrangement.
Correct — intention ‘'m going to’ + fixed arrangement ‘'m starting’ at a set time.
‘will … start’ misses both the intention and the arrangement nuance.

activities.prompt: Present continuous (personal arrangement) or present simple (timetable)?

items.t: She ___ her thesis on Friday (a set defence)
We ___ the new house next week

activities.why: Personal arrangements → present continuous; institutional schedules → present simple.
a future time clause takes the present → “when you land in Dubai.”

activities.prompt: I'm sure they will lose — the score is 4–0.
activities.why: a prediction from present evidence → 're going to lose.

activities.instruction: Rewrite this arrangement in the most natural form:
activities.prompt: My plan, already fixed with her, is to see Dr Amis at four.
activities.answer: i'm seeing dr amis at four
alts: i am seeing dr amis at four
i'm seeing the doctor at four
activities.why: a fixed personal arrangement → present continuous 'm seeing.

activities.scene: Choose the natural future form.

steps.npc: Are you free on Sunday?

options.t: Afraid not — I'm running a half-marathon. It starts at eight.
options.why: arrangement (present continuous) + timetable (present simple).

options.t: Afraid not — I run a half-marathon. It's starting at eight.
options.why: your arrangement → I'm running; the fixed start → it starts.

steps.npc: Fancy dinner after?

options.t: Sure — I'll book somewhere once I know my finish time.
options.why: instant decision (will) + time clause in the present (once I know).

options.t: Sure — I book somewhere once I'll know my finish time.
options.why: spontaneous offer → I'll book; time clause → once I know.

activities.why: Match the future form to your meaning: will for predictions/instant decisions, going to for intentions/evidence, present continuous for arrangements, present simple for timetables — and the present tense in future time and conditional clauses.

learn.title: Two 'shapes' of future time
learn.html:
Beyond will/going to, English can view a future event as in progress at a future moment, or as completed by a future moment.

This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Tokyo.in progress at a future point → future continuous
By Friday I'll have finished the report.completed before a future point → future perfect

learn.title: Form
learn.html: Future continuous: will be + -ing (I'll be working). Future perfect: will have + past participle (I'll have left). There is also a future perfect continuous: will have been + -ing — duration up to a future point (By May I'll have been living here for a year).

learn.title: Future continuous — uses
learn.html:
• In progress at a future time: Don't call at eight — we'll be having dinner.
  • Something planned/expected as a matter of course: I'll be seeing Tom later, so I'll tell him. (as part of the normal run of events)
  • Polite / tactful enquiries: Will you be using the car tonight? (softer than “Are you going to use…?”, it doesn't press for a decision)

learn.title: Future perfect — uses
learn.html:
will have + past participle looks back from a future point at something already completed. It usually needs a time marker (by, by the time, before, in … time):

By 2030 the company will have doubled in size. · By the time you read this, I'll have left. · In an hour they'll have landed.

learn.title: Future perfect continuous — duration to a future point
learn.html:
Stresses how long an activity will have lasted by a future moment: Next month I'll have been working here for ten years. · By midnight the team will have been coding for twelve hours straight.

Use the simple future perfect for a completed result/amount, the continuous for the ongoing activity/duration.

learn.title: Present tense in the time clause
learn.html: As always, the time clause stays in the present, not the future: By the time we arrive, the show will have started (not will arrive). The future perfect goes in the main clause.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ At ten I will fly. (meaning: in mid-flight) → ✓ At ten I'll be flying.

✗ Until June I will finish it. → ✓ By June I'll have finished it. (by, not until, for a deadline)

✗ By the time you will arrive… → ✓ By the time you arrive…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Future continuous (will be + -ing) = in progress at a future time; also for tactful enquiries.
  • Future perfect (will have + p.p.) = completed by a future time (needs by/by the time).
  • Future perfect continuous = duration up to a future point.
  • Keep the time clause in the present (By the time we arrive…).

ru.title: Две «формы» будущего времени
ru.html: Кроме will/going to, английский может видеть будущее событие как в процессе в будущий момент или как завершённое к будущему моменту: This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Tokyo (в процессе). By Friday I'll have finished the report (завершено).

kk.title: Болашақ уақыттың екі «пішіні»
kk.html: will/going to-дан бөлек, ағылшын болашақ оқиғаны болашақ сәтте үдерісте немесе болашақ сәтке дейін аяқталған етіп көре алады: This time tomorrow I'll be flying to Tokyo (үдерісте). By Friday I'll have finished the report (аяқталған).

ru.title: Форма
ru.html: Future continuous: will be + -ing. Future perfect: will have + past participle. Есть и future perfect continuous: will have been + -ing — длительность до будущей точки (By May I'll have been living here for a year).

kk.title: Форма
kk.html: Future continuous: will be + -ing. Future perfect: will have + past participle. Тағы future perfect continuous бар: will have been + -ing — болашақ нүктеге дейінгі ұзақтық (By May I'll have been living here for a year).

ru.title: Future continuous — использование
ru.html: В процессе в будущий момент: Don't call at eight — we'll be having dinner. Как часть обычного хода событий: I'll be seeing Tom later, so I'll tell him. Тактичный вопрос: Will you be using the car tonight? (не давит на решение).

kk.title: Future continuous — қолданысы
kk.html: Болашақ сәтте үдерісте: Don't call at eight — we'll be having dinner. Оқиғалардың қалыпты ағымының бөлігі ретінде жоспарланған: I'll be seeing Tom later, so I'll tell him. Сыпайы сұрақ: Will you be using the car tonight? (шешімге қыспайды).

ru.title: Future perfect — использование
ru.html: will have + past participle смотрит из будущей точки на завершённое. Обычно нужен маркер времени (by, by the time, before, in … time): By 2030 the company will have doubled in size. By the time you read this, I'll have left.

kk.title: Future perfect — қолданысы
kk.html: will have + past participle болашақ нүктеден аяқталған нәрсеге қарайды. Әдетте уақыт белгісін қажет етеді (by, by the time, before, in … time): By 2030 the company will have doubled in size. By the time you read this, I'll have left.

ru.title: Future perfect continuous — длительность
ru.html: Подчёркивает, как долго действие будет длиться к будущему моменту: Next month I'll have been working here for ten years. By midnight the team will have been coding for twelve hours. Завершённый результат → simple; процесс/длительность → continuous.

kk.title: Future perfect continuous — ұзақтық
kk.html: Болашақ сәтке дейін әрекеттің қанша уақыт созылғанын баса көрсетеді: Next month I'll have been working here for ten years. By midnight the team will have been coding for twelve hours. Аяқталған нәтиже/мөлшер → simple; жалғасқан әрекет/ұзақтық → continuous.

ru.title: Придаточное времени — present
ru.html: Придаточное времени в present, не в будущем: By the time we arrive, the show will have started (не will arrive). Future perfect — в главном предложении.

kk.title: Уақыт бағыныңқысы — present
kk.html: Уақыт бағыныңқысы present-те қалады, болашақ емес: By the time we arrive, the show will have started (will arrive емес). Future perfect басты сөйлемде тұрады.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ At ten I will fly (в значении «в полёте») → ✓ At ten I'll be flying. ✗ Until June I will finish it → ✓ By June I'll have finished it (для срока by, не until). ✗ By the time you will arrive → ✓ By the time you arrive.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ At ten I will fly (ұшу үстінде мағынасында) → ✓ At ten I'll be flying. ✗ Until June I will finish it → ✓ By June I'll have finished it (мерзімге until емес, by). ✗ By the time you will arrive → ✓ By the time you arrive.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Future continuous (will be + -ing) = в процессе в будущий момент; и для тактичных вопросов.
  • Future perfect (will have + p.p.) = завершено к будущему моменту (нужен by/by the time).
  • Future perfect continuous = длительность до будущей точки.
  • Придаточное времени — в present.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Future continuous (will be + -ing) = болашақ сәтте үдерісте; сыпайы сұраққа да.
  • Future perfect (will have + p.p.) = болашақ сәтке дейін аяқталған (by/by the time қажет).
  • Future perfect continuous = болашақ нүктеге дейінгі ұзақтық.
  • Уақыт бағыныңқысын present-те сақтаңыз.

activities.prompt: Sort each future form by the shape of the event.
buckets: in progress at a future point (continuous)

items.t: I'll be lying on a beach.
I'll have submitted it by noon.
They'll be waiting at the gate.
We'll have sold out by then.

activities.why: Future continuous frames an action in progress; future perfect frames it as completed before a future point.

items.s: By 2040, sea levels will have risen further.
This time next week I'll be relaxing in Bali.
By the time you will arrive, I'll have left.
At eight I will have dinner. (meaning: mid-meal)

activities.why: time clauses use the present (you arrive); ‘mid-meal’ needs the continuous (I'll be having dinner).

activities.typeLabel: In progress at a future time
activities.why: an action in progress at that future time → future continuous 'll be putting.

activities.before: By the end of the month, we
activities.why: completed before a future point → future perfect 'll have upgraded.
‘be’ is a state → future perfect simple 'll have been for duration (not ‘been being’).
time clause in the present (you get) + future in the main clause.

activities.q: You want to borrow the car without pressuring anyone. Which is most tactful?
options: Are you going to use the car tonight?
Will you be using the car tonight?
Do you use the car tonight?
why: this pushes for a stated intention/decision.
Correct — the future continuous asks about the normal course of events, without pressing → most tactful.
the present simple is wrong for a specific future occasion.

activities.q: “By the time the guests leave, I ___ for six hours straight.”
why: the simple states a completed amount, not the exhausting length of the activity.
Correct — stressing how long the activity will have lasted → future perfect continuous will have been cooking.

activities.prompt: Which word fits a future-perfect deadline vs a continuing situation?
buckets: until (up to a point → simple future)
activities.why: ‘by’ marks a deadline for completion (future perfect); ‘until’ marks a continuing action up to a point.
a completion deadline needs ‘by’, not ‘until’ → “By next year they will have built the bridge.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite to show the action in progress at that time:
activities.prompt: At midnight, the flight departs. (I'll be on it, in the air)
alts: this time at midnight i'll be flying
activities.why: an action in progress at a future moment → future continuous 'll be flying.

activities.prompt: I'll finish the course. Then it'll be June.
activities.answer: i'll have finished the course by june
alts: by june i'll have finished the course
i will have finished the course by june
activities.why: completed before a future point → future perfect 'll have finished + by June.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: planning around a busy day
activities.scene: Choose the right future 'shape'.

steps.npc: Can we meet at four tomorrow?

options.t: Sorry, I'll be interviewing candidates all afternoon.
options.why: an activity in progress at that future time → future continuous.

options.t: Sorry, I'll interview candidates all afternoon at four.
options.why: to say it's ongoing at four, use the future continuous: I'll be interviewing.

steps.npc: Will the report be ready by Friday?

options.t: Definitely — I'll have sent it well before then.
options.why: completed before a future deadline → future perfect.

options.t: Definitely — I'll send it until Friday, so it'll have been done.
options.why: a deadline needs ‘by’: I'll have sent it by Friday.

activities.why: Use the future continuous for an action in progress at a future time (and tactful enquiries), and the future perfect for something completed by a future deadline — keeping time clauses in the present.

learn.title: Precise, formal & 'looking-back' futures
learn.html:
Beyond the everyday forms, B2 adds structures for official plans, imminent actions, and the future seen from a past viewpoint.

  • be to — formal arrangements, instructions, official plans
  • be about to — an action on the point of happening
  • was going to / would / was to — the "future in the past"

learn.title: be to + infinitive — official & planned
learn.html:
be to + base verb announces fixed, often official arrangements, orders and plans — common in news, notices and instructions.

The President is to visit Kazakhstan next month. · You are to report to reception on arrival. · No one is to leave the room.

In conditionals it can mean "if X is to happen" (for X to be possible): If we are to succeed, we must act now.

learn.title: be about to & be on the point of
learn.html:
be about to + base verb = something will happen very soon, at any moment: Hurry — the train is about to leave. · I was about to call you.

More emphatic/formal: be on the point of + -ing: She was on the point of giving up when help arrived. Also be due to for scheduled events: The results are due to be announced at noon.

learn.title: Future in the past
learn.html:
To describe a future as seen from a past moment (a plan, expectation or prediction made in the past):

We were going to drive, but the car broke down.a past intention (often unrealised)
She said she would call back.a future promise reported from the past
The talks were to reshape the region.a formal ‘future in the past’ (often in narrative)
I was about to leave when he arrived.on the point of, in the past

learn.title: Unfulfilled plans: was going to / was to have
learn.html:
was going to + verb often implies the plan didn't happen: I was going to apologise, but I lost my nerve.

was to have + past participle makes the non-fulfilment explicit: The bridge was to have opened in May (but it didn't).

learn.title: Register
learn.html: be to and be to have are formal / written (news, official notices, reports). be about to is neutral. was going to is everyday and spoken. Choose be to for officialdom, was going to for casual past plans.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ The minister is to visiting the region. → ✓ is to visit (base verb after be to).

✗ I am about to leaving. → ✓ I'm about to leave.

✗ He said he will help. (reported from the past) → ✓ He said he would help.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• be to + base = official/planned future; also "if we are to…".
  • be about to / on the point of = imminent action; be due to = scheduled.
  • Future in the past: was going to / would / was to / was about to.
  • was going to & was to have often signal an unfulfilled plan.

ru.title: Точное, формальное и «взгляд назад» будущее
ru.html: Помимо обычных форм, B2 добавляет структуры для официальных планов, неминуемых действий и будущего с точки зрения прошлого: be to — официальный план/приказ; be about to — действие вот-вот; was going to / would / was to — «будущее в прошлом».

kk.title: Нақты, ресми және «артқа қараған» болашақ
kk.html: Кәдімгі формалардан бөлек, B2 ресми жоспар, жақын арада болатын әрекет және өткен көзқарастан көрінген болашақ үшін құрылымдар қосады: be to — ресми жоспар/бұйрық; be about to — тап-тұл болатын әрекет; was going to / would / was to — «өткендегі болашақ».

ru.title: be to + infinitive — официальное/плановое
ru.html: be to + база объявляет фиксированные, часто официальные планы/приказы (новости, объявления): The President is to visit Kazakhstan. You are to report to reception. No one is to leave. В условии «если X должно произойти»: If we are to succeed, we must act now.

kk.title: be to + infinitive — ресми/жоспарлы
kk.html: be to + негізгі етістік бекітілген, жиі ресми жоспар/бұйрықты хабарлайды (жаңалық, хабарландыру): The President is to visit Kazakhstan. You are to report to reception. No one is to leave. Шартта «егер X болу керек болса»: If we are to succeed, we must act now.

ru.title: be about to & be on the point of
ru.html: be about to + база = вот-вот, в любой момент: the train is about to leave. I was about to call you. Эмфатичнее/формальнее: be on the point of + -ing: on the point of giving up. Для расписания be due to: The results are due to be announced at noon.

kk.title: be about to & be on the point of
kk.html: be about to + негізгі етістік = өте жақын арада, кез келген сәтте: the train is about to leave. I was about to call you. Күштірек/ресми: be on the point of + -ing: on the point of giving up. Кестелі оқиғаға be due to: The results are due to be announced at noon.

ru.title: Будущее в прошлом
ru.html: Будущее с точки зрения прошлого (план/ожидание в прошлом): We were going to drive, but the car broke down (часто неосуществлённое). She said she would call back (обещание из прошлого). The talks were to reshape the region (формально). I was about to leave when he arrived.

kk.title: Өткендегі болашақ
kk.html: Өткен сәттен көрінген болашақ (өткенде жасалған жоспар/күту): We were going to drive, but the car broke down (жиі орындалмаған). She said she would call back (өткеннен хабарланған уәде). The talks were to reshape the region (ресми). I was about to leave when he arrived.

ru.title: Неосуществлённый план: was going to / was to have
ru.html: was going to + verb часто значит, что план не сбылся: I was going to apologise, but I lost my nerve. was to have + past participle делает несбывшееся явным: The bridge was to have opened in May (но не открылся).

kk.title: Орындалмаған жоспар: was going to / was to have
kk.html: was going to + verb жиі жоспар болмағанын білдіреді: I was going to apologise, but I lost my nerve. was to have + past participle орындалмауды анық етеді: The bridge was to have opened in May (бірақ ашылмады).

ru.title: Регистр
ru.html: be to и be to have — формально/письменно (новости, объявления, отчёты). be about to — нейтрально. was going to — обиходно/устно. Для официоза be to, для обычных прошлых планов was going to.

kk.title: Регистр
kk.html: be to және be to have — ресми/жазба (жаңалық, хабарландыру, есеп). be about to — бейтарап. was going to — күнделікті/ауызша. Ресмиге be to, кәдімгі өткен жоспарға was going to.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ is to visiting → ✓ is to visit (после be to — база). ✗ I am about to leaving → ✓ I'm about to leave. ✗ He said he will help → ✓ He said he would help.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ is to visiting → ✓ is to visit (be to-дан кейін негізгі етістік). ✗ I am about to leaving → ✓ I'm about to leave. ✗ He said he will help → ✓ He said he would help.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• be to + база = официальное/плановое будущее; и «if we are to…».
  • be about to / on the point of = неминуемое; be due to = по расписанию.
  • Будущее в прошлом: was going to / would / was to / was about to.
  • was going to & was to have часто = несбывшийся план.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• be to + база = ресми/жоспарлы болашақ; «if we are to…» да.
  • be about to / on the point of = жақын әрекет; be due to = кестелі.
  • Өткендегі болашақ: was going to / would / was to / was about to.
  • was going to & was to have жиі орындалмаған жоспарды білдіреді.

activities.prompt: Match each sentence to the structure it uses.
buckets: future in the past (was going to / would)

items.t: The minister is to open the summit.
Quick — the film is about to start.
We were going to fly, but it was too pricey.
I was about to text you!

activities.why: be to = official plans/orders; be about to = on the point of; was going to/would = a future viewed from the past.

items.s: You are to submit the form by Friday.
The train is due to arrive at six.
I am about to leaving now.
He said he will call me. (reported)

activities.why: base verb after ‘about to’ (about to leave); reported future → would call.

activities.before: According to the notice, the library
activities.after: close for renovation next week. (be to)
activities.why: a formal, announced plan → is to close (base verb follows).
on the point of happening → 's about to announce.

activities.after: phone you, but then you walked in. (be going to / phone)
activities.why: a past intention that didn't happen → was going to phone.
‘if … are to …’ = for this to be possible.

activities.typeLabel: Register: which fits a news report?
activities.q: A formal news bulletin announcing a state visit would say:
options: The King's gonna visit next week.
The King is going to visit, probably.
The King is to visit next week.
why: ‘gonna’ is very informal.
the hedge ‘probably’ is wrong for an official announcement.
Correct — the formal announced plan → is to visit.

activities.q: Which sentence clearly implies the concert did NOT take place?
options: The concert is to take place on Saturday.
The concert was to have taken place on Saturday.
The concert takes place on Saturday.
why: ‘is to’ describes a future plan.
Correct — ‘was to have taken place’ signals it was planned but didn't happen.
the present simple states a scheduled event.

activities.prompt: Sort each future structure by register.

items.t: was to have opened in May

activities.why: be to / was to have belong to formal writing; be about to / was going to are everyday.
‘be to’ takes the base verb → “All staff are to attend the briefing.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a formal, announced plan:
activities.prompt: The company will launch the product in June. (official announcement)
activities.answer: the company is to launch the product in june
alts: the company is to launch the product in june.
activities.why: an official plan → is to launch.

activities.instruction: Rewrite as an unfulfilled past plan:
activities.prompt: My plan was to study medicine, but I chose law instead.
activities.answer: i was going to study medicine, but i chose law instead
alts: i was going to study medicine but i chose law
i was going to study medicine, but i chose law
activities.why: a past intention that didn't happen → was going to study.

activities.scene: Choose the precise future structure.

steps.npc: Wasn't the new terminal supposed to be open by now?

options.t: It was to have opened in spring, but the works overran.
options.why: was to have + p.p. = a formal plan that wasn't fulfilled.

options.t: It is to have opened in spring, but the works overran.
options.why: a past unfulfilled plan → was to have opened.

steps.npc: So when will it actually open?

options.t: They're due to announce a new date any day now.
options.why: be due to = scheduled/expected.

options.t: They're about to announcing a new date.
options.why: ‘about to’ takes the base verb: about to announce (or be due to announce).

activities.why: Use be to for official/planned futures, be about to / be due to for imminent or scheduled events, and was going to / would / was to (have) for the future seen from the past — often an unfulfilled plan.

learn.title: Guessing with confidence
learn.html:
Modals let us show how certain we are about a situation, based on evidence or logic. This is deduction, not fact.

  • must — I'm almost certain it's true (logical conclusion)
  • can't / couldn't — I'm almost certain it's not true
  • might / may / could — it's possible (I'm not sure)

She's not answering — she must be asleep. · He can't be serious. · They might be stuck in traffic.

learn.title: Present deduction — form
learn.html:
modal + base verb, or modal + be + -ing for something in progress: She must know the answer. · He must be joking.

The negative of deductive must is can't (not mustn't): It must be true → It can't be true. (mustn't = prohibition, a different meaning.)

learn.title: Past deduction — modal + have + p.p.
learn.html:
To deduce about the past, use modal + have + past participle:

She must have left already.near-certain it happened
He can't / couldn't have known.near-certain it didn't happen
They might / may / could have missed the train.a past possibility

Progressive past: She must have been sleeping when I called.

learn.title: Degrees of certainty
learn.html:
Think of a scale from certain-yes to certain-no:

  • must (≈95% yes) · will (confident prediction: That'll be the postman)
  • should / ought to (expectation: They should be home by now)
  • may / might / could (≈50% possible)
  • might not / may not (possibly not) · can't / couldn't (≈95% no)

learn.title: could vs can, might vs may
learn.html:
For possibility, could / might / may are largely interchangeable, but can is not used for a specific deduction: He can be at home. → He could / might be at home.

could have + p.p. can also express an unrealised possibility or mild reproach: You could have told me! (but you didn't) — see Unit 14.

learn.title: Typical learner errors & register
learn.html:
✗ It mustn't be true. (meaning: surely not) → ✓ It can't be true.

✗ She must knew it. → ✓ She must have known it.

✗ He can be tired. (deduction) → ✓ He must / might be tired.

may is a touch more formal than might; could is neutral and very common in speech.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• must = near-certain yes; can't = near-certain no; might/may/could = possible.
  • Past: modal + have + past participle (must have gone, can't have seen).
  • The negative of deductive ‘must’ is ‘can't’, not ‘mustn't’.
  • Don't use ‘can’ for a specific deduction — use could/might.

ru.title: Уверенные догадки
ru.html: Модальные показывают, насколько мы уверены, опираясь на признаки/логику (это догадка, не факт): must — почти уверен, что так; can't/couldn't — почти уверен, что не так; might/may/could — возможно. She's not answering — she must be asleep. He can't be serious.

kk.title: Сенімділікпен болжау
kk.html: Модальдар айғаққа/логикаға сүйеніп қаншалықты сенімді екенімізді көрсетеді (бұл — болжам, факт емес): must — шындыққа сенімдімін; can't/couldn't — шын емесіне сенімдімін; might/may/could — мүмкін. She's not answering — she must be asleep. He can't be serious. They might be stuck in traffic.

ru.title: Догадка о настоящем — форма
ru.html: модальный + база или для процесса модальный + be + -ing: She must know the answer. He must be joking. Отрицание догадочного must — can't (не mustn't): It must be true → It can't be true. (mustn't = запрет, другое значение.)

kk.title: Осы шақтағы болжам — форма
kk.html: модаль + негізгі етістік немесе үдеріс үшін модаль + be + -ing: She must know the answer. He must be joking. Болжамдық must-тың болымсызы — can't (mustn't емес): It must be true → It can't be true. (mustn't = тыйым, басқа мағына.)

ru.title: Догадка о прошлом — modal + have + p.p.
ru.html: Догадка о прошлом: модальный + have + past participle: She must have left already (уверен, что было). He can't/couldn't have known (уверен, что не было). They might/may/could have missed the train (прошлая возможность). Процесс: She must have been sleeping when I called.

kk.title: Өткен болжам — modal + have + p.p.
kk.html: Өткен туралы болжам: модаль + have + past participle: She must have left already (болғанына сенімді). He can't/couldn't have known (болмағанына сенімді). They might/may/could have missed the train (өткен мүмкіндік). Үдеріс: She must have been sleeping when I called.

ru.title: Степени уверенности
ru.html: Шкала от уверенно-да до уверенно-нет: must (≈95% да) · will (уверенный прогноз: That'll be the postman) · should/ought to (ожидание: They should be home by now) · may/might/could (≈50%) · might not/may not (возможно нет) · can't/couldn't (≈95% нет).

kk.title: Сенімділік дәрежелері
kk.html: Сенімді-иә-ден сенімді-жоқ-қа шкала: must (≈95% иә) · will (сенімді болжам: That'll be the postman) · should/ought to (күту: They should be home by now) · may/might/could (≈50%) · might not/may not (мүмкін жоқ) · can't/couldn't (≈95% жоқ).

ru.title: could vs can, might vs may
ru.html: Для возможности could/might/may взаимозаменяемы, но для конкретной догадки can не используется: He can be at home → He could/might be at home. could have + p.p. выражает и несбывшуюся возможность/лёгкий упрёк: You could have told me! — раздел 14.

kk.title: could vs can, might vs may
kk.html: Мүмкіндікке could/might/may көбіне ауысады, бірақ нақты болжамға can қолданылмайды: He can be at home → He could/might be at home. could have + p.p. орындалмаған мүмкіндік/жеңіл кінәні де білдіреді: You could have told me! (айтпадың) — 14-бөлім.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки и регистр
ru.html: ✗ It mustn't be true (наверняка нет) → ✓ It can't be true. ✗ She must knew it → ✓ She must have known it. ✗ He can be tired (догадка) → ✓ He must/might be tired. may чуть формальнее might; could — нейтрально, часто в речи.

kk.title: Жиі қателер мен регистр
kk.html: ✗ It mustn't be true (сірә жоқ) → ✓ It can't be true. ✗ She must knew it → ✓ She must have known it. ✗ He can be tired (болжам) → ✓ He must/might be tired. may — might-тан сәл ресми; could — бейтарап, ауызекіде жиі.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• must = уверенно да; can't = уверенно нет; might/may/could = возможно.
  • Прошлое: modal + have + past participle.
  • Отрицание догадочного ‘must’ — ‘can't’, не ‘mustn't’.
  • Для конкретной догадки — не ‘can’, а could/might.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• must = сенімді иә; can't = сенімді жоқ; might/may/could = мүмкін.
  • Өткен: modal + have + past participle.
  • Болжамдық ‘must’-тың болымсызы — ‘can't’, ‘mustn't’ емес.
  • Нақты болжамға ‘can’ емес, could/might.

activities.prompt: Sort each modal by the certainty it shows.
activities.why: must = near-certain yes; can't/couldn't = near-certain no; might/could = possible.

items.s: The lights are off — they must be out.
He can't have finished already; he only just started.
It mustn't be true — I don't believe it.
She can be French, judging by her accent.

activities.why: the negative of deductive must is can't (can't be true); a specific deduction uses could/might, not can.

activities.before: Their car's in the drive, so they
activities.why: a confident logical conclusion → must be at home.

activities.after: the manager — she's on holiday in Spain. (can't / be)
activities.why: near-certain it's not her → can't be (not mustn't).

activities.before: The grass is wet, so it
activities.why: a confident conclusion about the past → must have rained.
a past possibility → might + have + past participle.

activities.q: “He's been studying all year. He ___ the exam — but we won't know until Friday.”
why: ‘must have passed’ overstates a certainty we don't have yet.
‘can't have passed’ contradicts a year of study.
Correct — a genuine possibility, still unknown → might have passed.

activities.q: “I called at nine and got no answer. She ___ asleep by then.”
why: the deduction is about a past moment (nine), not now.
Correct — past deduction → must have been asleep.
‘can't be’ reverses the intended meaning.

activities.prompt: Which modal use is deduction, and which is prohibition/permission?
activities.why: ‘can't/must’ can mean certainty (deduction) or rules (permission/obligation) — context decides.
past deduction → modal + have + p.p. → “She must have known about the change.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a confident past deduction:
activities.prompt: I'm sure they got lost.
activities.why: near-certain about the past → must have got lost.

activities.instruction: Rewrite to show it's almost certainly NOT true:
activities.prompt: Maybe he saw the message. (but you're sure he didn't)
activities.answer: he can't have seen the message
alts: he couldn't have seen the message
activities.why: near-certain negative about the past → can't have seen.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: a mystery at work
activities.scene: Match the modal to your level of certainty.

steps.npc: Someone's used my computer — the files are all moved.

options.t: It must have been the night cleaner; no one else has the key.
options.why: a confident past deduction → must have been.

options.t: It can have been the night cleaner; no one else has the key.
options.why: a specific deduction doesn't use ‘can’ → must have been / could have been.

steps.npc: Could it have been Sam?

options.t: No — he can't have done it; he was off sick all week.
options.why: near-certain negative about the past → can't have done.

options.t: No — he mustn't have done it; he was off sick.
options.why: the negative of deductive must is can't: he can't have done it.

activities.why: Use must/can't for near-certainty, might/may/could for possibility, and modal + have + past participle for the past — remembering that the negative of deductive ‘must’ is ‘can't’.

learn.title: Who imposes the obligation?
learn.html:
English distinguishes obligations by where they come from. must often expresses the speaker's own authority or strong feeling; have to usually expresses an external obligation (rules, circumstances).

I must phone my mother.I feel it's important (internal)
I have to wear a uniform at work.the rules require it (external)

learn.title: Form & the missing tenses
learn.html:
must has no past or future form — use have to for those: Yesterday I had to work late. · You'll have to wait.

have got to is a common spoken alternative to have to: I've got to go. Questions/negatives of have to use do: Do you have to go? · I don't have to work Sundays.

learn.title: The crucial negatives: mustn't vs don't have to
learn.html:
These mean opposite things:

You mustn't tell anyone.prohibition — it's forbidden
You don't have to tell anyone.no obligation — it's optional

So mustn't = "don't do it"; don't have to = "you can if you want, but you needn't".

learn.title: need & needn't
learn.html:
need works two ways: as an ordinary verb (You don't need to come) and as a modal (You needn't come) — both = no necessity.

needn't have + p.p. = you did it but it wasn't necessary: You needn't have cooked — we ate on the way. Contrast didn't need to + verb, which usually means it wasn't necessary and (often) you didn't do it: I didn't need to pay (so I didn't).

learn.title: Permission: can / be allowed to / may
learn.html:
can (informal), may (formal), be allowed to (rules) express permission: Can I leave early? · Guests may use the pool. · We aren't allowed to park here.

be allowed to supplies the past/future can lacks: As a child I wasn't allowed to stay up late.

learn.title: Register & should/ought to
learn.html: have to is neutral; must in obligations can sound strong or official (notices: Passengers must…). For softer necessity/advice use should / ought to / had better (Unit 14). be supposed to = a mild expected obligation: You're supposed to sign in.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Yesterday I must work late. → ✓ I had to work late.

✗ You mustn't come if you're busy. (meaning: no need) → ✓ You don't have to come.

✗ I didn't must pay. → ✓ I didn't have to pay.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• must = internal/speaker's obligation; have to = external obligation. Past/future → have to.
  • mustn't = prohibition; don't have to / needn't = no obligation.
  • needn't have done = it was done unnecessarily; didn't need to = it wasn't necessary.
  • Permission: can / may / be allowed to (past & future → be allowed to).

ru.title: Кто налагает обязанность?
ru.html: Английский различает обязанности по тому, откуда они. must часто — собственное чувство/авторитет говорящего; have to — внешняя обязанность (правила, обстоятельства). I must phone my mother (я считаю это важным). I have to wear a uniform at work (требуют правила).

kk.title: Міндетті кім жүктейді?
kk.html: Ағылшын міндетті қайдан шыққанына қарай ажыратады. must жиі сөйлеушінің өз сезімі/беделі; have to — сыртқы міндет (ереже, жағдай). I must phone my mother (маңызды деп сезінемін). I have to wear a uniform at work (ереже талап етеді).

ru.title: Форма и отсутствующие времена
ru.html: У must нет прошедшего/будущего — для них have to: Yesterday I had to work late. You'll have to wait. В речи have got to: I've got to go. Вопрос/отрицание have to через do: Do you have to go? I don't have to work Sundays.

kk.title: Форма және жетіспейтін шақтар
kk.html: must-тың өткен/болашақ формасы жоқ — оларға have to: Yesterday I had to work late. You'll have to wait. Ауызекіде have got to: I've got to go. have to-ның сұрақ/болымсызы do-мен: Do you have to go? I don't have to work Sundays.

ru.title: Ключевые отрицания: mustn't vs don't have to
ru.html: Они противоположны: You mustn't tell anyone (запрет — нельзя) vs You don't have to tell anyone (нет обязанности — по желанию). Итак mustn't = «не делай»; don't have to = «можно, но не обязательно».

kk.title: Маңызды болымсыздар: mustn't vs don't have to
kk.html: Бұлар қарама-қарсы: You mustn't tell anyone (тыйым — болмайды) vs You don't have to tell anyone (міндет жоқ — қаласаң). Сонымен mustn't = «істеме»; don't have to = «қаласаң істе, бірақ міндет емес».

ru.title: need & needn't
ru.html: need двояко: обычный глагол (You don't need to come) и модальный (You needn't come) — оба «не нужно». needn't have + p.p. = сделал, но было не нужно: You needn't have cooked. Сравни didn't need to + verb — не было необходимости (часто и не сделал): I didn't need to pay (поэтому не платил).

kk.title: need & needn't
kk.html: need екі түрлі: кәдімгі етістік (You don't need to come) және модаль (You needn't come) — екеуі де «қажет емес». needn't have + p.p. = істедің, бірақ қажет емес еді: You needn't have cooked. Салыстыр didn't need to + verb — қажет емес еді (жиі істемедің де): I didn't need to pay (сондықтан төлемедім).

ru.title: Разрешение: can / be allowed to / may
ru.html: can (неформ.), may (формально), be allowed to (правила): Can I leave early? Guests may use the pool. We aren't allowed to park here. be allowed to даёт прошедшее/будущее, которых нет у can: As a child I wasn't allowed to stay up late.

kk.title: Рұқсат: can / be allowed to / may
kk.html: can (бейресми), may (ресми), be allowed to (ереже): Can I leave early? Guests may use the pool. We aren't allowed to park here. be allowed to can-ның жоқ өткен/болашағын береді: As a child I wasn't allowed to stay up late.

ru.title: Регистр и should/ought to
ru.html: have to нейтрально; must в обязанностях звучит сильно/официально (Passengers must…). Для мягкой необходимости/совета — should/ought to/had better (раздел 14). be supposed to = мягкая ожидаемая обязанность: You're supposed to sign in.

kk.title: Регистр және should/ought to
kk.html: have to — бейтарап; міндеттегі must күшті/ресми естіледі (Passengers must…). Жұмсақ қажеттілік/кеңеске should/ought to/had better (14-бөлім). be supposed to = жеңіл күтілген міндет: You're supposed to sign in.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Yesterday I must work late → ✓ I had to work late. ✗ You mustn't come if you're busy (в значении «не нужно») → ✓ You don't have to come. ✗ I didn't must pay → ✓ I didn't have to pay.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Yesterday I must work late → ✓ I had to work late. ✗ You mustn't come if you're busy (қажет емес мағынасында) → ✓ You don't have to come. ✗ I didn't must pay → ✓ I didn't have to pay.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• must = внутренняя/говорящего обязанность; have to = внешняя. Прошлое/будущее → have to.
  • mustn't = запрет; don't have to / needn't = нет обязанности.
  • needn't have done = сделано зря; didn't need to = не было необходимости.
  • Разрешение: can / may / be allowed to (прошлое/будущее → be allowed to).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• must = ішкі/сөйлеуші міндеті; have to = сыртқы. Өткен/болашақ → have to.
  • mustn't = тыйым; don't have to / needn't = міндет жоқ.
  • needn't have done = қажетсіз істелді; didn't need to = қажет емес еді.
  • Рұқсат: can / may / be allowed to (өткен/болашақ → be allowed to).

activities.prompt: Sort each sentence by meaning.

items.t: You mustn't use your phone here.
You don't have to finish it today.
Visitors are not allowed past this point.
You must not feed the animals.
We don't need to book in advance.

activities.why: mustn't / not allowed = forbidden; don't have to / needn't = optional.

items.s: Yesterday I had to leave early.
You'll have to show ID at the door.
Yesterday I must leave early.
I didn't must pay the fee.

activities.why: must has no past/future → had to / will have to / didn't have to.

activities.before: The lift was broken, so we
activities.why: an external obligation in the past → had to take (must has no past).
no obligation → needn't / don't need to.

activities.before: When I was a student, we
activities.after: eat in the library. (not / be allowed to)
activities.why: can has no past for permission → weren't allowed to.
a prohibition → mustn't + base verb.

activities.typeLabel: needn't have vs didn't need to
activities.q: “I ___ cooked so much — half the guests had already eaten.”
why: Correct — you DID cook, but it turned out unnecessary → needn't have cooked.
‘didn't need to’ usually implies you didn't do it — but you did.
‘mustn't have’ isn't used for this meaning.

activities.q: On an official sign, which is most natural?
options: Passengers don't have to fasten seatbelts.
why: ‘have to’ is fine but less typical on formal notices.
Correct — official notices favour the strong, rule-giving must.
this states no obligation — the opposite of a safety rule.

activities.typeLabel: Which meaning does the modal carry?
activities.prompt: Sort by function.

items.t: Guests are allowed to bring pets.

activities.why: must = obligation; may/be allowed to = permission; don't have to = no necessity.
the meaning is ‘it's optional’, not ‘forbidden’ → “You don't have to pay now.”

activities.prompt: I must renew my passport. (last month)
activities.answer: i had to renew my passport last month
alts: last month i had to renew my passport
activities.why: must has no past → had to renew.

activities.instruction: Rewrite to say it was done unnecessarily:
activities.prompt: You brought your umbrella, but it didn't rain.
activities.answer: you needn't have brought your umbrella
alts: you needn't have brought your umbrella.
activities.why: an unnecessary completed action → needn't have brought.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: a new job's rules
activities.scene: Choose the correct obligation form.

steps.npc: Is there a dress code?

options.t: You have to wear smart clothes on client days, but you don't have to on Fridays.
options.why: external rule (have to) + no obligation (don't have to).

options.t: You must wear smart clothes on client days, but you mustn't on Fridays.
options.why: ‘mustn't on Fridays’ would forbid smart clothes; you mean it's optional → don't have to.

steps.npc: Anything I really can't do?

options.t: You mustn't share client data — that's strictly forbidden.
options.why: prohibition → mustn't.

options.t: You don't have to share client data — it's strictly forbidden.
options.why: a strict prohibition → mustn't, not ‘don't have to’ (which sounds optional).

activities.why: Distinguish must (internal) from have to (external, and the source of past/future forms), and above all mustn't (forbidden) from don't have to/needn't (optional); use be allowed to for permission across tenses.

learn.title: More than 'ability'
learn.html:
Modals of ability and willingness carry subtle differences at B2. can/could cover ability and possibility; will/would cover willingness, refusal and characteristic behaviour.

  • can — present ability/possibility · could — past/general ability, polite requests
  • will — willingness, offers, characteristic present habit · would — past habit, refusal

learn.title: could vs was able to / managed to
learn.html:
For general past ability, use could: I could swim when I was five. But for a specific past achievement (you actually did it on one occasion), use was able to or managed to: The exit was blocked, but we were able to / managed to escape. (not usually could escape).

In the negative, couldn't works for both: We couldn't open the door.

learn.title: will & won't for willingness & refusal
learn.html:
will = willingness/offers/promises: I'll help you. · The doctor will see you now.

won't = refusal (even of things!): She won't listen. · The car won't start. In the past, refusal is wouldn't: He wouldn't apologise. · The engine wouldn't start.

learn.title: will & would for typical/characteristic behaviour
learn.html:
will (present) and would (past) describe typical, characteristic behaviour — what someone habitually does: He'll sit for hours reading. · When she was little, she'd ask endless questions.

Stressed, this can sound critical: He WILL leave the door open! (= he always annoyingly does). Note: would for past habit needs an action, not a state (Unit 3).

learn.title: Requests, offers & permission
learn.html:
Politeness scale: Can you…? (casual) → Could you…? / Would you…? (polite) → Would you mind + -ing? (very polite): Would you mind opening the window?

Offers: Shall I…? / Can I…? / Would you like me to…? Permission requests: Can/Could/May I…? (could/may more polite/formal).

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Yesterday I could finally fix it. (a specific success) → ✓ I managed to / was able to fix it.

✗ The door didn't want to open. → ✓ The door wouldn't open.

✗ Would you to help me? → ✓ Would you help me? / Would you mind helping me?

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• could = general past ability; was able to / managed to = a specific past achievement.
  • won't/wouldn't = refusal (people and things); will/would = characteristic behaviour.
  • Politeness: Can → Could/Would → Would you mind + -ing.
  • would for past habit needs an action, not a state.

ru.title: Больше, чем «способность»
ru.html: У модальных способности и готовности на B2 тонкие различия. can/could — способность/возможность; will/would — готовность, отказ, характерное поведение. can — способность сейчас; could — прошлая/общая способность, вежливая просьба; will — готовность, привычка; would — прошлая привычка, отказ.

kk.title: «Қабілеттен» асып
kk.html: Қабілет пен ниет модальдарында B2-де нәзік айырма бар. can/could — қабілет/мүмкіндік; will/would — ниет, бас тарту, тән мінез-құлық. can — қазіргі қабілет; could — өткен/жалпы қабілет, сыпайы өтініш; will — ниет, тән әдет; would — өткен әдет, бас тарту.

ru.title: could vs was able to / managed to
ru.html: Для общей прошлой способности — could: I could swim when I was five. Но для конкретного успеха один раз — was able to / managed to: The exit was blocked, but we managed to escape (не could escape). В отрицании couldn't подходит для обоих.

kk.title: could vs was able to / managed to
kk.html: Жалпы өткен қабілетке — could: I could swim when I was five. Бірақ нақты бір рет орындалған табысқа — was able to / managed to: The exit was blocked, but we were able to / managed to escape (could escape емес). Болымсызда couldn't екеуіне де жарайды.

ru.title: will & won't — готовность и отказ
ru.html: will = готовность/предложение/обещание: I'll help you. The doctor will see you now. won't = отказ (и вещей!): She won't listen. The car won't start. В прошлом отказ — wouldn't: He wouldn't apologise. The engine wouldn't start.

kk.title: will & won't — ниет пен бас тарту
kk.html: will = ниет/ұсыныс/уәде: I'll help you. The doctor will see you now. won't = бас тарту (заттардың да!): She won't listen. The car won't start. Өткенде бас тарту — wouldn't: He wouldn't apologise. The engine wouldn't start.

ru.title: will & would — характерное поведение
ru.html: will (наст.) и would (прош.) — типичное, характерное поведение: He'll sit for hours reading. She'd ask endless questions. С ударением звучит как критика: He WILL leave the door open! (вечно, раздражающе). would для прошлой привычки нужен действие, не состояние (раздел 3).

kk.title: will & would — тән мінез-құлық
kk.html: will (осы) және would (өткен) — тән, әдеттегі мінез-құлық: He'll sit for hours reading. She'd ask endless questions. Екпінмен сынға ұқсауы мүмкін: He WILL leave the door open! (әрдайым мазалап). would өткен әдетте әрекет қажет, күй емес (3-бөлім).

ru.title: Просьбы, предложения, разрешение
ru.html: Вежливость: Can you…? (обычно) → Could/Would you…? (вежливо) → Would you mind + -ing? (очень вежливо): Would you mind opening the window? Предложения: Shall I…? / Can I…? / Would you like me to…? Разрешение: Can/Could/May I…? (could/may вежливее/формальнее).

kk.title: Өтініш, ұсыныс, рұқсат
kk.html: Сыпайылық: Can you…? (кәдімгі) → Could/Would you…? (сыпайы) → Would you mind + -ing? (өте сыпайы): Would you mind opening the window? Ұсыныс: Shall I…? / Can I…? / Would you like me to…? Рұқсат: Can/Could/May I…? (could/may сыпайырақ/ресми).

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Yesterday I could finally fix it (конкретный успех) → ✓ I managed to / was able to fix it. ✗ The door didn't want to open → ✓ The door wouldn't open. ✗ Would you to help me? → ✓ Would you help me? / Would you mind helping me?

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Yesterday I could finally fix it (нақты табыс) → ✓ I managed to / was able to fix it. ✗ The door didn't want to open → ✓ The door wouldn't open. ✗ Would you to help me? → ✓ Would you help me? / Would you mind helping me?

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• could = общая прошлая способность; was able to / managed to = конкретный успех.
  • won't/wouldn't = отказ (люди и вещи); will/would = характерное поведение.
  • Вежливость: Can → Could/Would → Would you mind + -ing.
  • would для прошлой привычки нужен действие, не состояние.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• could = жалпы өткен қабілет; was able to / managed to = нақты бір рет табыс.
  • won't/wouldn't = бас тарту (адам да, зат та); will/would = тән мінез.
  • Сыпайылық: Can → Could/Would → Would you mind + -ing.
  • would өткен әдетте әрекет қажет, күй емес.

activities.prompt: Sort each example by its function.

items.t: He won't take no for an answer.
Grandad would tell the same joke every time.
Could you touch your toes as a child?
She'll spend hours on her phone.

activities.why: can/could = ability; will/won't = willingness/refusal; will/would (habitual) = characteristic behaviour.

items.s: After hours of trying, we managed to open the safe.
The engine wouldn't start this morning.
After hours of trying, we could open the safe.
Would you to pass the salt?

activities.why: a specific past success → managed to (not could); polite request → Would you pass…? (no ‘to’).

activities.before: The road was flooded, but we
activities.why: a one-off achievement → managed to / were able to find (not could).

activities.before: I tried all morning, but the app just
activities.why: a past refusal, even of a thing → wouldn't open.

activities.after: you mind turning the music down? (Would)
activities.why: the most polite pattern → Would you mind + -ing.
a repeated past action → would + base verb.

activities.q: “The fire spread fast, but everyone ___ get out in time.”
why: ‘could’ works for general ability, not a specific past success.
Correct — a specific achievement on that occasion → managed to get out.
‘would’ expresses habit/willingness, not this success.

activities.q: Which sentence, said with stress on the modal, sounds like a complaint?
why: this is a neutral statement of ability.
Correct — stressed ‘WILL’ marks an annoying, characteristic habit → a complaint.
this expresses possibility, not habit.

activities.prompt: Which structure does each context need?
buckets: managed to / was able to (specific success)

items.t: I ___ read music by age six
Despite the crowd, I ___ get a ticket
The lock was stiff, but I ___ open it

activities.why: ‘could’ for a general past skill; ‘managed to/was able to’ for succeeding on one occasion.
a specific past success → “I managed to persuade him.” (could would suggest only general ability)

activities.instruction: Rewrite to describe an annoying, characteristic habit:
activities.prompt: He always borrows things without asking.
activities.answer: he will borrow things without asking
alts: he will keep borrowing things without asking
activities.why: characteristic (annoying) behaviour → will borrow (stress on will).

activities.instruction: Rewrite the refusal in the past:
activities.prompt: My little brother refuses to share his toys.
activities.answer: my little brother wouldn't share his toys
alts: my little brother would not share his toys
activities.why: a past refusal → wouldn't share.

activities.scene: Choose the natural modal.

steps.npc: You look stressed — what happened?

options.t: My car wouldn't start, and then the printer wouldn't work either.
options.why: refusal of things in the past → wouldn't.

options.t: My car couldn't start, and the printer couldn't work.
options.why: machines ‘refusing’ to work → wouldn't start / wouldn't work.

steps.npc: Did you fix them in the end?

options.t: Eventually — I managed to jump-start the car.
options.why: a specific past success → managed to.

options.t: Eventually — I could jump-start the car.
options.why: a one-off success → managed to / was able to jump-start.

activities.why: Use could for general past ability but managed to/was able to for a specific achievement; won't/wouldn't for refusal; and will/would for characteristic behaviour — scaling politeness from Can to Would you mind + -ing.

learn.title: Looking back with judgement
learn.html:
These modals evaluate actions: giving advice, expressing criticism of what someone did, or voicing regret about the past.

  • should / ought to — advice & mild obligation (present & future)
  • should have / ought to have + p.p. — criticism/regret about the past
  • could have / might have + p.p. — an unrealised past possibility or reproach

learn.title: should & ought to — advice now
learn.html:
should / ought to + base verb give advice or say what is (in the speaker's view) the right thing: You should see a doctor. · We ought to leave soon.

They are weaker than must/have to — a recommendation, not a command. had better (not) is stronger, for urgent advice with a warning: You'd better hurry, or you'll miss it. · You'd better not be late.

learn.title: should have + p.p. — past criticism & regret
learn.html:
should have / ought to have + past participle says the right thing didn't happen — regret or criticism:

I should have booked earlier.regret — I didn't, and now it's a problem
You shouldn't have said that.criticism — you did, and it was wrong

It always refers to something that did or didn't actually happen, contrary to what was advisable.

learn.title: could have / might have — missed chances & reproach
learn.html:
could have + p.p. = it was possible but didn't happen: We could have won, but we relaxed too early.

could have / might have + p.p. can also express reproach: You could have warned me! · You might have told me you were coming!

might/may have + p.p. also = a past possibility/deduction (Unit 11) — context tells you which.

learn.title: was supposed to & needn't have (recap)
learn.html:
was/were supposed to = an expectation that wasn't met: You were supposed to call me! needn't have + p.p. = an unnecessary action that was done (Unit 12): You needn't have paid.

Compare: shouldn't have = it was wrong to do it; needn't have = it wasn't necessary (but not necessarily wrong).

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ You should to see a doctor. → ✓ You should see a doctor.

✗ I should booked earlier. → ✓ I should have booked earlier.

✗ You had better to hurry. → ✓ You'd better hurry.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• should / ought to (+ base) = advice now; had better = stronger, with a warning.
  • should have / ought to have (+ p.p.) = past regret/criticism (it didn't happen as it should).
  • could have / might have (+ p.p.) = a missed possibility or reproach.
  • needn't have (unnecessary) ≠ shouldn't have (wrong).

ru.title: Взгляд назад с оценкой
ru.html: Эти модальные оценивают действия: совет, критика сделанного, сожаление о прошлом. should/ought to — совет (наст./буд.); should have + p.p. — критика/сожаление о прошлом; could have / might have + p.p. — несбывшаяся возможность/упрёк.

kk.title: Бағалаумен артқа қарау
kk.html: Бұл модальдар әрекетті бағалайды: кеңес, істелген іске сын, өткен туралы өкініш. should/ought to — кеңес (осы/болашақ); should have + p.p. — өткенге сын/өкініш; could have / might have + p.p. — орындалмаған мүмкіндік/сөгіс.

ru.title: should & ought to — совет сейчас
ru.html: should/ought to + база дают совет или (по мнению говорящего) верный вариант: You should see a doctor. We ought to leave soon. Слабее must/have to — рекомендация, не приказ. had better (not) сильнее, с предупреждением: You'd better hurry, or you'll miss it. You'd better not be late.

kk.title: should & ought to — қазіргі кеңес
kk.html: should/ought to + негізгі етістік кеңес береді немесе (сөйлеуші ойынша) дұрыс нәрсені айтады: You should see a doctor. We ought to leave soon. must/have to-дан әлсіз — ұсыныс, бұйрық емес. had better (not) күштірек, ескертумен: You'd better hurry, or you'll miss it. You'd better not be late.

ru.title: should have + p.p. — критика/сожаление о прошлом
ru.html: should have / ought to have + past participle говорит, что правильное не произошло — сожаление/критика: I should have booked earlier (сожаление — не забронировал). You shouldn't have said that (критика — сказал, зря). Всегда о реально бывшем/не бывшем, вопреки желательному.

kk.title: should have + p.p. — өткенге сын/өкініш
kk.html: should have / ought to have + past participle дұрыс нәрсе болмағанын айтады — өкініш/сын: I should have booked earlier (өкініш — брондамадым). You shouldn't have said that (сын — айттың, дұрыс емес еді). Ол әрдайым шынымен болған/болмаған, кеңеске қайшы іске сілтейді.

ru.title: could have / might have — упущенная возможность/упрёк
ru.html: could have + p.p. = было возможно, но не случилось: We could have won, but we relaxed too early. could have / might have + p.p. выражает и упрёк: You could have warned me! You might have told me! might/may have + p.p. — ещё и прошлая догадка (раздел 11) — решает контекст.

kk.title: could have / might have — жіберілген мүмкіндік/сөгіс
kk.html: could have + p.p. = мүмкін еді, бірақ болмады: We could have won, but we relaxed too early. could have / might have + p.p. сөгісті де білдіреді: You could have warned me! You might have told me! might/may have + p.p. өткен болжам да (11-бөлім) — контекст шешеді.

ru.title: was supposed to & needn't have
ru.html: was/were supposed to = невыполненное ожидание: You were supposed to call me! needn't have + p.p. = сделанное, но ненужное действие (раздел 12): You needn't have paid. Сравни: shouldn't have = делать было неправильно; needn't have = было не нужно (но не обязательно неправильно).

kk.title: was supposed to & needn't have
kk.html: was/were supposed to = орындалмаған күту: You were supposed to call me! needn't have + p.p. = істелген, бірақ қажетсіз әрекет (12-бөлім): You needn't have paid. Салыстыр: shouldn't have = істеу дұрыс емес еді; needn't have = қажет емес еді (бірақ міндетті түрде қате емес).

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ You should to see a doctor → ✓ You should see a doctor. ✗ I should booked earlier → ✓ I should have booked earlier. ✗ You had better to hurry → ✓ You'd better hurry.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ You should to see a doctor → ✓ You should see a doctor. ✗ I should booked earlier → ✓ I should have booked earlier. ✗ You had better to hurry → ✓ You'd better hurry.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• should / ought to (+ база) = совет сейчас; had better = сильнее, с предупреждением.
  • should have / ought to have (+ p.p.) = сожаление/критика о прошлом.
  • could have / might have (+ p.p.) = упущенная возможность или упрёк.
  • needn't have (ненужно) ≠ shouldn't have (неправильно).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• should / ought to (+ база) = қазіргі кеңес; had better = күштірек, ескертумен.
  • should have / ought to have (+ p.p.) = өткенге өкініш/сын.
  • could have / might have (+ p.p.) = жіберілген мүмкіндік немесе сөгіс.
  • needn't have (қажетсіз) ≠ shouldn't have (қате).

activities.typeLabel: Advice now or judgement about the past?
activities.prompt: Sort each structure.

items.t: We could have booked cheaper seats.

activities.why: Base-form modals give present/future advice; modal + have + p.p. judges the past.

items.s: You should see a specialist.
I should have called you sooner.
You should to apologise.
You had better to leave.

activities.why: no ‘to’ after should / had better: should apologise, had better leave.
a regret about something not done → should have warned.

activities.after: leave now, or you'll miss the last train. (had better)
activities.why: urgent advice with a consequence → 'd better leave.

activities.after: me you were allergic to nuts! (could / tell)
activities.why: reproach about the past → could have told me.
past regret → should + have + past participle.

activities.q: “You ___ bought flowers — they're gorgeous, but you really didn't have to spend so much.”
why: ‘shouldn't have’ implies it was wrong — but the flowers are welcome.
Correct — it was kind but unnecessary → needn't have bought.
‘couldn't have’ expresses impossibility, not this.

activities.q: “The ladder slipped — you ___ yourself!”
why: Correct — a dangerous but unrealised possibility → could have hurt yourself.
‘must have hurt’ claims it definitely happened.
‘should have hurt’ makes no sense as advice.

activities.prompt: Sort each past comment by meaning.
buckets: it was possible but didn't happen (could have)

items.t: You needn't have waited for me.
We could have caught the earlier flight.

activities.why: shouldn't have = wrong; needn't have = unnecessary but done; could have = an unrealised possibility.
a criticism about the past → “You should have told me that yesterday.”

activities.prompt: I didn't save my work, and I lost it all.
activities.answer: i should have saved my work
alts: i should have saved my work.
i ought to have saved my work
activities.why: regret about something not done → should have saved.

activities.instruction: Turn this into a reproach with ‘could have’:
activities.prompt: You knew about the change but said nothing.
activities.why: reproach about a missed action → could have told me.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: after a poor decision
activities.scene: Choose advice, regret or reproach.

steps.npc: The presentation went badly — I froze completely.

options.t: You should have practised out loud beforehand — but next time you'll be ready.
options.why: past regret (should have) + present advice.

options.t: You should practise out loud beforehand, and you shouldn't froze.
options.why: about the past use should have practised / shouldn't have frozen.

steps.npc: I wish someone had warned me about the tough questions.

options.t: To be fair, I could have prepared you better. Sorry.
options.why: an unrealised past possibility / mild self-reproach → could have.

options.t: To be fair, I can prepare you better yesterday.
options.why: about the past → could have prepared you better.

activities.why: Use should/ought to/had better for advice now, should have/ought to have + p.p. for past regret or criticism, and could have/might have + p.p. for a missed possibility or reproach — keeping needn't have (unnecessary) apart from shouldn't have (wrong).

learn.title: Two complement patterns
learn.html:
When one verb follows another, the first verb decides the pattern: some take the to-infinitive, some the -ing form, some an object + to-infinitive, and some take both (with or without a meaning change).

She decided to leave. · I enjoy cooking. · They want me to help. · He started to rain / raining.

learn.title: Verb + to-infinitive
learn.html:
Common verbs: agree, decide, hope, expect, offer, promise, refuse, manage, fail, seem, appear, tend, afford, arrange, learn, pretend, threaten.

We can't afford to move. · She seems to understand. · He threatened to resign.

Negative: not to + verb — I decided not to go.

learn.title: Verb + -ing
learn.html:
Common verbs: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, deny, consider, imagine, involve, keep, practise, risk, delay, postpone, resist, can't help, can't stand, feel like, give up.

She suggested waiting. · I can't stand queuing. · Would you consider relocating?

learn.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
learn.html:
Many verbs take an object before the to-infinitive: want, ask, tell, advise, expect, encourage, persuade, remind, warn, allow, force, enable, cause, get.

They persuaded us to stay. · The alarm caused everyone to panic.

make and let take a bare infinitive: They made me wait. · She let him go. (Passive of make → to: I was made to wait.)

learn.title: Verbs taking both — little change
learn.html:
After begin, start, continue, bother, both forms are fine with almost no difference: It began to rain / raining. But avoid a double -ing: It's starting to rain (not starting raining).

like, love, hate, prefer take both; would like/love/prefer take the to-infinitive: I'd like to come. (Unit 16 covers verbs where the meaning genuinely changes.)

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I enjoy to swim. → ✓ I enjoy swimming.

✗ She suggested to meet at six. → ✓ She suggested meeting at six.

✗ He wants that I help. → ✓ He wants me to help.

✗ They made me to wait. → ✓ They made me wait.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Learn each verb's pattern: to-infinitive (decide, hope), -ing (enjoy, avoid), or object + to (want, tell).
  • make/let take a bare infinitive; the passive of make takes ‘to’.
  • begin/start/continue take both with little change; would like/love/prefer take ‘to’.
  • English doesn't use ‘want that…’ — use object + to-infinitive.

ru.title: Две модели дополнения
ru.html: Когда один глагол следует за другим, первый выбирает модель: одни берут to-infinitive, другие -ing, третьи object + to-infinitive, а некоторые оба. She decided to leave. I enjoy cooking. They want me to help. It started to rain / raining.

kk.title: Екі толықтауыш үлгісі
kk.html: Бір етістік екіншісінен кейін келгенде, бірінші етістік үлгіні шешеді: кейбірі to-infinitive, кейбірі -ing, кейбірі object + to-infinitive, кейбірі екеуін де алады. She decided to leave. I enjoy cooking. They want me to help. It started to rain / raining.

ru.title: Verb + to-infinitive
ru.html: Частые: agree, decide, hope, expect, offer, promise, refuse, manage, fail, seem, appear, tend, afford, arrange, learn, pretend, threaten. We can't afford to move. He threatened to resign. Отрицание: not to + verb — I decided not to go.

kk.title: Verb + to-infinitive
kk.html: Жиі: agree, decide, hope, expect, offer, promise, refuse, manage, fail, seem, appear, tend, afford, arrange, learn, pretend, threaten. We can't afford to move. He threatened to resign. Болымсыз: not to + verb — I decided not to go.

ru.title: Verb + -ing
ru.html: Частые: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, deny, consider, imagine, involve, keep, practise, risk, delay, postpone, resist, can't help, can't stand, feel like, give up. She suggested waiting. I can't stand queuing.

kk.title: Verb + -ing
kk.html: Жиі: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, deny, consider, imagine, involve, keep, practise, risk, delay, postpone, resist, can't help, can't stand, feel like, give up. She suggested waiting. I can't stand queuing.

ru.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
ru.html: Многие глаголы берут дополнение перед to-инфинитивом: want, ask, tell, advise, expect, encourage, persuade, remind, warn, allow, force, enable, cause, get. They persuaded us to stay. make, let — без ‘to’: They made me wait. В пассиве make → to: I was made to wait.

kk.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
kk.html: Көп етістік to-инфинитив алдында object алады: want, ask, tell, advise, expect, encourage, persuade, remind, warn, allow, force, enable, cause, get. They persuaded us to stay. make, let — ‘to’-сыз инфинитив: They made me wait. Passive-те make → to: I was made to wait.

ru.title: Берущие оба — почти без разницы
ru.html: После begin, start, continue, bother оба варианта, разницы почти нет: It began to rain / raining. Но избегайте двойного -ing: It's starting to rain (не starting raining). like, love, hate, prefer — оба; would like/love/prefer — to-infinitive: I'd like to come.

kk.title: Екеуін де алатындар — айырма аз
kk.html: begin, start, continue, bother-дан кейін екеуі de жарайды, айырма жоқ: It began to rain / raining. Бірақ қос -ing болмасын: It's starting to rain (starting raining емес). like, love, hate, prefer — екеуі де; would like/love/prefer — to-infinitive: I'd like to come.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ I enjoy to swim → ✓ I enjoy swimming. ✗ She suggested to meet → ✓ She suggested meeting. ✗ He wants that I help → ✓ He wants me to help. ✗ They made me to wait → ✓ They made me wait.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ I enjoy to swim → ✓ I enjoy swimming. ✗ She suggested to meet → ✓ She suggested meeting. ✗ He wants that I help → ✓ He wants me to help. ✗ They made me to wait → ✓ They made me wait.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Учите модель каждого глагола: to (decide, hope), -ing (enjoy, avoid), object + to (want, tell).
  • make/let — без ‘to’; пассив make берёт ‘to’.
  • begin/start/continue — оба; would like/love/prefer — ‘to’.
  • «want that…» нет — object + to-infinitive.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Әр етістіктің үлгісін жаттаңыз: to (decide, hope), -ing (enjoy, avoid), object + to (want, tell).
  • make/let — ‘to’-сыз инфинитив; make-тің passive-і ‘to’ алады.
  • begin/start/continue — екеуі де; would like/love/prefer — ‘to’.
  • «want that…» жоқ — object + to-infinitive.

activities.typeLabel: Which pattern does the verb take?
activities.prompt: Sort each verb by its complement pattern.
activities.why: decide/refuse → to; avoid/admit → -ing; persuade/remind → object + to.

items.s: She managed to finish on time.
They let us leave early.
He suggested to take a break.
I want that you come.

activities.why: suggest + -ing (suggested taking); want + object + to (want you to come).
afford + to-infinitive → to rent.
encourage + object + to-infinitive → to work.

activities.before: The film was so tense it
activities.why: make + object + bare infinitive → made me hold.
deny + -ing (perfect -ing for an earlier action).

activities.q: “The heavy rain ___ the match ___.”
why: ‘force’ needs an object + to: forced them to postpone.
Correct — cause + object + to-infinitive → caused the match to be postponed.
‘make’ takes a bare infinitive, but here the passive infinitive needs ‘cause … to’.

activities.q: “I'd love ___ you, but I'm afraid I can't afford ___.”
why: ‘would love’ takes the to-infinitive, not -ing.
Correct — would love to help + can't afford it (object).
the second gap needs an object (it), not another ‘to’.

activities.prompt: Which complement does each verb take here?
activities.why: promise/pretend → to; risk/postpone → -ing.
force + object + to-infinitive → “forced him to sign.” (make/let take the bare infinitive, but force needs ‘to’)

activities.prompt: ‘Please stay a bit longer,’ they said, and I agreed.
activities.answer: they persuaded me to stay longer
alts: they persuaded me to stay a bit longer
activities.why: persuade + object + to-infinitive.

activities.prompt: My parents said I could go to the concert.
activities.answer: my parents let me go to the concert
alts: my parents let me go to the concert.
activities.why: let + object + bare infinitive.

activities.scene: Choose the correct complement pattern.

steps.npc: How did you handle the delay?

options.t: I admitted underestimating the workload and offered to bring in help.
options.why: admit + -ing; offer + to-infinitive.

options.t: I admitted to underestimate the workload and offered bringing in help.
options.why: admit + -ing (admitted underestimating); offer + to (offered to bring).

steps.npc: And the client?

options.t: They asked us to revise the timeline and warned us not to miss the next deadline.
options.why: ask/warn + object + to-infinitive (and ‘not to’ for the negative).

options.t: They asked that we revise the timeline and warned us missing the deadline.
options.why: ask us to revise; warn us not to miss (object + to-infinitive).

activities.why: Learn which pattern each verb takes — to-infinitive, -ing, or object + to-infinitive — and remember make/let take a bare infinitive while English avoids ‘want that…’.

learn.title: When the form changes the meaning
learn.html:
A small group of verbs take both the to-infinitive and the -ing form, but with a genuine change in meaning. Choosing wrongly changes what you actually say. The key pattern: -ing looks back / to-infinitive looks forward.

I remember locking the door (a memory) vs Remember to lock the door (a future task).

learn.title: remember / forget / regret
learn.html:
I remember meeting her. -ing = the memory of a past act
Remember to meet her.to = don't forget this task
I regret saying that.-ing = I'm sorry for a past act
We regret to inform you…to = we're sorry to say (bad news now)

learn.title: stop / go on
learn.html:
stop + -ing = end that activity; stop + to-infinitive = pause in order to do something else: He stopped smoking (quit) vs He stopped to smoke (paused for a cigarette).
