go on + -ing = continue the same thing; go on + to-infinitive = move on to something new: She went on talking (kept talking) vs She went on to become a judge (next stage of life).

learn.title: try / mean / come
learn.html:
try + -ing = experiment with a solution; try + to = make an effort: Try restarting it (as a test) vs I tried to lift it (but couldn't).

mean + -ing = involve/result in; mean + to = intend: This job means travelling a lot vs I meant to call you.

come + to = gradually reach a state: I came to realise he was right.

learn.title: need & want (passive -ing)
learn.html:
need / want + -ing has a passive meaning (= need to be done): The car needs cleaning = needs to be cleaned. Your hair wants cutting.

Compare active need + to: You need to clean the car (you do it). can't help + -ing = can't stop yourself: I can't help laughing.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Remember locking the door! (as a reminder) → ✓ Remember to lock the door!

✗ I stopped to smoke (meaning: I quit) → ✓ I stopped smoking.

✗ The room needs to clean. → ✓ The room needs cleaning / needs to be cleaned.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Rule of thumb: -ing looks back (memory/experience); to-infinitive looks forward (task/effort/intention).
  • stop -ing (quit) vs stop to (pause to do); go on -ing (continue) vs go on to (new thing).
  • try -ing (experiment) vs try to (make an effort); mean -ing (involve) vs mean to (intend).
  • need/want + -ing = passive (needs doing = needs to be done).

ru.title: Когда форма меняет смысл
ru.html: Небольшая группа глаголов берёт обе формы, но с настоящей сменой смысла. Ключ: -ing смотрит назад / to-infinitive — вперёд. I remember locking the door (воспоминание) vs Remember to lock the door (будущая задача).

kk.title: Форма мағынаны өзгерткенде
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктер to-инфинитив пен -ing-ті екеуін де алады, бірақ мағынасы шынымен өзгереді. Негізгі үлгі: -ing артқа қарайды / to-infinitive алға қарайды. I remember locking the door (еске алу) vs Remember to lock the door (алдағы міндет).

ru.title: remember / forget / regret
ru.html: I remember meeting her (-ing = память о прошлом) vs Remember to meet her (to = не забудь). I regret saying that (-ing = сожаление о прошлом) vs We regret to inform you… (to = с сожалением сообщаем, плохая новость сейчас).

kk.title: remember / forget / regret
kk.html: I remember meeting her (-ing = өткен істің есі) vs Remember to meet her (to = ұмытпа). I regret saying that (-ing = өткенге өкіну) vs We regret to inform you… (to = өкінішпен хабарлаймыз, қазіргі жаман хабар).

ru.title: stop / go on
ru.html: stop + -ing = прекратить это; stop + to = остановиться, чтобы сделать другое: He stopped smoking (бросил) vs He stopped to smoke (остановился покурить). go on + -ing = продолжать то же; go on + to = перейти к новому: She went on talking vs She went on to become a judge.

kk.title: stop / go on
kk.html: stop + -ing = сол істі тоқтату; stop + to = басқа істі істеу үшін тоқтау: He stopped smoking (тастады) vs He stopped to smoke (темекі шегу үшін тоқтады). go on + -ing = сол істі жалғастыру; go on + to = жаңа іске көшу: She went on talking vs She went on to become a judge.

ru.title: try / mean / come
ru.html: try + -ing = попробовать решение; try + to = приложить усилие: Try restarting it (тест) vs I tried to lift it (но не смог). mean + -ing = влечь/означать; mean + to = намереваться: This job means travelling a lot vs I meant to call you. come + to = постепенно прийти к: I came to realise he was right.

kk.title: try / mean / come
kk.html: try + -ing = шешімді сынап көру; try + to = күш салу: Try restarting it (сынау) vs I tried to lift it (бірақ көтере алмадым). mean + -ing = әкеп соғу/қамту; mean + to = ниеттену: This job means travelling a lot vs I meant to call you. come + to = біртіндеп жету: I came to realise he was right.

ru.title: need & want (пассивный -ing)
ru.html: need / want + -ing имеет пассивное значение (= нужно сделать): The car needs cleaning = needs to be cleaned. Your hair wants cutting. Сравни активное need + to: You need to clean the car (сам). can't help + -ing = не удержаться: I can't help laughing.

kk.title: need & want (пассив -ing)
kk.html: need / want + -ing пассив мағына береді (= істелуі керек): The car needs cleaning = needs to be cleaned. Your hair wants cutting. Салыстыр актив need + to: You need to clean the car (өзің). can't help + -ing = өзіңді тоқтата алмау: I can't help laughing.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Remember locking the door! (как напоминание) → ✓ Remember to lock the door! ✗ I stopped to smoke (в значении «бросил») → ✓ I stopped smoking. ✗ The room needs to clean → ✓ The room needs cleaning / needs to be cleaned.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Remember locking the door! (ескерту) → ✓ Remember to lock the door! ✗ I stopped to smoke (тастадым мағынасында) → ✓ I stopped smoking. ✗ The room needs to clean → ✓ The room needs cleaning / needs to be cleaned.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Правило: -ing — назад (память/опыт); to-infinitive — вперёд (задача/усилие/намерение).
  • stop -ing (бросить) vs stop to (остановиться, чтобы); go on -ing/to.
  • try -ing (попробовать) vs try to (стараться); mean -ing (влечь) vs mean to (намереваться).
  • need/want + -ing = пассив (needs doing = needs to be done).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Ереже: -ing артқа (ес/тәжірибе); to-infinitive алға (міндет/күш/ниет).
  • stop -ing (тастау) vs stop to (сол үшін тоқтау); go on -ing/to.
  • try -ing (сынау) vs try to (күш салу); mean -ing (қамту) vs mean to (ниет).
  • need/want + -ing = пассив (needs doing = needs to be done).

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase by the meaning the form gives.
activities.why: -ing refers to a past act; the to-infinitive points to a task, effort or news.

activities.typeLabel: Does the form match the meaning?

items.s: Try restarting the router. (as a test)
This role means managing a team.
I stopped to smoke = I quit.
Remember locking up = a reminder for later.

activities.why: stopped to smoke = paused to smoke; a reminder → Remember to lock up.

activities.before: On the way home we stopped
activities.why: pause in order to → stop to get.

activities.after: a bestselling novelist. (on / become — a new stage)
activities.why: move on to a new thing → go on to become.

activities.before: These windows are filthy — they really
activities.why: need + -ing = passive → need cleaning (= need to be cleaned).
mean + to = intend.

activities.q: “I distinctly remember ___ the oven off — so why is it still on?”
why: Correct — a memory of a past action → remember turning it off.
‘to turn’ would be a future task, which contradicts the memory.

activities.q: “The soup tastes bland. Why not ___ a pinch of salt?”
why: ‘try to add’ = make an effort — odd for a simple suggestion.
Correct — experiment with a solution → try adding.

activities.prompt: Match each ‘mean’/‘go on’ use to its sense.

items.t: I didn't mean to hurt you.
He went on complaining for an hour.
She went on to win gold.

activities.why: mean -ing = involves/results in; mean to = intends; go on -ing = keep doing; go on to = move to a new stage.
a reminder about a future task → forget to post (not the memory sense).

activities.instruction: Express present regret about a past act:
activities.prompt: I'm sorry I ever agreed to this.
alts: i regret ever agreeing to this
activities.why: regret + -ing = sorry about a past action.

activities.prompt: Someone should repair this fence.
alts: this fence needs to be repaired
activities.why: need + -ing = passive → needs repairing.

activities.scene: Choose the form the meaning requires.

steps.npc: Did you send the invoice?

options.t: Oh no — I meant to, but I forgot to attach it.
options.why: mean to (intend) + forget to (a missed task).

options.t: Oh no — I meant sending it, but I forgot attaching it.
options.why: intention → meant to send; a missed task → forgot to attach.

steps.npc: Weren't you meant to double-check it?

options.t: I remember checking the totals, but I'll try adding a second reviewer next time.
options.why: remember + -ing (memory) + try + -ing (experiment).

options.t: I remember to check the totals, but I'll try to add a second reviewer... wait.
options.why: a memory of checking → remember checking; and as an experiment → try adding.

activities.why: With meaning-change verbs, let the -ing form look back (memory, experience, ongoing) and the to-infinitive look forward (task, effort, intention) — and remember need/want + -ing carries a passive sense.

learn.title: Extending infinitives & -ing forms
learn.html: Infinitives and -ing forms have perfect and passive versions, letting you show time and voice inside a non-finite clause. B2 also refines perception verbs (see/hear + do/doing) and the used to / be used to contrast.

learn.title: Perfect infinitive & perfect -ing
learn.html:
to have + past participle and having + past participle mark an action as earlier than the main verb:

  • She seems to have left. (leaving is before ‘seems’)
  • He pretended to have read it.
  • Having finished lunch, we set off. (after finishing)
  • She admitted having taken the money.

learn.title: Passive infinitive & passive -ing
learn.html:
to be + past participle and being + past participle put the non-finite clause into the passive:

  • The documents need to be signed.
  • He expects to be promoted.
  • She hates being interrupted.
  • Having been warned, we left early. (perfect + passive)

learn.title: Perception verbs: see/hear + do vs doing
learn.html:
After see, hear, watch, feel, notice, listen to, a bare infinitive shows the complete action; the -ing form shows it in progress:

I saw her cross the road.the whole action (start to finish)
I saw her crossing the road.caught in the middle of it

No ‘to’: I saw her to cross. In the passive, ‘to’ appears: She was seen to leave.

learn.title: used to do vs be/get used to doing
learn.html:
used to + base verb = a past habit (Unit 3): I used to smoke.

be used to + -ing/noun = be accustomed to; get used to + -ing = become accustomed: I'm used to working nights. · She's slowly getting used to the cold.

Here to is a preposition, so a verb after it takes -ing.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He seems to leave already. → ✓ He seems to have left already.

✗ I'm used to work nights. → ✓ I'm used to working nights.

✗ I saw him to fall. → ✓ I saw him fall / falling.

✗ The car needs to be repair. → ✓ needs to be repaired / needs repairing.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Perfect infinitive/-ing (to have done / having done) = earlier than the main verb.
  • Passive infinitive/-ing (to be done / being done) = the subject receives the action.
  • Perception verbs: bare infinitive = whole action; -ing = in progress (no ‘to’; passive adds ‘to’).
  • used to do (past habit) vs be/get used to doing (accustomed) — the second ‘to’ is a preposition.

ru.title: Расширяем инфинитивы и -ing формы
ru.html: У инфинитивов и -ing есть перфектные и пассивные версии — они показывают время и залог внутри неличной клаузы. B2 также уточняет глаголы восприятия (see/hear + do/doing) и различие used to / be used to.

kk.title: Инфинитив мен -ing формаларын кеңейту
kk.html: Инфинитив пен -ing-тің perfect және passive нұсқалары бар — бейфинитивті сөйлемде уақыт пен ырықты көрсетеді. B2 қабылдау етістіктерін (see/hear + do/doing) және used to / be used to айырмасын да нақтылайды.

ru.title: Perfect infinitive & perfect -ing
ru.html: to have + past participle и having + past participle помечают действие как более раннее, чем главный глагол: She seems to have left. He pretended to have read it. Having finished lunch, we set off. She admitted having taken the money.

kk.title: Perfect infinitive & perfect -ing
kk.html: to have + past participle және having + past participle әрекетті негізгі етістіктен ертерек етеді: She seems to have left. He pretended to have read it. Having finished lunch, we set off. She admitted having taken the money.

ru.title: Passive infinitive & passive -ing
ru.html: to be + past participle и being + past participle переводят неличную клаузу в пассив: The documents need to be signed. He expects to be promoted. She hates being interrupted. Having been warned, we left early (перфект + пассив).

kk.title: Passive infinitive & passive -ing
kk.html: to be + past participle және being + past participle бейфинитивті сөйлемді пассивке қояды: The documents need to be signed. He expects to be promoted. She hates being interrupted. Having been warned, we left early (perfect + passive).

ru.title: Глаголы восприятия: see/hear + do vs doing
ru.html: После see, hear, watch, feel, notice, listen to инфинитив без ‘to’ — полное действие; -ing — в процессе: I saw her cross the road (целиком) vs I saw her crossing the road (в середине). ‘to’ нет: saw her to cross. В пассиве ‘to’ появляется: She was seen to leave.

kk.title: Қабылдау етістіктері: see/hear + do vs doing
kk.html: see, hear, watch, feel, notice, listen to-дан кейін ‘to’-сыз инфинитив — толық әрекет; -ing — үдерісте: I saw her cross the road (толық) vs I saw her crossing the road (ортасында). ‘to’ жоқ: saw her to cross. Passive-те ‘to’ шығады: She was seen to leave.

ru.title: used to do vs be/get used to doing
ru.html: used to + база = прошлая привычка (раздел 3): I used to smoke. be used to + -ing/noun = быть привыкшим; get used to + -ing = привыкать: I'm used to working nights. She's getting used to the cold. Здесь to — предлог, поэтому глагол после — -ing.

kk.title: used to do vs be/get used to doing
kk.html: used to + база = өткен әдет (3-бөлім): I used to smoke. be used to + -ing/noun = үйреніп қалу; get used to + -ing = үйрене бастау: I'm used to working nights. She's getting used to the cold. Мұнда to — предлог, сондықтан кейінгі етістік -ing.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ He seems to leave already → ✓ He seems to have left already. ✗ I'm used to work nights → ✓ I'm used to working nights. ✗ I saw him to fall → ✓ I saw him fall / falling. ✗ needs to be repair → ✓ needs to be repaired / needs repairing.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ He seems to leave already → ✓ He seems to have left already. ✗ I'm used to work nights → ✓ I'm used to working nights. ✗ I saw him to fall → ✓ I saw him fall / falling. ✗ needs to be repair → ✓ needs to be repaired / needs repairing.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Perfect infinitive/-ing (to have done / having done) = раньше главного глагола.
  • Passive infinitive/-ing (to be done / being done) = субъект получает действие.
  • Восприятие: инфинитив без ‘to’ = целиком; -ing = в процессе (пассив добавляет ‘to’).
  • used to do (прошлая привычка) vs be/get used to doing (привычка) — второе ‘to’ — предлог.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Perfect infinitive/-ing (to have done / having done) = негізгіден ертерек.
  • Passive infinitive/-ing (to be done / being done) = субъект әрекетті қабылдайды.
  • Қабылдау: ‘to’-сыз инфинитив = толық; -ing = үдерісте (passive ‘to’ қосады).
  • used to do (өткен әдет) vs be/get used to doing (үйрену) — екінші ‘to’ — предлог.

activities.prompt: Sort each form by what it signals.
activities.why: ‘to have/having + p.p.’ = earlier; ‘to be/being + p.p.’ = passive.

items.s: She seems to have forgotten.
I'm used to getting up early.
I saw him to leave.
He hates to being told what to do.

activities.why: perception verbs take a bare infinitive/-ing (saw him leave); ‘hate’ + passive -ing = hates being told (no ‘to’).

activities.before: The keys aren't here — someone seems
activities.why: an action earlier than ‘seems’ → perfect infinitive to have taken.
the form receives the action → passive infinitive to be signed.

activities.after: in three languages every day. (used to / work)
activities.why: be used to + -ing (preposition ‘to’) → used to working.
perfect + passive -ing clause (earlier + they received the warning).

activities.q: “As I passed the studio, I heard someone ___ the same phrase over and over.”
why: the bare infinitive suggests a single complete action.
Correct — an action caught in progress → heard someone playing.

activities.typeLabel: used to do or be used to doing?
activities.q: “It felt strange at first, but now I ___ driving on the left.”
why: ‘used to drive’ would mean a past habit now stopped.
Correct — accustomed now → 'm used to driving (be used to + -ing).
‘would’ expresses past habit, not present familiarity.

activities.prompt: Sort each structure by meaning.

items.t: We used to commute by train

activities.why: used to + base = a past habit; be/get used to + -ing = being/becoming accustomed.
admit + -ing → “admitted taking” (or the perfect -ing ‘having taken’ for an earlier act); ‘to take’ is wrong.

activities.prompt: It appears that she left before dawn.
activities.answer: she appears to have left before dawn
alts: she appears to have left before dawn.
activities.why: an earlier action reported now → to have left.

activities.prompt: She doesn't like it when people criticise her.
activities.why: she receives the action → passive -ing being criticised.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: adjusting to a new role
activities.scene: Choose the correct non-finite form.

steps.npc: How are you settling into management?

options.t: Slowly getting used to being in charge — I'm not used to making so many decisions.
options.why: get/be used to + -ing (passive and active).

options.t: Slowly getting used to be in charge — I'm not used to make so many decisions.
options.why: the ‘to’ here is a preposition → being in charge, making decisions.

steps.npc: Any early wins?

options.t: The team seems to have accepted the changes, and I expect to be given more autonomy soon.
options.why: perfect infinitive (earlier) + passive infinitive (to be given).

options.t: The team seems to accept the changes already, and I expect to give more autonomy.
options.why: already done → to have accepted; you receive autonomy → to be given.

activities.why: Use perfect infinitives/-ing for earlier actions and passive infinitives/-ing when the subject receives the action; with perception verbs, a bare infinitive shows the whole action and -ing shows it in progress; and be/get used to + -ing means ‘accustomed’, not a past habit.

learn.title: Real vs unreal — the big divide
learn.html:
Conditionals split into real (things that can happen) and unreal (imaginary or contrary to fact). The tense you choose signals how likely or possible you think the situation is.

  • Zero — general truths (always true)
  • First — real future possibility
  • Second — unreal/unlikely present or future
  • Third — unreal past (didn't happen)

learn.title: Zero & first — real conditionals
learn.html:
If you heat ice, it melts.zero: present + present — a general truth
If it rains, we'll stay in.first: present + will — a real future

In the if-clause use the present, not will. Variations in the first: may/might/could/should/must/imperative in the main clause: If you see him, tell him. · If you're tired, you should rest.

learn.title: Second — unreal present/future
learn.html:
If + past simple, … would + base verb for imaginary or unlikely situations now or in the future:

If I won the lottery, I'd travel the world. · If she were here, she'd know.

Use were for all persons in careful/formal English (If I were you…), though was is common in speech. Main clause can use would/could/might.

learn.title: Third — unreal past
learn.html:
If + past perfect, … would have + past participle for the imagined opposite of what really happened (a past regret or speculation):

If I had known, I'd have come. · If they hadn't helped, we'd have failed.

Main clause can use could/might have for possibility: We might have won if we'd tried harder.

learn.title: Nuance: probability & the ‘if I were to’
learn.html: The tense shift signals distance from reality: first = likely; second = unlikely/imaginary; third = impossible (it's over). To make a future hypothesis sound more tentative/formal: If I were to resign, who would replace me? or Should you change your mind, call me (Unit 20).

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ If it will rain, we'll stay. → ✓ If it rains, we'll stay.

✗ If I would have money, I'd buy it. → ✓ If I had money, I'd buy it. (no would in the if-clause)

✗ If I had known, I would came. → ✓ I would have come.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Zero: present + present (general truth). First: present + will (real future).
  • Second: past simple + would (unreal present/future); ‘were’ for all persons.
  • Third: past perfect + would have + p.p. (unreal past).
  • Never use ‘will/would’ in the if-clause.

ru.title: Реальное vs нереальное — главное деление
ru.html: Условные делятся на реальные (могут случиться) и нереальные (воображаемые/противоречащие фактам). Выбранное время показывает, насколько вероятной ты считаешь ситуацию. Zero — общая истина; First — реальное будущее; Second — нереальное/маловероятное настоящее/будущее; Third — нереальное прошлое.

kk.title: Нақты vs шартты-қиял — басты бөліну
kk.html: Шартты сөйлемдер нақты (болуы мүмкін) және қиял (шындыққа қайшы) болып бөлінеді. Таңдаған шақ қаншалықты мүмкін деп ойлайтыныңды білдіреді. Zero — жалпы шындық; First — нақты болашақ; Second — қиял/екіталай осы/болашақ; Third — қиял өткен (болмады).

ru.title: Zero & first — реальные условия
ru.html: If you heat ice, it melts (zero: present + present — общая истина). If it rains, we'll stay in (first: present + will — реальное будущее). В if-части — present, не will. В главной части — may/might/could/should/повелит.: If you see him, tell him.

kk.title: Zero & first — нақты шарттар
kk.html: If you heat ice, it melts (zero: present + present — жалпы шындық). If it rains, we'll stay in (first: present + will — нақты болашақ). If-бөлікте present, will емес. Бас сөйлемде may/might/could/should/бұйрық болуы мүмкін: If you see him, tell him.

ru.title: Second — нереальное настоящее/будущее
ru.html: If + past simple, … would + база — воображаемое/маловероятное сейчас или в будущем: If I won the lottery, I'd travel the world. If she were here, she'd know. В аккуратном языке для всех лиц were: If I were you… В главной части would/could/might.

kk.title: Second — қиял осы/болашақ
kk.html: If + past simple, … would + база — қазіргі/болашақтағы қиял немесе екіталай: If I won the lottery, I'd travel the world. If she were here, she'd know. Ресми тілде барлық жаққа were: If I were you… Бас сөйлемде would/could/might.

ru.title: Third — нереальное прошлое
ru.html: If + past perfect, … would have + past participle — воображаемая противоположность реального (сожаление/предположение о прошлом): If I had known, I'd have come. If they hadn't helped, we'd have failed. В главной части could/might have: We might have won if we'd tried harder.

kk.title: Third — қиял өткен
kk.html: If + past perfect, … would have + past participle — шын болғанның қиял қарама-қарсысы (өткенге өкініш/жорамал): If I had known, I'd have come. If they hadn't helped, we'd have failed. Бас сөйлемде could/might have: We might have won if we'd tried harder.

ru.title: Нюанс: вероятность и ‘if I were to’
ru.html: Сдвиг времени показывает отдалённость от реальности: first = вероятно; second = маловероятно; third = невозможно (уже прошло). Чтобы будущая гипотеза звучала тактичнее/формальнее: If I were to resign, who would replace me? или Should you change your mind, call me (раздел 20).

kk.title: Нюанс: ықтималдық және ‘if I were to’
kk.html: Шақ ауысуы шындықтан алшақтықты білдіреді: first = ықтимал; second = екіталай; third = мүмкін емес (бітті). Болашақ жорамалды сыпайырақ/ресмиірек ету үшін: If I were to resign, who would replace me? немесе Should you change your mind, call me (20-бөлім).

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ If it will rain, we'll stay → ✓ If it rains, we'll stay. ✗ If I would have money → ✓ If I had money (в if-части нет would). ✗ If I had known, I would came → ✓ I would have come.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ If it will rain, we'll stay → ✓ If it rains, we'll stay. ✗ If I would have money → ✓ If I had money (if-бөлікте would жоқ). ✗ If I had known, I would came → ✓ I would have come.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Zero: present + present. First: present + will.
  • Second: past simple + would; ‘were’ для всех лиц.
  • Third: past perfect + would have + p.p.
  • В if-части никогда ‘will/would’.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Zero: present + present. First: present + will.
  • Second: past simple + would; барлық жаққа ‘were’.
  • Third: past perfect + would have + p.p.
  • If-бөлікте ‘will/would’ ешқашан.

activities.prompt: Sort each sentence.

items.t: If you freeze water, it expands.
If we hurry, we'll catch it.
If I were taller, I'd play basketball.
If she had asked, I'd have helped.
If metal gets hot, it expands.
If they'd left earlier, they'd have arrived.

activities.why: present+present = zero; present+will = first; past+would = second; past perfect+would have = third.

items.s: If it rains, we'll cancel.
If I were rich, I'd help.
If it will rain, we'll cancel.
If I would know, I'd tell you.

activities.why: the if-clause never takes will/would: If it rains…; If I knew…
unreal present → past ‘were’ (formal for all persons) → were.

activities.before: If we had booked earlier, we
activities.why: unreal past result → would have got.

activities.after: the manager, ask her to call me. (see)
activities.why: real future, present in the if-clause → see (main clause = imperative).
third conditional: past perfect + would have + p.p.

activities.q: You think winning is very unlikely but possible. Which fits?
options: If we had won, we'd have celebrated.
why: the first conditional treats it as a real, likely possibility.
Correct — unlikely/imaginary → second conditional ‘If we won, we'd celebrate.’
the third conditional is for the past — it's over.

activities.q: “If you'd warned me, I ___ avoided the mistake.”
why: Correct — third conditional → would have avoided.
‘will have’ can't follow a past-perfect if-clause.
‘would’ alone lacks the past ‘have + p.p.’

activities.prompt: Sort each if-clause by whether it treats the situation as real or unreal.
activities.why: present-tense if-clauses treat things as real; past/past-perfect if-clauses mark them as unreal.
no ‘would’ in the if-clause → “If I had time, I would help you.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a third conditional (it didn't happen):
activities.prompt: I didn't set an alarm, so I overslept.
activities.answer: if i had set an alarm, i wouldn't have overslept
alts: if i'd set an alarm, i wouldn't have overslept
activities.why: unreal past → past perfect + would(n't) have + p.p.

activities.prompt: I don't have a car, so I can't drive you.
activities.answer: if i had a car, i could drive you
alts: if i had a car, i'd drive you
if i had a car i could drive you
activities.why: unreal present → past simple + could/would.

activities.scene: Choose the conditional that fits the reality.

steps.npc: Do you regret not taking that job?

options.t: A bit. If I'd accepted it, I'd probably be living in Berlin now.
options.why: a past condition with a present result (a mixed conditional — see Unit 19).

options.t: A bit. If I accepted it, I'd probably live in Berlin now.
options.why: the decision is over → past perfect: If I'd accepted it…

steps.npc: Would you move abroad if you could?

options.t: If I got a good offer, I'd seriously consider it.
options.why: an imaginable future → second conditional.

options.t: If I will get a good offer, I'd seriously consider it.
options.why: no ‘will’ in the if-clause → If I got a good offer…

activities.why: Match the conditional to reality: zero/first for real situations, second for unreal present/future, third for the unreal past — never using will/would in the if-clause.

learn.title: When the two times don't match
learn.html: Real life isn't always same-time. Mixed conditionals combine a condition and a result from different times — most often a past condition with a present result, or a present condition with a past result.

learn.title: Past condition → present result
learn.html:
If + past perfect, … would + base verb (present result):

If I had studied medicine, I'd be a doctor now. (past choice → present state)

If they hadn't missed the flight, they'd be home by now.

learn.title: Present condition → past result
learn.html:
If + past simple, … would have + past participle (past result):

If I weren't so clumsy, I wouldn't have dropped it. (a permanent trait → a past event)

If he were more careful, he wouldn't have crashed.

learn.title: Alternatives to if
learn.html:
• unless = if … not: Unless you hurry, we'll miss it.
  • provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that = only if: You can go as long as you're back by ten.
  • in case = as a precaution (not ‘if’): Take an umbrella in case it rains.
  • even if = whether or not: I'll go even if it snows.

learn.title: but for, otherwise, suppose/imagine
learn.html:
but for + noun/-ing = if it weren't/hadn't been for: But for your help, we'd have failed. (formal)

otherwise = if not, the result would differ: Write it down; otherwise you'll forget.

suppose/supposing/imagine introduce hypotheses: Suppose you lost your job — what would you do?

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ If I studied harder in school, I'd have a better job now. (past cause) → ✓ If I had studied harder…, I'd have a better job now.

✗ Unless you don't hurry… → ✓ Unless you hurry… (unless already = if not)

✗ But for you helped me… → ✓ But for your help…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Mixed: past perfect + would (past cause → present result); past simple + would have (present cause → past result).
  • unless = if not; provided/as long as = only if; even if = whether or not; in case = precaution.
  • but for + noun = if it weren't/hadn't been for; otherwise = if not.
  • Don't add a second ‘not’ after unless.

ru.title: Когда два времени не совпадают
ru.html: Жизнь не всегда одновременна. Смешанные условные соединяют условие и результат из разных времён — чаще прошлое условие + настоящий результат или настоящее условие + прошлый результат.

kk.title: Екі уақыт сәйкес келмегенде
kk.html: Өмір әрдайым бір уақытта емес. Аралас шарттылар әр түрлі уақыттағы шарт пен нәтижені біріктіреді — жиі өткен шарт + қазіргі нәтиже немесе қазіргі шарт + өткен нәтиже.

ru.title: Прошлое условие → настоящий результат
ru.html: If + past perfect, … would + база (настоящий результат): If I had studied medicine, I'd be a doctor now (прошлый выбор → настоящее состояние). If they hadn't missed the flight, they'd be home by now.

kk.title: Өткен шарт → қазіргі нәтиже
kk.html: If + past perfect, … would + база (қазіргі нәтиже): If I had studied medicine, I'd be a doctor now (өткен таңдау → қазіргі күй). If they hadn't missed the flight, they'd be home by now.

ru.title: Настоящее условие → прошлый результат
ru.html: If + past simple, … would have + past participle (прошлый результат): If I weren't so clumsy, I wouldn't have dropped it (постоянная черта → прошлое событие). If he were more careful, he wouldn't have crashed.

kk.title: Қазіргі шарт → өткен нәтиже
kk.html: If + past simple, … would have + past participle (өткен нәтиже): If I weren't so clumsy, I wouldn't have dropped it (тұрақты қасиет → өткен оқиға). If he were more careful, he wouldn't have crashed.

ru.title: Альтернативы if
ru.html: unless = if … not: Unless you hurry, we'll miss it. provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that = только если. in case = на всякий случай (не if): Take an umbrella in case it rains. even if = даже если: I'll go even if it snows.

kk.title: if-тің баламалары
kk.html: unless = if … not: Unless you hurry, we'll miss it. provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that = тек ... болса. in case = сақтық үшін (if емес): Take an umbrella in case it rains. even if = болса да: I'll go even if it snows.

ru.title: but for, otherwise, suppose
ru.html: but for + noun/-ing = если бы не: But for your help, we'd have failed (формально). otherwise = иначе: Write it down; otherwise you'll forget. suppose/supposing/imagine вводят гипотезу: Suppose you lost your job — what would you do?

kk.title: but for, otherwise, suppose
kk.html: but for + noun/-ing = егер ... болмағанда: But for your help, we'd have failed (ресми). otherwise = олай болмаса: Write it down; otherwise you'll forget. suppose/supposing/imagine жорамал енгізеді: Suppose you lost your job — what would you do?

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ If I studied harder in school, I'd have a better job now (прошлая причина) → ✓ If I had studied harder…, I'd have a better job now. ✗ Unless you don't hurry → ✓ Unless you hurry. ✗ But for you helped me → ✓ But for your help.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ If I studied harder in school, I'd have a better job now (өткен себеп) → ✓ If I had studied harder…, I'd have a better job now. ✗ Unless you don't hurry → ✓ Unless you hurry. ✗ But for you helped me → ✓ But for your help.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Смешанные: past perfect + would (прошлая причина → настоящий результат); past simple + would have (настоящая причина → прошлый результат).
  • unless = if not; provided/as long as = только если; even if = даже если; in case = предосторожность.
  • but for + noun = если бы не; otherwise = иначе.
  • После unless не добавляйте второе ‘not’.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Аралас: past perfect + would (өткен себеп → қазіргі нәтиже); past simple + would have (қазіргі себеп → өткен нәтиже).
  • unless = if not; provided/as long as = тек ... болса; even if = болса да; in case = сақтық.
  • but for + noun = егер ... болмағанда; otherwise = олай болмаса.
  • unless-тен кейін екінші ‘not’ қоспаңыз.

activities.prompt: Match each connector to its meaning.

items.t: take cash in case the card fails

activities.why: unless = if not; provided/as long as = only if; in case = precaution; even if = whether or not.

items.s: If I'd married her, I'd be happier now.
Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late.
But for your help, we'd have lost.

activities.why: unless already means ‘if not’ — no second negative.

activities.after: that email, I wouldn't be in this mess now. (not / send)
activities.why: past cause with a present result → past perfect hadn't sent + would (present).

activities.after: so forgetful, she wouldn't have missed the meeting. (not / be)
activities.why: a permanent trait (present) with a past result → past simple weren't + would have.

activities.after: the traffic, we'd have arrived on time. (But for)
activities.why: but for + noun = if it hadn't been for → But for the traffic.
as long as = only if / on condition that (+ present).

activities.q: “He crashed the car yesterday. He's a reckless driver.” Combine:
options: If he weren't so reckless, he wouldn't have crashed.
If he hadn't been reckless, he wouldn't crash.
If he isn't reckless, he won't crash.
why: Correct — a present trait (weren't so reckless) + a past result (wouldn't have crashed).
this loses the ongoing trait — his recklessness isn't just past.
a real conditional doesn't fit a past event that already happened.

activities.q: “I'll accept the offer ___ they raise the salary — otherwise, I'm staying put.”
why: ‘unless they raise it’ would mean you accept when they DON'T raise it — the opposite.
Correct — the condition for accepting is a raise → if they raise it.
‘even if’ would mean the raise makes no difference.

activities.typeLabel: Which time is the result in?
activities.prompt: For each mixed conditional, is the result present or past?

items.t: If I'd saved money, I'd be richer now
If I were fitter, I'd have finished the race
If she'd taken the job, she'd live abroad now
If he weren't so shy, he'd have spoken up

activities.why: ‘would + base’ = present result; ‘would have + p.p.’ = past result.
unless already = if not → “Unless you water it, it will die.”

activities.prompt: I didn't learn to drive, so I can't take the job now.
activities.answer: if i had learned to drive, i could take the job now
alts: if i'd learned to drive, i could take the job now
if i had learnt to drive, i could take the job now
activities.why: past cause → present result: past perfect + could (present).

activities.prompt: If you hadn't reminded me, I would have forgotten.
activities.answer: but for your reminder, i would have forgotten
alts: but for you reminding me, i would have forgotten
activities.why: but for + noun/-ing = if it hadn't been for.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: what-ifs about the past
activities.scene: Choose the mixed conditional or alternative that fits.

steps.npc: You seem happy in this city.

options.t: I am — but if I hadn't taken that scholarship, I wouldn't be here at all.
options.why: past cause → present result (mixed).

options.t: I am — but if I didn't take that scholarship, I wouldn't be here.
options.why: the decision is over → If I hadn't taken that scholarship…

steps.npc: Will you renew the lease?

options.t: Yes, provided the rent doesn't go up. Otherwise, I'll look elsewhere.
options.why: provided = only if; otherwise = if not.

options.t: Yes, unless the rent doesn't go up. Otherwise, I'll look elsewhere.
options.why: you'd renew if the rent stays the same → provided it doesn't go up (unless would reverse it).

activities.why: Use mixed conditionals when condition and result are in different times, and know the alternatives to if — unless (if not), provided/as long as (only if), even if (whether or not), in case (precaution), and but for (if it weren't/hadn't been for).

learn.title: Dropping ‘if’ — a formal option
learn.html:
In formal or literary English, you can omit if and invert the subject and auxiliary. It sounds more elegant and is common in writing, business and hypotheticals.

If I had known, I'd have come.→ Had I known, I'd have come.
If you should need help, call.→ Should you need help, call.

learn.title: The three inversion patterns
learn.html:
• Were + subject (second conditional): If I were rich → Were I rich, I'd travel.
  • Had + subject (third conditional): If she had asked → Had she asked, I'd have agreed.
  • Should + subject (first, tentative): If you should see him → Should you see him, tell him.

learn.title: Were to & negatives
learn.html:
Were + subject + to-infinitive makes a future hypothesis more tentative: Were the deal to collapse, thousands would lose their jobs.

Negatives don't contract — put not after the subject: Had he not warned us… (not Hadn't he). Were it not for your support…

learn.title: Register & meaning
learn.html:
Inverted conditionals are more formal than if-clauses — typical of official writing, news and polite requests: Should you require further information, please contact us.

They carry the same meaning as the ordinary conditional; only the register changes. Don't overuse them in casual speech, where they can sound stiff.

learn.title: Only used with these auxiliaries
learn.html: Inversion for conditionals works with were, had, should — not with ordinary verbs. You cannot invert a present-simple if-clause: Rain it tomorrow…. For those, keep if, or use should: Should it rain tomorrow…

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Would I have known… → ✓ Had I known… (use had, not would)

✗ Hadn't he helped, we'd have failed. → ✓ Had he not helped…

✗ If were I you… → ✓ Were I you… (invert, and drop if)

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Drop ‘if’ and invert with were, had or should for a formal tone.
  • Were I…, Had I…, Should you…; Were … to for tentative future hypotheses.
  • Negatives: not goes after the subject (Had he not…, Were it not for…).
  • Same meaning as the if-conditional; just more formal.

ru.title: Опустить ‘if’ — формальный вариант
ru.html: В формальном/литературном английском можно опустить if и сделать инверсию подлежащего и вспомогательного. Звучит изящнее, часто в письме: If I had known → Had I known. If you should need help → Should you need help.

kk.title: ‘if’-ті түсіру — ресми нұсқа
kk.html: Ресми немесе әдеби ағылшында if-ті түсіріп, бастауыш пен көмекшіні орын алмастыруға болады. Бұл әсем естіледі, жазуда жиі: If I had known → Had I known. If you should need help → Should you need help.

ru.title: Три модели инверсии
ru.html: Were + subject (second): If I were rich → Were I rich, I'd travel. Had + subject (third): If she had asked → Had she asked, I'd have agreed. Should + subject (first, осторожно): If you should see him → Should you see him, tell him.

kk.title: Үш инверсия үлгісі
kk.html: Were + subject (second): If I were rich → Were I rich, I'd travel. Had + subject (third): If she had asked → Had she asked, I'd have agreed. Should + subject (first, сақтықпен): If you should see him → Should you see him, tell him.

ru.title: Were to & отрицание
ru.html: Were + subject + to-infinitive делает будущую гипотезу тактичнее: Were the deal to collapse, thousands would lose their jobs. Отрицание не стягивается — not после подлежащего: Had he not warned us… (не Hadn't he). Were it not for your support…

kk.title: Were to & болымсыз
kk.html: Were + subject + to-infinitive болашақ жорамалды сыпайырақ етеді: Were the deal to collapse, thousands would lose their jobs. Болымсыз қысқармайды — not бастауыштан кейін: Had he not warned us… (Hadn't he емес). Were it not for your support…

ru.title: Регистр и значение
ru.html: Инверсионные условные формальнее if — официальное письмо, новости, вежливые просьбы: Should you require further information, please contact us. Значение то же, меняется лишь регистр. В обычной речи не злоупотребляйте.

kk.title: Регистр және мағына
kk.html: Инверсиялы шарттылар if-тен ресмиірек — ресми жазу, жаңалық, сыпайы өтініш: Should you require further information, please contact us. Мағынасы бірдей, тек регистр өзгереді. Кәдімгі сөйлеуде асыра қолданбаңыз.

ru.title: Только с этими вспомогательными
ru.html: Инверсия условий работает с were, had, should — не с обычными глаголами. Present-simple if-часть инвертировать нельзя: Rain it tomorrow…. Для них оставьте if или используйте should: Should it rain tomorrow…

kk.title: Тек осы көмекшілермен
kk.html: Шарттылардағы инверсия were, had, should-пен ғана — кәдімгі етістіктермен емес. Present-simple if-бөлікті инверсиялауға болмайды: Rain it tomorrow…. Ол үшін if-ті сақтаңыз немесе should: Should it rain tomorrow…

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Would I have known… → ✓ Had I known… ✗ Hadn't he helped… → ✓ Had he not helped… ✗ If were I you… → ✓ Were I you…

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Would I have known… → ✓ Had I known… ✗ Hadn't he helped… → ✓ Had he not helped… ✗ If were I you… → ✓ Were I you…

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Опустите ‘if’ и инвертируйте с were, had, should — формальный тон.
  • Were I…, Had I…, Should you…; Were … to — тактичная будущая гипотеза.
  • Отрицание: not после подлежащего (Had he not…, Were it not for…).
  • Значение как у if-условного, просто формальнее.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• ‘if’-ті түсіріп, were, had, should-пен инверсия — ресми стиль.
  • Were I…, Had I…, Should you…; Were … to — сақ болашақ жорамал.
  • Болымсыз: not бастауыштан кейін (Had he not…, Were it not for…).
  • Мағынасы if-шарттымен бірдей, тек ресмиірек.

activities.prompt: Match each conditional type to the word that inverts.
activities.why: were → second; had → third; should → first (tentative).

items.s: Had I known, I'd have called.
Should you need anything, let me know.
Would I have known, I'd have called.
Hadn't he arrived, we'd have started.

activities.why: invert with had (not would); negatives keep ‘not’ after the subject: Had he not arrived…

activities.after: I realised the risk, I'd never have signed. (Had)
activities.why: third conditional inversion → Had I realised…

activities.after: I in your position, I'd resign. (Were)
activities.why: second conditional inversion → Were I in your position…

activities.after: warned us, the accident would have happened. (not)
activities.why: the negative goes after the subject → Had he not warned us…
tentative first conditional inversion with ‘should’.

activities.q: Formal version of “If the company had invested earlier, it would be profitable now”:
options: Would the company invest earlier, it would be profitable now.
Had the company invested earlier, it would be profitable now.
Should the company invest earlier, it would be profitable now.
why: you can't invert with ‘would’.
Correct — third-condition inversion → Had the company invested…
‘should’ signals a tentative future, not a past cause.

activities.q: Which makes the future hypothesis most tentative and formal?
options: If the fund collapses, investors lose everything.
Were the fund to collapse, investors would lose everything.
If the fund will collapse, investors would lose everything.
why: a plain first conditional, not tentative.
Correct — ‘Were … to collapse’ is the formal, tentative option.
‘if … will’ is ungrammatical.

activities.prompt: Sort by whether inversion is possible.
activities.why: only were/had/should invert; present-simple if-clauses stay with ‘if’ (or use ‘should’).
third-conditional inversion → “Had you not helped, we would have failed.”

activities.prompt: If I had known about the meeting, I would have prepared.
activities.answer: had i known about the meeting, i would have prepared
alts: had i known about the meeting i would have prepared
activities.why: drop ‘if’, invert had → Had I known…

activities.prompt: If you require assistance, please ask a member of staff.
activities.answer: should you require assistance, please ask a member of staff
alts: should you require assistance please ask a member of staff
activities.why: tentative/polite first conditional → Should you require assistance…

activities.scene: Choose the formal inverted form.

steps.npc: How should I end the client email?

options.t: “Should you have any questions, please don't hesitate to contact me.”
options.why: formal tentative conditional with inversion (Should you…).

options.t: “Would you have any questions, please contact me.”
options.why: inversion uses ‘should’, not ‘would’: Should you have any questions…

steps.npc: And to explain the earlier delay?

options.t: “Had we been informed sooner, we would have acted immediately.”
options.why: third-conditional inversion (Had we been…).

options.t: “If we would have been informed sooner, we would have acted.”
options.why: drop ‘if’ and invert: Had we been informed sooner…

activities.why: For a formal tone, drop ‘if’ and invert with were, had or should (and Were … to for tentative futures); the meaning is unchanged, and negatives keep ‘not’ after the subject.

learn.title: The 'unreal past' beyond conditionals
learn.html: Several structures use past forms to talk about the present or future — expressing wishes, regrets, preferences and things that are overdue. The past tense signals unreality, not past time.

learn.title: wish & if only
learn.html:
I wish I knew the answer.wish + past = a present situation you'd like to be different
I wish I had studied harder.wish + past perfect = a past regret
I wish he would stop.wish + would = annoyance / desire for change (others' behaviour)

if only works the same way but is more emphatic: If only I had more time!

learn.title: wish … would — the rules
learn.html:
wish + would expresses a wish for someone/something to change their behaviour, often with irritation: I wish you'd listen. · I wish it would stop raining.

But you can't use would about yourself for a state — use the past: I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

learn.title: it's (high/about) time
learn.html:
It's time + past simple says something is overdue: It's time we left. · It's high time you found a job.

Alternatively, it's time + (for someone) to: It's time to go. · It's time for you to decide.

learn.title: would rather & would sooner
learn.html:
would rather + base verb (same subject): I'd rather stay in. But would rather + someone + past (different subject) for preferences about others: I'd rather you didn't smoke. · I'd rather she came tomorrow.

For the past: would rather + have + p.p.: I'd rather have stayed home.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I wish I know the answer. → ✓ I wish I knew the answer.

✗ It's time we go. → ✓ It's time we went.

✗ I'd rather you don't do that. → ✓ I'd rather you didn't do that.

✗ I wish I would be richer. → ✓ I wish I were richer.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• wish/if only + past = present wish; + past perfect = past regret; + would = wish for change (others).
  • It's (high) time + past simple = it's overdue (It's time we left).
  • would rather + base (same subject); + someone + past (different subject).
  • The past form here signals unreality, not past time.

ru.title: «Нереальное прошлое» вне условных
ru.html: Несколько структур используют прошедшие формы для настоящего/будущего — желания, сожаления, предпочтения, назревшее. Прошедшее время сигналит нереальность, а не прошлое время.

kk.title: Шарттыдан тыс «шартты өткен»
kk.html: Бірнеше құрылым өткен форманы осы/болашақ туралы қолданады — тілек, өкініш, қалау, кешіккен нәрсе. Өткен шақ шындыққа жат екенін білдіреді, өткен уақытты емес.

ru.title: wish & if only
ru.html: I wish I knew the answer (wish + past = настоящее, которое хочешь изменить). I wish I had studied harder (wish + past perfect = сожаление о прошлом). I wish he would stop (wish + would = раздражение/желание перемен — поведение других). if only — так же, но эмфатичнее: If only I had more time!

kk.title: wish & if only
kk.html: I wish I knew the answer (wish + past = өзгерткің келетін қазіргі жағдай). I wish I had studied harder (wish + past perfect = өткенге өкініш). I wish he would stop (wish + would = ренжу/өзгеріс тілеу — басқалардың мінезі). if only — солай, бірақ күштірек: If only I had more time!

ru.title: wish … would — правила
ru.html: wish + would выражает желание, чтобы кто-то/что-то изменил поведение, часто с раздражением: I wish you'd listen. I wish it would stop raining. Но о своём состоянии would нельзя — бери прошедшее: I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

kk.title: wish … would — ережелер
kk.html: wish + would біреу/бірдеңенің мінезін өзгертуін тілейді, жиі ренжумен: I wish you'd listen. I wish it would stop raining. Бірақ өз күйің туралы would қолданылмайды — өткенді ал: I wish I would be taller → I wish I were taller.

ru.title: it's (high/about) time
ru.html: It's time + past simple говорит, что что-то назрело: It's time we left. It's high time you found a job. Или it's time + (for someone) to: It's time to go. It's time for you to decide.

kk.title: it's (high/about) time
kk.html: It's time + past simple бірдеңенің кешіккенін айтады: It's time we left. It's high time you found a job. Немесе it's time + (for someone) to: It's time to go. It's time for you to decide.

ru.title: would rather & would sooner
ru.html: would rather + база (один субъект): I'd rather stay in. Но would rather + кто-то + past (другой субъект) — о предпочтениях к другим: I'd rather you didn't smoke. I'd rather she came tomorrow. Для прошлого: would rather + have + p.p.: I'd rather have stayed home.

kk.title: would rather & would sooner
kk.html: would rather + база (бір субъект): I'd rather stay in. Бірақ would rather + біреу + past (басқа субъект) — басқалар туралы қалау: I'd rather you didn't smoke. I'd rather she came tomorrow. Өткен үшін: would rather + have + p.p.: I'd rather have stayed home.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ I wish I know the answer → ✓ I wish I knew the answer. ✗ It's time we go → ✓ It's time we went. ✗ I'd rather you don't do that → ✓ I'd rather you didn't do that. ✗ I wish I would be richer → ✓ I wish I were richer.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ I wish I know the answer → ✓ I wish I knew the answer. ✗ It's time we go → ✓ It's time we went. ✗ I'd rather you don't do that → ✓ I'd rather you didn't do that. ✗ I wish I would be richer → ✓ I wish I were richer.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• wish/if only + past = желание о настоящем; + past perfect = сожаление о прошлом; + would = желание перемен (другие).
  • It's (high) time + past simple = назрело (It's time we left).
  • would rather + база (один субъект); + кто-то + past (другой субъект).
  • Прошедшая форма здесь = нереальность, не прошлое.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• wish/if only + past = қазіргі тілек; + past perfect = өткенге өкініш; + would = өзгеріс тілеу (басқалар).
  • It's (high) time + past simple = кешікті (It's time we left).
  • would rather + база (бір субъект); + біреу + past (басқа субъект).
  • Мұндағы өткен форма шындыққа жат екенін білдіреді.

activities.typeLabel: Present wish, past regret, or wish for change?
activities.prompt: Sort each ‘wish’ sentence.

items.t: I wish I lived by the sea.
I wish I'd taken that job.
I wish you would call more often.
I wish I were more confident.
I wish they hadn't sold the house.
I wish it would stop snowing.

activities.why: wish + past = present wish; + past perfect = past regret; + would = a wish for someone/something to change.

items.s: It's high time you learned to drive.
I'd rather you didn't tell anyone.
I wish I know the answer.
It's time we go home.

activities.why: unreal past forms: I wish I knew…; It's time we went home.
a present situation you'd change → wish + past knew.
it's (high) time + past simple → did.
would rather + someone + past → asked.
if only + past perfect = an emphatic past regret.

activities.q: “I wish my neighbour ___ his music down — it's every night!”
why: ‘turned’ would describe a hypothetical state, not a change of behaviour.
Correct — a wish for someone to change annoying behaviour → wish + would turn.
the present simple can't express this wish.

activities.q: “The film was a disaster. I ___ stayed home.”
why: ‘would rather stayed’ is ungrammatical.
Correct — a past preference → 'd rather have stayed home.
‘wish’ needs its own clause: I wish I'd stayed home.

activities.prompt: For each, does ‘would rather’ take a base verb or a past-tense clause?
activities.why: same subject → base verb; different subject → past-tense clause.
a past regret → wish + past perfect → “I wish I hadn't said that.”

activities.prompt: I'm sorry I can't speak French.
activities.answer: i wish i could speak french
activities.why: a present wish → wish + could/past.

activities.prompt: You should have started revising by now.
alts: it's high time you started revising
it's about time you started revising
activities.why: overdue → It's (high) time + past simple.

activities.scene: Choose the unreal-past structure that fits.

steps.npc: You seem down about your job.

options.t: I am. I wish I'd trained as a nurse — and it's high time I made a change.
options.why: past regret (wish + past perfect) + overdue (it's high time + past).

options.t: I am. I wish I trained as a nurse — and it's high time I make a change.
options.why: a past regret → wish I'd trained; overdue → it's high time I made a change.

steps.npc: Would you go back to studying?

options.t: Honestly, I'd rather retrain now than regret it later.
options.why: would rather + base verb (same subject).

options.t: Honestly, I'd rather to retrain now than regret it later.
options.why: would rather + base verb, no ‘to’: I'd rather retrain.

activities.why: Use unreal past forms for present situations: wish/if only + past (or past perfect for regrets, would for change), It's (high) time + past simple, and would rather + base (same subject) or + someone + past (different subject).

learn.title: Why choose the passive?
learn.html:
The passive isn't just a mechanical flip of the active. B2 is about why and when a good writer chooses it. We use the passive when the action or its receiver matters more than the doer — or when the doer is unknown, obvious, unimportant, or best left unsaid.

Someone stole my bike.active — focus on ‘someone’
My bike was stolen.passive — the doer is unknown/irrelevant

learn.title: Form across the tenses
learn.html:
be + past participle, with be in the right tense:

  • present: is/are made · continuous: is/are being made
  • past: was/were made · past continuous: was/were being made
  • present perfect: has/have been made · past perfect: had been made
  • future: will be made · modals: must be made, can be done

learn.title: Reasons to use it
learn.html:
• Unknown/unimportant agent: The window has been broken.
  • Focus on the receiver/process: The vaccine is produced in three factories.
  • Formal / impersonal / scientific style: The samples were then heated to 90°C.
  • Tact / avoiding blame: Mistakes were made.
  • Keeping known information first (given → new), for smooth flow.

learn.title: The agent: by + doer
learn.html:
Include by + agent only when it adds important information: The novel was written by a teenager. Omit it when the doer is unknown, obvious or generic (people, they): The road is being repaired (obviously by workers).

Verbs with two objects can passivise either: They gave me a prize → I was given a prize (usual) / A prize was given to me.

learn.title: The get-passive
learn.html:
get + past participle is a more informal, dynamic passive, often for sudden or unwelcome events: He got arrested. · Our flight got cancelled. · They got married.

It stresses the change or the impact on the subject and is common in speech; avoid it in formal writing, where be is preferred.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ The bike was stole. → ✓ The bike was stolen. (past participle, not past simple)

✗ It was happened yesterday. → ✓ It happened yesterday. (intransitive verbs have no passive)

✗ The house is build now. → ✓ The house is being built now.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Passive = be + past participle; choose it to focus on the action/receiver or hide/omit the doer.
  • Add ‘by + agent’ only when it's informative.
  • The get-passive is informal and dynamic (got arrested/cancelled).
  • Only transitive verbs passivise; ‘happen/arrive’ can't.

ru.title: Зачем выбирать пассив?
ru.html: Пассив — не механический переворот актива. На B2 важно почему и когда его выбирают. Пассив нужен, когда действие или его получатель важнее деятеля, либо деятель неизвестен/очевиден/неважен/лучше умолчать. Someone stole my bike → My bike was stolen.

kk.title: Ырықсызды не үшін таңдаймыз?
kk.html: Ырықсыз — тек ырықтының механикалық аудармасы емес. B2-де маңыздысы — неге және қашан таңдау. Ырықсызды әрекет немесе оны қабылдаушы орындаушыдан маңыздырақ болғанда, немесе орындаушы белгісіз/анық/маңызсыз/айтпаған дұрыс болғанда қолданамыз. Someone stole my bike → My bike was stolen.

ru.title: Форма — по временам
ru.html: be + past participle, be в нужном времени: present is/are made; continuous is being made; past was made; perfect has been made; future will be made; modals must be made, can be done.

kk.title: Форма — шақтар бойынша
kk.html: be + past participle, be тиісті шақта: present is/are made; continuous is being made; past was made; perfect has been made; future will be made; modals must be made, can be done.

ru.title: Причины использования
ru.html: Неизвестный/неважный деятель: The window has been broken. Фокус на получателе/процессе: The vaccine is produced in three factories. Формальный/безличный/научный стиль: The samples were then heated. Такт/уход от вины: Mistakes were made. Известное вперёд (given → new).

kk.title: Қолдану себептері
kk.html: Белгісіз/маңызсыз орындаушы: The window has been broken. Қабылдаушыға/үдеріске назар: The vaccine is produced in three factories. Ресми/бейтарап/ғылыми стиль: The samples were then heated. Әдеп/кінәні болдырмау: Mistakes were made. Белгілі ақпаратты алдыға қою (given → new).

ru.title: Деятель: by + doer
ru.html: by + agent только если добавляет важное: The novel was written by a teenager. Если деятель неизвестен/очевиден/общий — опускайте: The road is being repaired. Глаголы с двумя дополнениями: They gave me a prize → I was given a prize (обычно) / A prize was given to me.

kk.title: Орындаушы: by + doer
kk.html: by + agent тек маңызды ақпарат қосса: The novel was written by a teenager. Орындаушы белгісіз/анық/жалпы болса, түсіріңіз: The road is being repaired. Екі объектілі етістіктер: They gave me a prize → I was given a prize (жиі) / A prize was given to me.

ru.title: get-пассив
ru.html: get + past participle — более неформальный, динамичный пассив, часто для внезапных/неприятных событий: He got arrested. Our flight got cancelled. They got married. Подчёркивает изменение/влияние на субъект, част в речи; в формальном письме предпочтителен be.

kk.title: get-passive
kk.html: get + past participle — бейресми, динамикалық ырықсыз, жиі кенет/жағымсыз оқиғаға: He got arrested. Our flight got cancelled. They got married. Ол субъектіге әсерді/өзгерісті баса көрсетеді, ауызекіде жиі; ресми жазуда be жақсырақ.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ The bike was stole → ✓ was stolen (причастие). ✗ It was happened → ✓ It happened (непереходный глагол без пассива). ✗ The house is build now → ✓ is being built now.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ The bike was stole → ✓ was stolen (past participle). ✗ It was happened → ✓ It happened (ырықсыз болмайтын етістік). ✗ The house is build now → ✓ is being built now.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Пассив = be + past participle; для фокуса на действии/получателе или чтобы скрыть деятеля.
  • ‘by + agent’ только если информативно.
  • get-пассив — неформальный, динамичный.
  • Пассив только у переходных; happen/arrive — нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Ырықсыз = be + past participle; әрекетке/қабылдаушыға назар не орындаушыны жасыру үшін.
  • ‘by + agent’ тек маңызды болса.
  • get-passive — бейресми, динамикалық.
  • Тек сабақты етістіктер ырықсызданады; happen/arrive — жоқ.

activities.prompt: Match each passive sentence to the reason for choosing it.

items.t: My car was broken into last night.
Errors were made in the report.
The data were collected over a year.
Your account may have been misused.

activities.why: The passive foregrounds the action/receiver and lets the doer be unknown, impersonal, or diplomatically hidden.

items.s: The bridge was built in 1905.
A new law is being introduced.
The accident was happened at noon.
The window was broke by the storm.

activities.why: happen is intransitive (no passive); the participle of break is ‘broken’.

activities.before: You can't use the lift; it
activities.why: an action in progress → present continuous passive is being repaired.
a completed action with present relevance → present perfect passive has been dispatched.
modal passive → must + be + past participle → must be submitted.
passive with the person as subject (verb with two objects).

activities.q: Which sentence best keeps the agent because it's genuinely informative?
options: The report was written by someone.
The theory was proposed by Einstein.
The rubbish is collected by people.
why: ‘by someone’ adds nothing.
Correct — ‘by Einstein’ is important information worth including.
‘by people’ is generic and better omitted.

activities.q: In a formal accident report, which is most appropriate?
why: the get-passive is too informal for a report.
Correct — formal writing prefers the be-passive → were injured.
‘injured’ alone lacks the passive auxiliary.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by whether a passive is possible.
activities.why: Only verbs with an object (build, design) passivise; arrive/happen can't.
future passive needs ‘be’ → “will be announced tomorrow.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite in the passive, dropping the vague doer:
activities.prompt: Someone has moved my desk.
activities.why: the doer is unknown/unimportant → present perfect passive.

activities.instruction: Rewrite in the passive, keeping the informative agent:
activities.prompt: A local artist painted the mural.
activities.answer: the mural was painted by a local artist
alts: the mural was painted by a local artist.
activities.why: the agent adds information → keep ‘by a local artist’.

activities.scene: Choose the natural passive form and register.

steps.npc: Has my complaint been dealt with?

options.t: Yes — it's being investigated, and you'll be contacted within 48 hours.
options.why: passive with unknown/impersonal agents, present continuous + future.

options.t: Yes — we are investigating it, and we will contact you... it got looked at.
options.why: a formal reply keeps the be-passive: it's being investigated; you'll be contacted.

steps.npc: Why was there a delay?

options.t: Some errors were made in processing, and they've now been corrected.
options.why: tactful passive (errors were made) + present perfect passive.

options.t: Someone made some errors and got them corrected.
options.why: the diplomatic, formal choice is the be-passive: errors were made / have been corrected.

activities.why: Choose the passive to foreground the action or receiver and to leave out an unknown, obvious or awkward doer; add ‘by + agent’ only when informative, and reserve the get-passive for informal, dynamic events.

learn.title: Reporting impersonally
learn.html:
To report what people in general say, believe or know — without naming a source — English uses passive reporting structures. They are formal and common in news, academic and cautious writing.

It is said that she is very wealthy. · She is said to be very wealthy.

learn.title: Two patterns
learn.html:
It + passive + that-clauseIt is believed that the painting is genuine.
Subject + passive + to-infinitiveThe painting is believed to be genuine.

Common verbs: say, believe, think, know, report, expect, consider, claim, understand, allege, rumour (usually passive: is rumoured to…).

learn.title: Getting the time right
learn.html:
Match the infinitive to the time of the reported action:

  • same/later time → to + base: He is thought to be abroad.
  • earlier time → to have + p.p.: He is thought to have left the country.
  • ongoing → to be + -ing: They are said to be living in Rome.

learn.title: Past reporting
learn.html:
When the reporting verb is past, keep the frame consistent: It was reported that hundreds had fled. · The suspect was believed to have acted alone.

These structures let a writer report claims cautiously, without committing to their truth or naming who said them.

learn.title: there is said to be… & supposed to
learn.html:
With there: There are said to be caves beneath the city.

be supposed to often reports general belief or expectation: This restaurant is supposed to be excellent (people say so). Note the separate meaning ‘should’: You're supposed to book ahead.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ It is said she to be rich. → ✓ She is said to be rich. / It is said that she is rich.

✗ He is believed to left. → ✓ He is believed to have left.

✗ They say to be twins. → ✓ They are said to be twins.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Two frames: It + passive + that-clause; Subject + passive + to-infinitive.
  • Time: to + base (same/later), to have + p.p. (earlier), to be + -ing (ongoing).
  • Verbs: say, believe, think, know, report, claim, allege, rumour, understand.
  • Formal & cautious — reports claims without naming or endorsing them.

ru.title: Безличное сообщение
ru.html: Чтобы сообщить, что говорят/считают люди вообще, не называя источник, английский использует пассивные структуры сообщения. Они формальны, часты в новостях/академическом письме: It is said that she is very wealthy. She is said to be very wealthy.

kk.title: Бейтарап хабарлау
kk.html: Жалпы адамдардың не айтатынын/сенетінін дереккөз атамай хабарлау үшін ағылшын ырықсыз хабарлау құрылымдарын қолданады. Олар ресми, жаңалық/академиялық жазуда жиі: It is said that she is very wealthy. She is said to be very wealthy.

ru.title: Две модели
ru.html: It + passive + that-clause: It is believed that the painting is genuine. Subject + passive + to-infinitive: The painting is believed to be genuine. Глаголы: say, believe, think, know, report, expect, consider, claim, understand, allege, rumour (обычно пассив: is rumoured to…).

kk.title: Екі үлгі
kk.html: It + passive + that-clause: It is believed that the painting is genuine. Subject + passive + to-infinitive: The painting is believed to be genuine. Етістіктер: say, believe, think, know, report, expect, consider, claim, understand, allege, rumour (әдетте ырықсыз: is rumoured to…).

ru.title: Правильное время
ru.html: Согласуйте инфинитив со временем сообщаемого: то же/позже → to + база: He is thought to be abroad. Раньше → to have + p.p.: He is thought to have left. Длящееся → to be + -ing: They are said to be living in Rome.

kk.title: Уақытты дұрыс беру
kk.html: Инфинитивті хабарланған әрекет уақытымен сәйкестендіріңіз: бір уақыт/кейін → to + база: He is thought to be abroad. Ертерек → to have + p.p.: He is thought to have left. Жалғасқан → to be + -ing: They are said to be living in Rome.

ru.title: Сообщение в прошлом
ru.html: Если глагол сообщения прошедший, сохраняйте согласование: It was reported that hundreds had fled. The suspect was believed to have acted alone. Эти структуры позволяют сообщать осторожно, не ручаясь за истинность и не называя источник.

kk.title: Өткен хабарлау
kk.html: Хабарлау етістігі өткен болса, шеңберді сақтаңыз: It was reported that hundreds had fled. The suspect was believed to have acted alone. Бұл құрылымдар пікірлерді сақтықпен, растығына кепіл бермей әрі кім айтқанын атамай хабарлайды.

ru.title: there is said to be… & supposed to
ru.html: С there: There are said to be caves beneath the city. be supposed to часто сообщает общее мнение/ожидание: This restaurant is supposed to be excellent (так говорят). Отличайте значение ‘should’: You're supposed to book ahead.

kk.title: there is said to be… & supposed to
kk.html: there-мен: There are said to be caves beneath the city. be supposed to жиі жалпы сенімді/күтуді хабарлайды: This restaurant is supposed to be excellent (солай дейді). ‘should’ мағынасын да ажыратыңыз: You're supposed to book ahead.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ It is said she to be rich → ✓ She is said to be rich / It is said that she is rich. ✗ He is believed to left → ✓ He is believed to have left. ✗ They say to be twins → ✓ They are said to be twins.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ It is said she to be rich → ✓ She is said to be rich / It is said that she is rich. ✗ He is believed to left → ✓ He is believed to have left. ✗ They say to be twins → ✓ They are said to be twins.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Два каркаса: It + passive + that-clause; Subject + passive + to-infinitive.
  • Время: to + база (то же/позже), to have + p.p. (раньше), to be + -ing (длящееся).
  • Глаголы: say, believe, think, know, report, claim, allege, rumour, understand.
  • Формально и осторожно — сообщает мнение, не называя/не одобряя.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Екі шеңбер: It + passive + that-clause; Subject + passive + to-infinitive.
  • Уақыт: to + база (бір/кейін), to have + p.p. (ертерек), to be + -ing (жалғасқан).
  • Етістіктер: say, believe, think, know, report, claim, allege, rumour, understand.
  • Ресми әрі сақ — пікірді атамай/қолдамай хабарлайды.

activities.prompt: Sort each by its structure.

items.t: It is thought that he is guilty.
He is thought to be guilty.
It is said that they left.
They are said to have left.
It is reported that talks failed.
The talks are reported to have failed.

activities.why: Both frames report impersonally; one uses ‘It + that’, the other ‘Subject + to-infinitive’.

items.s: She is believed to be innocent.
It is known that the drug works.
He is said to left the country.
It is said she to be rich.

activities.why: earlier action → to have left; and the ‘It…’ frame needs a that-clause: It is said that she is rich.

activities.after: losses this year. (report / make — general belief)
activities.why: an ongoing situation reported now → is reported to be making (or ‘to make’).
an action earlier than the reporting → to have + p.p. → are believed to have entered.

activities.after: that the two companies will merge. (It / expect)
activities.why: the ‘It…’ frame → It is expected that…
subject + passive + perfect infinitive (an earlier action).

activities.q: They think the volcano erupted centuries ago. → The volcano ___ centuries ago.
why: ‘to erupt’ places it at the same/later time.
Correct — an earlier event → is thought to have erupted.
‘to be erupting’ would mean it's happening now.

activities.q: Rewrite “It is alleged that he stole the funds” with ‘He’ as subject:
options: He is alleged to steal the funds.
He is alleged to have stolen the funds.
He is alleged stealing the funds.
why: the theft is earlier than the reporting.
Correct — earlier action → He is alleged to have stolen the funds.
the pattern needs a to-infinitive, not -ing.

activities.prompt: Which infinitive does each reported meaning need?
activities.why: Match the infinitive to when the reported action happens relative to the report.
subject + passive + to-infinitive → “is expected to resign tomorrow.”

activities.prompt: People say the castle is haunted.
activities.answer: it is said that the castle is haunted
alts: it is said that the castle is haunted.
activities.why: impersonal report → It is said that…

activities.prompt: Police believe the suspect fled abroad.
activities.answer: the suspect is believed to have fled abroad
alts: the suspect is believed to have fled abroad.
activities.why: earlier action → is believed to have fled.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: reporting the news cautiously
activities.scene: Choose the impersonal reporting form.

steps.npc: What's the latest on the merger?

options.t: The two firms are understood to be finalising the deal.
options.why: subject + passive + to be + -ing (ongoing, unconfirmed).

options.t: The two firms understand to finalise the deal.
options.why: impersonal report → are understood to be finalising.

steps.npc: And the earlier rumours?

options.t: It was reported that talks had collapsed, but that proved false.
options.why: past reporting frame with backshift (had collapsed).

options.t: It reported that talks collapse, but that proved false.
options.why: passive past reporting → It was reported that talks had collapsed.

activities.why: Report impersonally with ‘It + passive + that-clause’ or ‘Subject + passive + to-infinitive’, matching the infinitive to the time (to have + p.p. for earlier actions) — a formal, cautious way to relay claims.

learn.title: Arranging for someone else to do it
learn.html:
The causative says you arrange for someone else to do a job — you don't do it yourself. The focus is on the result, not the worker.

I painted the house.I did it myself
I had the house painted.I arranged for someone to paint it

learn.title: Form: have/get + object + past participle
learn.html:
have + object + past participle (neutral) and get + object + past participle (more informal): We're having the roof fixed. · I need to get my hair cut.

It works in any tense: I've had my eyes tested. · She's getting her car serviced. · You should have it checked.

learn.title: Word order matters
learn.html:
The order object + past participle is essential. Compare:

I had cut my hair.past perfect — I cut it myself
I had my hair cut.causative — a hairdresser did it

Getting this order wrong changes the meaning entirely.

learn.title: The ‘experience’ meaning
learn.html:
The same structure can describe something (often unpleasant) that happened to the subject — no arranging involved: She had her bag stolen. · He got his phone confiscated. · They had their flight cancelled.

Context tells you whether it's a service you arranged or an event you experienced.

learn.title: have/get someone to do; get + -ing
learn.html:
To name the doer and stress the action: have somebody do (bare infinitive) / get somebody to do: I'll have the technician check it. · I'll get the technician to check it.

get + object + -ing = make something start working: I finally got the engine running.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I cut my hair yesterday. (at the salon) → ✓ I had my hair cut yesterday.

✗ I had repaired my car. (by a mechanic) → ✓ I had my car repaired.

✗ She had stolen her purse. → ✓ She had her purse stolen.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• have/get + object + past participle = arrange for a service (focus on the result).
  • Word order (object before participle) is what makes it causative.
  • The same form can mean something happened to you (had my bag stolen).
  • Name the doer with have sb do / get sb to do; get sth -ing = make it start.

ru.title: Организовать, чтобы сделал другой
ru.html: Каузатив означает, что ты организуешь, чтобы работу сделал кто-то другой, а не сам. Фокус — на результате. I painted the house (сам) vs I had the house painted (мне покрасили).

kk.title: Басқа біреуге істетуді ұйымдастыру
kk.html: Каузатив — жұмысты өзің емес, басқа біреуге істетуді ұйымдастырғаныңды білдіреді. Назар — нәтижеде. I painted the house (өзім) vs I had the house painted (біреуге бояттым).

ru.title: Форма: have/get + object + past participle
ru.html: have + object + past participle (нейтрально) и get + object + past participle (неформальнее): We're having the roof fixed. I need to get my hair cut. В любом времени: I've had my eyes tested. She's getting her car serviced. You should have it checked.

kk.title: Форма: have/get + object + past participle
kk.html: have + object + past participle (бейтарап) және get + object + past participle (бейресмиірек): We're having the roof fixed. I need to get my hair cut. Кез келген шақта: I've had my eyes tested. She's getting her car serviced. You should have it checked.

ru.title: Порядок слов важен
ru.html: Порядок object + past participle обязателен. Сравни: I had cut my hair (past perfect — сам постриг) vs I had my hair cut (каузатив — постригли в салоне). Ошибка в порядке меняет смысл полностью.

kk.title: Сөз реті маңызды
kk.html: object + past participle реті міндетті. Салыстыр: I had cut my hair (past perfect — өзім кестім) vs I had my hair cut (каузатив — шаштараз кесті). Ретті шатастырсаң, мағына мүлде өзгереді.

ru.title: Значение «пережить»
ru.html: Та же структура описывает и то, что (часто неприятное) случилось с субъектом — без организации: She had her bag stolen. He got his phone confiscated. They had their flight cancelled. Контекст подскажет: услуга или пережитое событие.

kk.title: «Бастан кешу» мағынасы
kk.html: Дәл сол құрылым субъектіге (жиі жағымсыз) болып өткен нәрсені де білдіреді — ұйымдастыру жоқ: She had her bag stolen. He got his phone confiscated. They had their flight cancelled. Контекст ұйымдастырылған қызмет пе, әлде басынан өткен оқиға ма — соны айтады.

ru.title: have/get someone to do; get + -ing
ru.html: Чтобы назвать деятеля и подчеркнуть действие: have somebody do (без ‘to’) / get somebody to do: I'll have the technician check it. I'll get the technician to check it. get + object + -ing = заставить заработать: I finally got the engine running.

kk.title: have/get someone to do; get + -ing
kk.html: Орындаушыны атап, әрекетке назар аудару үшін: have somebody do (‘to’-сыз) / get somebody to do: I'll have the technician check it. I'll get the technician to check it. get + object + -ing = істей бастату: I finally got the engine running.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ I cut my hair yesterday (в салоне) → ✓ I had my hair cut yesterday. ✗ I had repaired my car (механик) → ✓ I had my car repaired. ✗ She had stolen her purse → ✓ She had her purse stolen.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ I cut my hair yesterday (салонда) → ✓ I had my hair cut yesterday. ✗ I had repaired my car (механик) → ✓ I had my car repaired. ✗ She had stolen her purse → ✓ She had her purse stolen.
