ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• have/get + object + past participle = организовать услугу (фокус на результате).
  • Порядок слов (object перед причастием) — суть каузатива.
  • Та же форма означает «случилось с тобой» (had my bag stolen).
  • Назвать деятеля: have sb do / get sb to do; get sth -ing = заставить заработать.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• have/get + object + past participle = қызметті ұйымдастыру (нәтижеге назар).
  • Сөз реті (object участиеден бұрын) — каузативтің кілті.
  • Дәл сол форма басыңнан өткенді де білдіреді (had my bag stolen).
  • Орындаушыны атау: have sb do / get sb to do; get sth -ing = істей бастату.

activities.typeLabel: Did you do it or arrange it?
activities.prompt: Sort each sentence by meaning.

items.t: He got his licence taken away.

activities.why: Plain verb = you did it; have/get + object + p.p. = a service; the same form can mean an event happened to you.

items.s: We had the windows cleaned.
She's getting her teeth whitened.
I had cleaned my windows. (by a company)
He had stolen his bike.

activities.why: causative needs object + past participle: had my windows cleaned; had his bike stolen.

activities.before: We can't use the kitchen; we're having it
activities.why: an arranged service in progress → have + object + past participle → redecorated.
arrange a service → get/have my eyes tested.

activities.after: last week at the salon. (her hair / colour — causative)
activities.why: object + past participle → had her hair coloured.
the ‘experience’ causative — it happened to them.

activities.q: “I had my photo taken” means:
options: A photographer took a photo of me.
I had already taken the photo.
why: that would be ‘I took my photo’.
Correct — causative: someone took the photo for me.
the past perfect would be ‘I had taken my photo’ (different word order).

activities.q: “I'll ___ the plumber ___ the leak.”
why: ‘have’ takes a bare infinitive, not ‘to’.
Correct — have + somebody + bare infinitive → have the plumber fix it.
‘get’ needs ‘to’: get the plumber to fix it.

activities.prompt: Sort each causative by whether it's an arranged service or something that happened.
activities.why: Some causatives are services you arrange; others describe an event (often unwelcome) that happened to you.
causative word order → “I had my laptop repaired at the shop.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a causative (a garage did it):
activities.prompt: A mechanic checked my brakes.
activities.why: arrange a service → have/get + object + past participle.

activities.prompt: Someone hacked her email.
activities.why: an event that happened to her → had her email hacked.

activities.scene: Choose the causative form.

steps.npc: Did you decorate the flat yourselves?

options.t: No, we had it done professionally — we're getting the floors sanded next.
options.why: arranged services → had it done, getting … sanded.

options.t: No, we had done it professionally — we're getting sand the floors next.
options.why: causative order: had it done; getting the floors sanded.

steps.npc: Any trouble along the way?

options.t: We had some tools stolen from the van, unfortunately.
options.why: experience causative — it happened to them.

options.t: We had stolen some tools from the van, unfortunately.
options.why: that means you stole them; you mean ‘had some tools stolen’.

activities.why: Use have/get + object + past participle to say you arranged a service (or that something happened to you), keeping the object-before-participle order — and name the doer with have sb do / get sb to do.

learn.title: Backshift: one step into the past
learn.html:
When the reporting verb is past (said, told), the reported verbs usually shift one tense back — the present becomes past, the past becomes past perfect. This is backshift.

“I'm tired.”→ He said he was tired.
“I saw it.”→ She said she had seen it.

learn.title: The backshift table
learn.html:
present simple → past simplework → worked
present cont. → past cont.am working → was working
present perfect / past → past perfecthave seen / saw → had seen
will → would · can → could · may → mightmust → had to

Also shift pronouns and time/place words: I→he, my→his, now→then, today→that day, here→there, this→that, tomorrow→the next day, ago→before.

learn.title: When you DON'T backshift
learn.html:
Backshift is optional (or skipped) when the statement is still true or general, or the reporting verb is present:

  • Still true: She said the earth is/was round. (both fine)
  • Present reporting verb: He says he 's busy.
  • Recent/immediate report: She just told me she can't come.

learn.title: Modals that don't shift
learn.html:
Some modals have no further past form, so they stay the same: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, had better: “I would help.” → He said he would help.

The past perfect also can't shift further: “I had already left.” → She said she had already left.

learn.title: say vs tell
learn.html:
tell needs a person object: She told me (that)… say doesn't: She said (that)… / She said to me…

✗ She said me… ✗ She told that… The conjunction that is optional after both.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He said me he was late. → ✓ He told me / He said he was late.

✗ She said she is tired. (past report) → ✓ She said she was tired.

✗ He said he will come. → ✓ He said he would come.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Past reporting verb → backshift one step (is→was, saw→had seen, will→would).
  • Shift pronouns and time/place words (now→then, tomorrow→the next day).
  • Don't backshift when it's still true or the reporting verb is present.
  • tell needs a person object; say doesn't.

ru.title: Backshift: на шаг в прошлое
ru.html: Если глагол сообщения прошедший (said, told), сообщаемые глаголы обычно сдвигаются на одно время назад — настоящее → прошедшее, прошедшее → past perfect. Это backshift. “I'm tired.” → He said he was tired. “I saw it.” → She said she had seen it.

kk.title: Backshift: бір қадам артқа
kk.html: Хабарлау етістігі өткен болса (said, told), хабарланған етістіктер әдетте бір шақ артқа жылжиды — осы шақ өткенге, өткен past perfect-ке. Бұл — backshift. “I'm tired.” → He said he was tired. “I saw it.” → She said she had seen it.

ru.title: Таблица backshift
ru.html: present simple → past simple; present cont. → past cont.; present perfect/past → past perfect; will → would, can → could, may → might, must → had to. Также местоимения и слова времени/места: I→he, now→then, today→that day, here→there, tomorrow→the next day, ago→before.

kk.title: Backshift кестесі
kk.html: present simple → past simple; present cont. → past cont.; present perfect/past → past perfect; will → would, can → could, may → might, must → had to. Есімдіктер мен уақыт/орын сөздері де: I→he, now→then, today→that day, here→there, tomorrow→the next day, ago→before.

ru.title: Когда backshift НЕ делаем
ru.html: Backshift необязателен (или отсутствует), если утверждение всё ещё верно/общее или глагол сообщения в настоящем: Still true: She said the earth is/was round. Present verb: He says he's busy. Свежее сообщение: She just told me she can't come.

kk.title: Backshift ҚАШАН болмайды
kk.html: Backshift міндетті емес (немесе жоқ), егер тұжырым әлі рас/жалпы болса немесе хабарлау етістігі осы шақта болса: Still true: She said the earth is/was round. Present verb: He says he's busy. Жаңа хабар: She just told me she can't come.

ru.title: Модальные, которые не сдвигаются
ru.html: У некоторых модальных нет дальнейшей прошедшей формы, поэтому они не меняются: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, had better: “I would help.” → He said he would help. Past perfect тоже дальше не сдвигается: “I had already left.” → She said she had already left.

kk.title: Жылжымайтын модальдар
kk.html: Кейбір модальдардың басқа өткен формасы жоқ, сондықтан өзгермейді: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, had better: “I would help.” → He said he would help. Past perfect те одан әрі жылжымайды: “I had already left.” → She said she had already left.

ru.title: say vs tell
ru.html: tell требует дополнение-лицо: She told me (that)… say — нет: She said (that)… / She said to me… ✗ She said me… ✗ She told that… that после обоих необязателен.

kk.title: say vs tell
kk.html: tell адам объектісін қажет етеді: She told me (that)… say — жоқ: She said (that)… / She said to me… ✗ She said me… ✗ She told that… that екеуінен кейін де міндетті емес.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ He said me he was late → ✓ He told me / He said he was late. ✗ She said she is tired (сообщение в прошлом) → ✓ She said she was tired. ✗ He said he will come → ✓ He said he would come.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ He said me he was late → ✓ He told me / He said he was late. ✗ She said she is tired (өткен хабар) → ✓ She said she was tired. ✗ He said he will come → ✓ He said he would come.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Прошедший глагол сообщения → backshift на шаг (is→was, saw→had seen, will→would).
  • Сдвигайте местоимения и слова времени/места.
  • Не сдвигаем, если всё ещё верно или глагол в настоящем.
  • tell требует лицо; say — нет.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Өткен хабарлау етістігі → бір қадам backshift (is→was, saw→had seen, will→would).
  • Есімдіктер мен уақыт/орын сөздерін жылжытыңыз.
  • Әлі рас болса не етістік осы шақта болса — backshift жоқ.
  • tell адам объектісін қажет етеді; say — жоқ.

activities.prompt: Match each direct form to its backshifted form.
activities.why: present → past; present perfect/past → past perfect; will/can/may → would/could/might.

items.s: He told me he was busy.
She said she had already eaten.
He said me he was busy.
She told that she was leaving.

activities.why: say doesn't take a person object (said he was busy); tell needs one (told me that…).
present continuous → past continuous → was learning.
will → would.

activities.after: me that the shop was closed.
activities.why: a person object follows → told me.
past simple → past perfect (had seen); ‘ago’-type shift not needed here.

activities.q: He told me yesterday, “Water boils at 100°C.” Reporting it now, which is best?
options: He told me water boiled at 100°C.
He told me water boils at 100°C.
He told me water had boiled at 100°C.
why: backshift is possible but sounds odd for a permanent truth.
Correct — a general truth stays present → water boils.
the past perfect is wrong for a timeless fact.

activities.q: On Monday she said, “I'll finish it tomorrow.” Reported later:
options: She said she'd finish it tomorrow.
She said she'd finish it the next day.
She said she will finish it the next day.
why: ‘tomorrow’ must shift when the report is later.
Correct — tomorrow → the next day, will → would.
‘will’ should backshift to ‘would’.

activities.prompt: Sort each modal/tense by whether it backshifts.
activities.why: can→could, will→would, must→had to; but would/might/should are already ‘past’ modals and don't shift.
‘say’ takes no person object → “She said that she was leaving” (or ‘She told me…’).

activities.instruction: Report this (she said it earlier today):
activities.prompt: “I've lost my keys and I can't get in.”
activities.answer: she said she had lost her keys and couldn't get in
alts: she said she'd lost her keys and couldn't get in
she said that she had lost her keys and couldn't get in
activities.why: present perfect → past perfect; can't → couldn't; I→she, my→her.

activities.prompt: “We're moving house next week.”
activities.answer: they said they were moving house the following week
alts: they said they were moving house the next week
they said that they were moving house the following week
activities.why: present continuous → past continuous; next week → the following/next week.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: passing on a message
activities.scene: Report accurately, with correct backshift.

steps.npc: What did the manager say about the deadline?

options.t: She said the report had to be ready by Friday and that she'd review it herself.
options.why: must → had to; will → 'd (would).

options.t: She said the report must be ready by Friday and she will review it.
options.why: backshift: had to be ready; she'd (would) review it.

steps.npc: And is the meeting still on?

options.t: She just told me it's cancelled, so no.
options.why: immediate report with a present verb → no backshift needed.

options.t: She just told me it was cancelled the next day, so no.
options.why: for a fresh, still-relevant report, present is natural: it's cancelled.

activities.why: After a past reporting verb, backshift the tenses one step and shift pronouns and time/place words — but keep the present when the statement is still true or the reporting verb is present, and use tell with a person, say without.

learn.title: Questions become statements
learn.html:
When you report a question, it turns into a statement: normal word order (subject before verb), no auxiliary do/does/did, and no question mark.

“Where do you live?”→ She asked where I lived.
“Are you ready?”→ He asked if I was ready.

learn.title: Wh- vs yes/no questions
learn.html:
Wh-questions: keep the question word, then statement order: “What time does it start?” → She asked what time it started.

Yes/no questions: use if or whether: “Did you call?” → He asked if/whether I had called.

Backshift applies as usual (do live → lived; are → was).

learn.title: Orders & requests → to-infinitive
learn.html:
Reports of commands and requests use verb + object + to-infinitive:

“Wait here.” (order)→ She told me to wait there.
“Please help me.” (request)→ He asked me to help him.
“Don't touch it.” (negative)→ She told me not to touch it.

Common verbs: tell, ask, order, advise, warn, remind, invite, encourage, beg.

learn.title: Suggestions & offers
learn.html:
suggest takes -ing or that + should: “Let's eat out.” → He suggested eating out / that we (should) eat out.

offer / promise / agree / refuse / threaten take a to-infinitive without an object: “I'll pay.” → She offered to pay. “No, I won't.” → He refused to help.

learn.title: Reported questions inside sentences
learn.html: The same statement word order appears in embedded/indirect questions generally: Can you tell me where the station is? (not where is the station). I wonder what he wants.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She asked where did I live. → ✓ She asked where I lived.

✗ He asked me that I was ready. → ✓ He asked me if I was ready.

✗ She told me wait. → ✓ She told me to wait.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Reported questions use statement order, no do/does/did, no question mark.
  • Wh- keep the word; yes/no use if/whether.
  • Orders/requests → (tell/ask) + object + (not) to-infinitive.
  • suggest + -ing / that + should; offer/promise/refuse + to-infinitive.

ru.title: Вопросы превращаются в утверждения
ru.html: Когда сообщаешь вопрос, он становится утверждением: обычный порядок (подлежащее перед глаголом), без do/does/did, без вопросительного знака. “Where do you live?” → She asked where I lived. “Are you ready?” → He asked if I was ready.

kk.title: Сұрақтар хабарлы сөйлемге айналады
kk.html: Сұрақты хабарлағанда ол хабарлы сөйлемге айналады: қалыпты сөз реті (бастауыш етістіктен бұрын), do/does/did жоқ, сұрақ белгісі жоқ. “Where do you live?” → She asked where I lived. “Are you ready?” → He asked if I was ready.

ru.title: Wh- vs yes/no вопросы
ru.html: Wh-вопросы: сохраняем вопросительное слово, затем порядок утверждения: “What time does it start?” → She asked what time it started. Yes/no вопросы: if или whether: “Did you call?” → He asked if/whether I had called. Backshift как обычно.

kk.title: Wh- vs yes/no сұрақтар
kk.html: Wh-сұрақтар: сұрау сөзін сақтап, хабарлы рет: “What time does it start?” → She asked what time it started. Yes/no сұрақтар: if немесе whether: “Did you call?” → He asked if/whether I had called. Backshift әдеттегідей.

ru.title: Приказы и просьбы → to-infinitive
ru.html: Сообщение команд/просьб: verb + object + to-infinitive: “Wait here.” → She told me to wait there. “Please help me.” → He asked me to help him. “Don't touch it.” → She told me not to touch it. Глаголы: tell, ask, order, advise, warn, remind, invite, encourage, beg.

kk.title: Бұйрық пен өтініш → to-infinitive
kk.html: Бұйрық/өтініш хабары verb + object + to-infinitive: “Wait here.” → She told me to wait there. “Please help me.” → He asked me to help him. “Don't touch it.” → She told me not to touch it. Етістіктер: tell, ask, order, advise, warn, remind, invite, encourage, beg.

ru.title: Предложения и обещания
ru.html: suggest — -ing или that + should: “Let's eat out.” → He suggested eating out / that we (should) eat out. offer/promise/agree/refuse/threaten — to-infinitive без дополнения: “I'll pay.” → She offered to pay. “No, I won't.” → He refused to help.

kk.title: Ұсыныс пен ұсыну
kk.html: suggest — -ing немесе that + should: “Let's eat out.” → He suggested eating out / that we (should) eat out. offer/promise/agree/refuse/threaten — объектсіз to-infinitive: “I'll pay.” → She offered to pay. “No, I won't.” → He refused to help.

ru.title: Косвенные вопросы внутри предложений
ru.html: Тот же порядок утверждения — и во встроенных/косвенных вопросах: Can you tell me where the station is? (не where is the station). I wonder what he wants.

kk.title: Сөйлем ішіндегі жанама сұрақтар
kk.html: Дәл сол хабарлы рет ендірілген/жанама сұрақтарда да: Can you tell me where the station is? (where is the station емес). I wonder what he wants.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ She asked where did I live → ✓ She asked where I lived. ✗ He asked me that I was ready → ✓ He asked me if I was ready. ✗ She told me wait → ✓ She told me to wait.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ She asked where did I live → ✓ She asked where I lived. ✗ He asked me that I was ready → ✓ He asked me if I was ready. ✗ She told me wait → ✓ She told me to wait.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Косвенный вопрос: порядок утверждения, без do/does/did, без знака вопроса.
  • Wh- сохраняем слово; yes/no — if/whether.
  • Приказ/просьба → (tell/ask) + object + (not) to-infinitive.
  • suggest + -ing / that + should; offer/promise/refuse + to-infinitive.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Жанама сұрақ: хабарлы рет, do/does/did жоқ, сұрақ белгісі жоқ.
  • Wh- сөзін сақтаңыз; yes/no — if/whether.
  • Бұйрық/өтініш → (tell/ask) + object + (not) to-infinitive.
  • suggest + -ing / that + should; offer/promise/refuse + to-infinitive.

activities.prompt: Match each direct speech to its reporting pattern.
activities.why: Wh- questions keep the word; yes/no use if/whether; orders/requests use a to-infinitive.

items.s: She asked where I worked.
He asked if I could drive.
She asked where did I work.
He told me wait outside.

activities.why: reported questions use statement order (where I worked); orders need ‘to’ (told me to wait).

activities.before: “What time does the train leave?” → He asked what time the train
activities.why: statement order, no ‘does’, backshift → left.
yes/no question → if/whether.

activities.before: “Don't be late.” → The coach told us
activities.why: negative order → not to be.
yes/no reported question with backshift (had seen).

activities.q: “I'll definitely pay you back,” he said. →
options: He promised to pay me back.
He asked me to pay back.
why: ‘suggest’ doesn't fit a personal commitment.
Correct — a commitment → promised to pay me back.
that reverses who pays.

activities.q: Which is correct?
options: Could you tell me where is the exit?
Could you tell me where the exit is?
Could you tell me where does the exit is?
why: embedded questions use statement order.
Correct — where the exit is (subject before verb).
no ‘does’ in an embedded question.

activities.prompt: Match each speech act to its structure.
activities.why: suggest → -ing/that + should; offer/refuse → to-infinitive; requests → object + to-infinitive.
reported questions use statement order → “She asked me what I wanted.”

activities.prompt: “How long have you lived here?”
activities.answer: she asked how long i had lived there
alts: she asked me how long i had lived there
he asked how long i had lived there
activities.why: wh- word + statement order; present perfect → past perfect; here → there.

activities.prompt: “Don't touch the wet paint!”
activities.answer: he told me not to touch the wet paint
alts: she told me not to touch the wet paint
he warned me not to touch the wet paint
activities.why: negative order → (told/warned) me not to touch.

activities.scene: Report questions and requests correctly.

steps.npc: What did they ask you at the interview?

options.t: They asked why I'd left my last job and whether I could start in June.
options.why: wh- (why I'd left) + yes/no (whether I could).

options.t: They asked why did I leave and if can I start in June.
options.why: statement order: why I'd left; whether I could start.

steps.npc: Any instructions afterwards?

options.t: They told me to send references and not to contact them before Monday.
options.why: orders → (not) to-infinitive.

options.t: They told me send references and that I don't contact them.
options.why: orders use to-infinitive: to send…, not to contact…

activities.why: Report questions with statement order and if/whether (no do/does/did or ‘?’), orders and requests with (not) to-infinitive, and choose the right reporting verb for suggestions, offers and refusals.

learn.title: Beyond say & tell
learn.html:
Fluent reporting rarely just repeats ‘said’. A well-chosen reporting verb compresses the speech act and its function into one word — but each verb has its own grammatical pattern, which you must learn.

“Sorry, it was me.” → He admitted breaking it. · “You should rest.” → She advised me to rest.

learn.title: The main patterns
learn.html:
verb + (that) clauseadmit, deny, claim, explain, complain, insist, suggest, mention
verb + -ingadmit, deny, suggest, recommend, mention, regret
verb + to-infinitiveoffer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, claim, decide
verb + object + to-infinitiveadvise, ask, tell, warn, remind, encourage, invite, persuade
verb + preposition + -ingapologise for, insist on, accuse sb of, congratulate sb on

learn.title: Choosing the right verb
learn.html:
• promise/refuse/offer → to: He offered to drive.
  • admit/deny/suggest → -ing or that: She denied taking it / denied that she'd taken it.
  • advise/warn/remind → object + to: He warned us not to swim.
  • apologise/insist/accuse → preposition + -ing: She apologised for being late. · They accused him of lying.

learn.title: Time & place shifts (recap)
learn.html:
With a past reporting verb, shift references to now/here: now→then, today→that day, tomorrow→the next/following day, yesterday→the day before, last week→the week before, ago→before, here→there, this→that.

Only shift when the report is later and the reference has moved — if you report on the same day, ‘today’ may stay.

learn.title: Modal changes in reporting
learn.html: can→could, will→would, may→might, must (obligation)→had to, shall→should/would. Modals with no further past form stay: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to. must as deduction usually stays: “She must be tired.” → He said she must be tired.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He suggested me to leave. → ✓ He suggested (that) I leave / suggested leaving.

✗ She apologised to be late. → ✓ She apologised for being late.

✗ He admitted to break it. → ✓ He admitted breaking it / admitted that he had broken it.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Reporting verbs pack the function in — but each takes a fixed pattern (to / -ing / object + to / preposition + -ing).
  • suggest ≠ advise: suggest (that) I leave / suggest leaving vs advise me to leave.
  • apologise for, insist on, accuse of, congratulate on + -ing.
  • Backshift modals (will→would, must→had to) and shift time/place words.

ru.title: Дальше say & tell
ru.html: Беглый пересказ редко просто повторяет ‘said’. Точно выбранный глагол сообщения сжимает речевой акт и его функцию в одно слово — но у каждого своя грамматическая модель. “Sorry, it was me.” → He admitted breaking it. “You should rest.” → She advised me to rest.

kk.title: say & tell-ден асып
kk.html: Еркін хабарлау сирек тек ‘said’-ты қайталайды. Дұрыс таңдалған хабарлау етістігі сөйлеу актісі мен қызметін бір сөзге сыйдырады — бірақ әр етістіктің өз грамматикалық үлгісі бар. “Sorry, it was me.” → He admitted breaking it. “You should rest.” → She advised me to rest.

ru.title: Основные модели
ru.html: verb + (that) clause: admit, deny, claim, explain, complain, insist, suggest. verb + -ing: admit, deny, suggest, recommend, regret. verb + to-infinitive: offer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, decide. verb + object + to: advise, ask, tell, warn, remind, persuade. verb + preposition + -ing: apologise for, insist on, accuse sb of, congratulate sb on.

kk.title: Негізгі үлгілер
kk.html: verb + (that) clause: admit, deny, claim, explain, complain, insist, suggest. verb + -ing: admit, deny, suggest, recommend, regret. verb + to-infinitive: offer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, decide. verb + object + to: advise, ask, tell, warn, remind, persuade. verb + preposition + -ing: apologise for, insist on, accuse sb of, congratulate sb on.

ru.title: Выбор правильного глагола
ru.html: promise/refuse/offer → to: He offered to drive. admit/deny/suggest → -ing/that: She denied taking it / denied that she'd taken it. advise/warn/remind → object + to: He warned us not to swim. apologise/insist/accuse → preposition + -ing: She apologised for being late. They accused him of lying.

kk.title: Дұрыс етістікті таңдау
kk.html: promise/refuse/offer → to: He offered to drive. admit/deny/suggest → -ing/that: She denied taking it / denied that she'd taken it. advise/warn/remind → object + to: He warned us not to swim. apologise/insist/accuse → preposition + -ing: She apologised for being late. They accused him of lying.

ru.title: Сдвиги времени/места
ru.html: С прошедшим глаголом сообщения: now→then, today→that day, tomorrow→the next/following day, yesterday→the day before, last week→the week before, ago→before, here→there, this→that. Сдвигайте, только если сообщение позже и точка отсчёта изменилась.

kk.title: Уақыт/орын жылжулары
kk.html: Өткен хабарлау етістігімен: now→then, today→that day, tomorrow→the next/following day, yesterday→the day before, last week→the week before, ago→before, here→there, this→that. Тек хабар кейінірек болып, сілтеме ауысқанда жылжытыңыз.

ru.title: Изменения модальных при пересказе
ru.html: can→could, will→would, may→might, must (обязанность)→had to, shall→should/would. Без дальнейшей формы остаются: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to. Догадочный must обычно остаётся: “She must be tired.” → He said she must be tired.

kk.title: Хабарлаудағы модаль өзгерістері
kk.html: can→could, will→would, may→might, must (міндет)→had to, shall→should/would. Одан әрі формасы жоқтар қалады: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to. Болжамдық must әдетте қалады: “She must be tired.” → He said she must be tired.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ He suggested me to leave → ✓ He suggested (that) I leave / suggested leaving. ✗ She apologised to be late → ✓ apologised for being late. ✗ He admitted to break it → ✓ admitted breaking it / admitted that he had broken it.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ He suggested me to leave → ✓ He suggested (that) I leave / suggested leaving. ✗ She apologised to be late → ✓ apologised for being late. ✗ He admitted to break it → ✓ admitted breaking it / admitted that he had broken it.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Глаголы сообщения упаковывают функцию — но у каждого фиксированная модель.
  • suggest ≠ advise: suggest (that) I leave / leaving vs advise me to leave.
  • apologise for, insist on, accuse of, congratulate on + -ing.
  • Backshift модальных (will→would, must→had to) и сдвиг слов времени/места.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Хабарлау етістіктері қызметті сыйдырады — бірақ әрқайсысының тұрақты үлгісі бар.
  • suggest ≠ advise: suggest (that) I leave / leaving vs advise me to leave.
  • apologise for, insist on, accuse of, congratulate on + -ing.
  • Модальдарды backshift (will→would, must→had to) және уақыт/орын сөздерін жылжытыңыз.

activities.typeLabel: Which pattern does the verb take?
activities.prompt: Sort each reporting verb by its pattern.
activities.why: promise/refuse → to; deny → -ing/that; advise → object + to; apologise/accuse → preposition + -ing.

items.s: She offered to help.
He admitted taking the money.
They apologised for being late.
He suggested me to leave.

activities.why: suggest doesn't take ‘object + to’ → suggested (that) I leave / suggested leaving.

activities.before: “It was me who broke it.” → He admitted
activities.why: admit + -ing → breaking (or the perfect -ing ‘having broken’).
accuse sb + of + -ing → of cheating.

activities.before: “You really should see a doctor.” → She advised me
activities.why: advise + object + to-infinitive → to see.
apologise for + (not) -ing.

activities.q: “No, I absolutely will not sign this.” →
why: ‘offer’ reverses the meaning.
Correct — a firm ‘no’ → refused to sign.
‘suggest’ doesn't fit a refusal.

activities.q: Which reports “Why don't you take the train?” correctly?
options: She suggested me to take the train.
why: ‘advise’ needs an object: advised me to take.
‘suggest’ can't take object + to.
Correct — suggest + -ing → suggested taking the train.

activities.prompt: Match each utterance to the verb that best captures it.

items.t: “No, I really must pay — I insist.”

activities.why: admit (confess), warn (of danger), congratulate (on success), insist (on something).
apologise + for + -ing → “apologised for interrupting the meeting.”

activities.prompt: “If you do that again, I'll call the police,” he said.
activities.answer: he threatened to call the police
alts: he threatened to call the police.
activities.why: threaten + to-infinitive.

activities.prompt: “Well done on winning the award!” she said to him.
activities.answer: she congratulated him on winning the award
alts: she congratulated him on winning the award.
activities.why: congratulate sb + on + -ing.

activities.scene: Choose the reporting verb and pattern that fit.

steps.npc: So what did each side say?

options.t: The supplier denied missing the deadline and insisted on being paid in full.
options.why: deny + -ing; insist on + -ing.

options.t: The supplier denied to miss the deadline and insisted to be paid.
options.why: deny + -ing; insist on + -ing (not to-infinitive).

steps.npc: And your manager?

options.t: She advised us to get legal advice and warned us not to sign anything yet.
options.why: advise/warn + object + (not) to-infinitive.

options.t: She advised getting legal advice and warned us to don't sign anything.
options.why: advise us to get / warned us not to sign — object + to-infinitive.

activities.why: Pick a precise reporting verb, but respect its pattern — to-infinitive (promise/refuse), -ing/that (admit/deny/suggest), object + to (advise/warn), or preposition + -ing (apologise for/accuse of) — and backshift modals and time words as needed.

learn.title: Two jobs, two kinds of clause
learn.html:
A relative clause adds information about a noun. The crucial B2 distinction is whether that information is essential (identifies which one — defining) or just extra (non-defining, set off by commas).

The woman who lives next door is a doctor.defining — tells us which woman
My mother, who lives next door, is a doctor.non-defining — extra info; we already know who

learn.title: The relative words
learn.html:
peoplewho / that (defining); who / whom (non-def.)
thingswhich / that (defining); which (non-def.)
possessionwhose (both)
place / time / reasonwhere / when / why

that is common in defining clauses but never in non-defining ones.

learn.title: Omitting the pronoun (defining only)
learn.html:
In a defining clause you can omit the relative pronoun when it's the object: the film (that) I saw · the man (who) I met.

You cannot omit it when it's the subject: the man who called (not the man called). And you never omit or use ‘that’ in a non-defining clause.

learn.title: Prepositions in relative clauses
learn.html:
In everyday English the preposition goes at the end: the person (who) I spoke to. In formal English it goes before whom/which: the person to whom I spoke · the tool with which it was made.

‘that’ can't follow a preposition: in that → in which / …I live in.

learn.title: which for a whole clause; quantifier + of whom/which
learn.html:
In non-defining clauses, which can refer to a whole idea: She passed, which delighted everyone. (never ‘what’)

Formal quantifier patterns: He has three sisters, two of whom are teachers. · She made promises, none of which she kept.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ My car, that is new, keeps breaking down. → ✓ My car, which is new, …

✗ the man who he called me → ✓ the man who called me (no double subject)

✗ She won, what surprised us. → ✓ She won, which surprised us.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Defining = essential (no commas); non-defining = extra (commas).
  • ‘that’ only in defining clauses; never in non-defining.
  • Omit the object pronoun in defining clauses; never a subject pronoun.
  • which can refer to a whole clause; use preposition + whom/which formally.

ru.title: Две роли, два вида придаточного
ru.html: Относительное придаточное добавляет информацию о существительном. Ключевое различие B2 — эта информация существенна (какой именно — определяющее) или просто дополнительна (неопределяющее, с запятыми). The woman who lives next door is a doctor. My mother, who lives next door, is a doctor.

kk.title: Екі қызмет, екі түрлі сөйлем
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлем зат есім туралы ақпарат қосады. B2-нің басты айырмасы — ол ақпарат маңызды ма (қайсы екенін көрсетеді — айқындаушы) әлде тек қосымша ма (айқындамаушы, үтірмен). The woman who lives next door is a doctor (айқындаушы). My mother, who lives next door, is a doctor (қосымша).

ru.title: Относительные слова
ru.html: Люди: who/that (определяющее); who/whom (неопред.). Вещи: which/that; which. Принадлежность: whose. Место/время/причина: where/when/why. that част в определяющих, но никогда в неопределяющих.

kk.title: Қатыстық сөздер
kk.html: Адам: who/that (айқындаушы); who/whom (айқындамаушы). Зат: which/that; which. Иелік: whose. Орын/уақыт/себеп: where/when/why. that айқындаушыда жиі, бірақ айқындамаушыда ешқашан.

ru.title: Опущение местоимения (только определяющее)
ru.html: В определяющем придаточном местоимение-дополнение можно опустить: the film (that) I saw. Подлежащее опускать нельзя: the man who called. В неопределяющем не опускаем и не используем ‘that’.

kk.title: Есімдікті түсіру (тек айқындаушы)
kk.html: Айқындаушы сөйлемде есімдік толықтауыш болса, оны түсіруге болады: the film (that) I saw. Бастауыш болса, түсіруге болмайды: the man who called. Айқындамаушыда ешқашан түсірмейміз әрі ‘that’ қолданбаймыз.

ru.title: Предлоги в относительных придаточных
ru.html: В разговорном предлог в конце: the person (who) I spoke to. В формальном — перед whom/which: the person to whom I spoke; the tool with which it was made. ‘that’ после предлога нельзя: in that → in which / …I live in.

kk.title: Қатыстық сөйлемдегі предлогтар
kk.html: Күнделікті тілде предлог соңында: the person (who) I spoke to. Ресми тілде whom/which алдында: the person to whom I spoke; the tool with which it was made. ‘that’ предлогтан кейін болмайды: in that → in which / …I live in.

ru.title: which — на всё предложение; quantifier + of whom/which
ru.html: В неопределяющем which может указывать на всю мысль: She passed, which delighted everyone (никогда ‘what’). Формально: He has three sisters, two of whom are teachers. She made promises, none of which she kept.

kk.title: which — бүкіл сөйлемге; quantifier + of whom/which
kk.html: Айқындамаушыда which бүкіл ойға сілтей алады: She passed, which delighted everyone (‘what’ ешқашан). Ресми: He has three sisters, two of whom are teachers. She made promises, none of which she kept.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ My car, that is new, … → ✓ My car, which is new, … ✗ the man who he called me → ✓ the man who called me. ✗ She won, what surprised us → ✓ She won, which surprised us.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ My car, that is new, … → ✓ My car, which is new, … ✗ the man who he called me → ✓ the man who called me. ✗ She won, what surprised us → ✓ She won, which surprised us.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Определяющее = существенно (без запятых); неопределяющее = дополнительно (запятые).
  • ‘that’ только в определяющих; в неопределяющих никогда.
  • Опускайте местоимение-дополнение в определяющих; подлежащее — никогда.
  • which может указывать на всё предложение; формально preposition + whom/which.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Айқындаушы = маңызды (үтірсіз); айқындамаушы = қосымша (үтірмен).
  • ‘that’ тек айқындаушыда; айқындамаушыда ешқашан.
  • Айқындаушыда толықтауыш есімдігін түсіріңіз; бастауышты ешқашан.
  • which бүкіл сөйлемге сілтей алады; ресми: preposition + whom/which.

activities.prompt: Sort each clause by whether it identifies (essential) or just adds (extra).

items.t: The book that won the prize is here.
This book, which won the prize, is here.
People who smoke risk their health.
My uncle, who smokes, is unwell.
The house we bought needs work.
Our house, which we bought in 2019, needs work.

activities.why: Defining clauses pick out which one (no commas); non-defining ones add extra information between commas.

items.s: Rome, which I love, is chaotic.
the man whose car was stolen
My phone, that is new, broke.
the woman who she called me

activities.why: non-defining never uses ‘that’ (which); don't repeat the subject (who called).
possession → whose novels.

activities.after: upset me a lot. (refers to the whole fact)
activities.why: a whole idea in a non-defining clause → which (not ‘what’).
formal → preposition before whom → with whom.
non-defining clause between commas, using ‘which’.

activities.q: “My brother who lives in Tokyo is a chef.” (no commas) implies:
options: I have one brother, and he's a chef.
I have more than one brother; the Tokyo one is a chef.
why: that would need commas (non-defining).
Correct — a defining clause distinguishes this brother from others → I have several brothers.
the clause simply identifies which brother.

activities.q: Which is fully correct AND can drop the relative pronoun?
options: the man, who I met, was kind
why: ‘who’ is the subject here — can't be omitted.
Correct — object relative in a defining clause → ‘the book I borrowed’.
non-defining clauses never drop the pronoun or use commas like a defining one.

activities.prompt: Can the relative pronoun be omitted?
activities.why: Omit when the pronoun is the object; keep it when it's the subject of the clause.
non-defining clause → ‘that’ is not allowed → “Paris, which I adore, is expensive.”

activities.prompt: My neighbour is very kind. She often helps me.
activities.answer: my neighbour, who often helps me, is very kind
alts: my neighbour, who often helps me, is very kind.
activities.why: extra info about a known person → , who…,

activities.typeLabel: Combine, using ‘which’ for the whole idea
activities.prompt: The train was delayed. This made us miss the connection.
activities.answer: the train was delayed, which made us miss the connection
alts: the train was delayed, which made us miss the connection.
activities.why: which refers to the whole event.

activities.scene: Choose the right relative clause and punctuation.

steps.npc: Which colleague are you meaning?

options.t: The one who sits by the window — the tall guy.
options.why: defining clause identifies which colleague.

options.t: The one, who sits by the window, the tall guy.
options.why: identifying which one → defining clause, no commas: the one who sits…

steps.npc: And the venue?

options.t: The Grand Hotel, which we used last year, has a bigger room.
options.why: extra info about a named, known place → non-defining, commas, ‘which’.

options.t: The Grand Hotel that we used last year, has a bigger room.
options.why: extra info about a specific known place → non-defining: , which we used last year,

activities.why: Use defining clauses (no commas, ‘that’ allowed, object pronoun droppable) to identify, and non-defining clauses (commas, who/which, never ‘that’, never omitted) to add extra information — with ‘which’ able to refer to a whole clause.

learn.title: Trimming relative clauses
learn.html:
You can often shorten a relative clause to a participle clause — more economical and typical of good written style. An -ing clause replaces an active relative clause; an -ed clause replaces a passive one.

the man who is talking to Sam→ the man talking to Sam
a letter which was written in 1900→ a letter written in 1900

learn.title: When you can reduce
learn.html:
Reduce when the relative pronoun is the subject of the clause:

  • -ing (active): people who are waiting → people waiting
  • -ing (a simple active meaning too): anyone who wants a ticket → anyone wanting a ticket
  • -ed / p.p. (passive): cars that were made in Japan → cars made in Japan

learn.title: Perfect & continuous reductions
learn.html:
Reduce a perfect passive relative clause with having been + p.p.: the town, which had been destroyed, → the town, having been destroyed, was rebuilt.

You can't reduce when the pronoun is the object: the book (that) I read stays as it is (no participle).

learn.title: With non-defining clauses & indefinite meaning
learn.html:
Participle clauses also work non-defining (with commas): The manager, wanting to save time, cancelled the meeting.

They're common with quantity/indefinite subjects: Those interested should apply. · Anyone found cheating will be disqualified.

learn.title: Style: why writers use them
learn.html: Participle clauses make writing tighter and more sophisticated, packing information without extra ‘who is/which was’. They're everywhere in news, reports and academic prose: Passengers travelling to Gate 12… · The measures introduced last year…

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ the man standed there → ✓ the man standing there (active → -ing)

✗ the goods delivering yesterday (passive meaning) → ✓ the goods delivered yesterday

✗ the book reading by me (object) → ✓ the book (that) I'm reading (can't reduce an object relative)

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• -ing clause replaces an active subject relative (people waiting).
  • -ed/p.p. clause replaces a passive subject relative (a letter written…).
  • Only reduce subject relatives; object relatives stay full.
  • Great for tight, formal writing (Passengers travelling…, measures introduced…).

ru.title: Сокращаем относительные придаточные
ru.html: Относительное придаточное часто можно сократить до причастного оборота — экономнее, признак хорошего письменного стиля. -ing заменяет активное, -ed — пассивное придаточное. the man who is talking to Sam → the man talking to Sam. a letter which was written in 1900 → a letter written in 1900.

kk.title: Қатыстық сөйлемдерді қысқарту
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлемді жиі есімше сөйлеміне қысқартуға болады — үнемдірек, жақсы жазба стиль. -ing сөйлемі активті, -ed сөйлемі пассивті қатыстық сөйлемді алмастырады. the man who is talking to Sam → the man talking to Sam. a letter which was written in 1900 → a letter written in 1900.

ru.title: Когда можно сократить
ru.html: Сокращайте, когда местоимение — подлежащее: -ing (актив): people who are waiting → people waiting. -ing (простой актив): anyone who wants → anyone wanting. -ed/p.p. (пассив): cars that were made in Japan → cars made in Japan.

kk.title: Қашан қысқартуға болады
kk.html: Есімдік сөйлемнің бастауышы болғанда қысқартыңыз: -ing (актив): people who are waiting → people waiting. -ing (жай актив): anyone who wants → anyone wanting. -ed/p.p. (пассив): cars that were made in Japan → cars made in Japan.

ru.title: Перфектные и продолженные сокращения
ru.html: Перфектное пассивное придаточное — через having been + p.p.: the town, which had been destroyed → the town, having been destroyed, was rebuilt. Если местоимение — дополнение, сокращать нельзя: the book (that) I read остаётся.

kk.title: Perfect және continuous қысқартулар
kk.html: Perfect passive қатыстық сөйлемді having been + p.p.-мен: the town, which had been destroyed → the town, having been destroyed, was rebuilt. Есімдік толықтауыш болса, қысқартуға болмайды: the book (that) I read — сол күйінде қалады.

ru.title: С неопределяющими и неопределённым смыслом
ru.html: Причастные обороты бывают и неопределяющими (с запятыми): The manager, wanting to save time, cancelled the meeting. Часты с неопределёнными субъектами: Those interested should apply. Anyone found cheating will be disqualified.

kk.title: Айқындамаушы және белгісіз мағына
kk.html: Есімше сөйлемдері айқындамаушы да болады (үтірмен): The manager, wanting to save time, cancelled the meeting. Белгісіз субъектілермен жиі: Those interested should apply. Anyone found cheating will be disqualified.

ru.title: Стиль: зачем их используют
ru.html: Причастные обороты делают текст компактнее и изящнее, вмещая информацию без ‘who is/which was’. Часты в новостях, отчётах, академическом письме: Passengers travelling to Gate 12… The measures introduced last year…

kk.title: Стиль: жазушылар неге қолданады
kk.html: Есімше сөйлемдері жазуды ықшам әрі жетілдірілген етеді, ‘who is/which was’-сыз ақпаратты сыйдырады. Жаңалық, есеп, академиялық мәтінде жиі: Passengers travelling to Gate 12… The measures introduced last year…

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ the man standed there → ✓ the man standing there (актив → -ing). ✗ the goods delivering yesterday (пассив) → ✓ the goods delivered yesterday. ✗ the book reading by me (дополнение) → ✓ the book (that) I'm reading.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ the man standed there → ✓ the man standing there (актив → -ing). ✗ the goods delivering yesterday (пассив) → ✓ the goods delivered yesterday. ✗ the book reading by me (толықтауыш) → ✓ the book (that) I'm reading.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• -ing заменяет активное подлежащное придаточное (people waiting).
  • -ed/p.p. заменяет пассивное подлежащное (a letter written…).
  • Сокращаем только подлежащные; дополнительные остаются полными.
  • Отлично для компактного, формального письма.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• -ing сөйлемі активті бастауыш қатыстықты алмастырады (people waiting).
  • -ed/p.p. сөйлемі пассивті бастауыш қатыстықты (a letter written…).
  • Тек бастауыш қатыстықты қысқартыңыз; толықтауыш толық қалады.
  • Ықшам, ресми жазуға өте қолайлы.

activities.prompt: Sort each reduced clause by its meaning.

items.t: the money stolen from the safe

activities.why: -ing = the noun does the action (active); -ed/p.p. = the noun receives it (passive).

items.s: the students studying abroad
the car damaged in the crash
the man sat there (= who is sitting)
the report writing by the intern

activities.why: active ‘sitting’ for who is sitting; passive ‘written’ for a report written by someone.
active subject relative → playing (= who are playing).
passive subject relative → made (= which are made).

activities.after: been warned, the residents evacuated calmly. (Have)
activities.why: perfect passive reduction → Having been warned (= who had been warned).
reduced active relative (= anyone who wishes).

activities.q: “The information ___ in the leak was confidential.” (= that was contained)
why: ‘containing’ would be active (the info contains).
Correct — passive → contained (= which was contained).

activities.q: Which relative clause CANNOT be reduced to a participle?
options: the guests who are arriving now
the parcel that was sent today
the film that I watched last night
why: a subject active relative → the guests arriving now.
a subject passive relative → the parcel sent today.
Correct — an OBJECT relative (I watched it) can't be reduced.

activities.prompt: Sort by whether the relative clause can be reduced.

items.t: the man who owns the shop

activities.why: Reduce only when the relative pronoun is the subject; object relatives stay full.
passive meaning (= who was injured) → “the boy injured in the match.”

activities.prompt: The woman who is standing by the door is my boss.
activities.answer: the woman standing by the door is my boss
alts: the woman standing by the door is my boss.
activities.why: active subject relative → standing.

activities.prompt: The measures which were introduced last year have helped.
activities.answer: the measures introduced last year have helped
alts: the measures introduced last year have helped.
activities.why: passive subject relative → introduced.

activities.scene: Choose the correct participle reduction.

steps.npc: Can you make this sentence more concise?

options.t: “Employees working from home report higher satisfaction.”
options.why: reduced active relative (= who work).

options.t: “Employees worked from home report higher satisfaction.”
options.why: active meaning → working (who work), not ‘worked’.

steps.npc: And this one about the survey?

options.t: “The data collected last quarter show a clear trend.”
options.why: reduced passive relative (= which was collected).

options.t: “The data collecting last quarter show a clear trend.”
options.why: passive meaning → collected (which was collected).

activities.why: Reduce subject relative clauses to participle clauses — -ing for active, -ed/p.p. for passive — to write more concisely; object relatives can't be reduced.

learn.title: Linking events economically
learn.html:
Participle clauses do more than describe nouns — they can connect whole events, expressing time, reason, result or condition without a conjunction. The subject of the participle is usually the same as the main clause.

Feeling tired, she went to bed. · Having finished, we left. · The bomb exploded, killing two people.

learn.title: Time & sequence
learn.html:
• Simultaneous: Walking home, I met an old friend. (= while I was walking)
  • Just before/after: Opening the door, she stepped inside.
  • Clearly earlier: Having locked up, he left. (= after he had locked up)
  • With after/before/since/on: After finishing, … · On arriving, we checked in.

learn.title: Reason & result
learn.html:
Being ill, she stayed home.reason (= because she was ill)
Not knowing the way, we asked.negative reason (= as we didn't know)
Prices rose, causing unrest.result (= and this caused…)

For a passive reason: Built in a hurry, the bridge soon failed. · Left alone, the baby cried.

learn.title: The dangling participle trap
learn.html:
The participle's subject must match the main clause's subject, or the sentence goes wrong (a ‘dangling participle’):

✗ Walking home, the rain started. (the rain wasn't walking!) → ✓ Walking home, I got caught in the rain.

Always check: who is doing the participle action?

learn.title: Register & with-clauses
learn.html: Participle clauses are formal/literary and common in written narrative and reports. A with-clause adds an accompanying circumstance: With the deadline approaching, we worked late. · She sat there, with her arms folded.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Having ate lunch, we left. → ✓ Having eaten lunch, we left.

✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep. (the film wasn't tired) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film.

✗ After to arrive, we ate. → ✓ After arriving, we ate.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Participle clauses link events: time, reason, result or condition — same subject.
  • -ing = active/simultaneous; having + p.p. = earlier; -ed/being = passive/reason.
  • Avoid the dangling participle: the doer must match the main subject.
  • Formal & concise; with + noun + participle adds a circumstance.

ru.title: Экономно связываем события
ru.html: Причастные обороты не только описывают существительные — они связывают целые события, выражая время, причину, результат, условие без союза. Субъект причастия обычно совпадает с главным. Feeling tired, she went to bed. Having finished, we left. The bomb exploded, killing two people.

kk.title: Оқиғаларды үнемді байланыстыру
kk.html: Есімше сөйлемдері зат есімді сипаттаудан асып, бүкіл оқиғаларды байланыстырады — жалғаулықсыз уақыт, себеп, нәтиже, шарт. Есімшенің субъектісі әдетте басты сөйлеммен бірдей. Feeling tired, she went to bed. Having finished, we left. The bomb exploded, killing two people.

ru.title: Время и последовательность
ru.html: Одновременно: Walking home, I met an old friend. Прямо перед/после: Opening the door, she stepped inside. Явно раньше: Having locked up, he left. С after/before/since/on: After finishing, … On arriving, we checked in.

kk.title: Уақыт пен реттілік
kk.html: Қатар: Walking home, I met an old friend. Тап алдында/соң: Opening the door, she stepped inside. Анық ертерек: Having locked up, he left. after/before/since/on-мен: After finishing, … On arriving, we checked in.

ru.title: Причина и результат
ru.html: Being ill, she stayed home (причина). Not knowing the way, we asked (отрицательная причина). Prices rose, causing unrest (результат). Пассивная причина: Built in a hurry, the bridge soon failed. Left alone, the baby cried.

kk.title: Себеп пен нәтиже
kk.html: Being ill, she stayed home (себеп). Not knowing the way, we asked (болымсыз себеп). Prices rose, causing unrest (нәтиже). Пассив себеп: Built in a hurry, the bridge soon failed. Left alone, the baby cried.

ru.title: Ловушка «висячего причастия»
ru.html: Субъект причастия должен совпадать с субъектом главного, иначе ошибка (dangling participle): ✗ Walking home, the rain started (дождь не шёл пешком!) → ✓ Walking home, I got caught in the rain. Всегда проверяйте: кто выполняет действие причастия?

kk.title: «Салбыраған есімше» тұзағы
kk.html: Есімшенің субъектісі басты сөйлемнің субъектісімен сәйкес келуі керек, әйтпесе қате (dangling participle): ✗ Walking home, the rain started (жаңбыр жүрген жоқ!) → ✓ Walking home, I got caught in the rain. Әрдайым тексеріңіз: есімше әрекетін кім істеп тұр?

ru.title: Регистр и with-обороты
ru.html: Причастные обороты формальны/литературны, часты в письменном рассказе и отчётах. With-оборот добавляет сопутствующее обстоятельство: With the deadline approaching, we worked late. She sat there, with her arms folded.

kk.title: Регистр және with-сөйлемдер
kk.html: Есімше сөйлемдері ресми/әдеби, жазба әңгіме мен есепте жиі. With-сөйлемі қосымша жағдай қосады: With the deadline approaching, we worked late. She sat there, with her arms folded.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Having ate lunch → ✓ Having eaten lunch. ✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep (фильм не устал) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film. ✗ After to arrive → ✓ After arriving.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Having ate lunch → ✓ Having eaten lunch. ✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep (фильм шаршаған жоқ) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film. ✗ After to arrive → ✓ After arriving.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Причастные обороты связывают события: время, причина, результат, условие — один субъект.
  • -ing = актив/одновременно; having + p.p. = раньше; -ed/being = пассив/причина.
  • Избегайте висячего причастия: деятель совпадает с главным субъектом.
  • Формально и компактно; with + noun + participle добавляет обстоятельство.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Есімше сөйлемдері оқиғаларды байланыстырады: уақыт, себеп, нәтиже, шарт — бір субъект.
  • -ing = актив/қатар; having + p.p. = ертерек; -ed/being = пассив/себеп.
  • Салбыраған есімшеден аулақ: орындаушы басты субъектпен сәйкес.
  • Ресми әрі ықшам; with + noun + participle жағдай қосады.

activities.typeLabel: What does the participle clause express?
activities.prompt: Sort each clause by its meaning.

items.t: The storm hit, flooding the town.

activities.why: Participle clauses can express time, reason or result depending on context.

items.s: Feeling ill, she went home.
Having finished, they left.
Walking home, the sky darkened on me.
Built in 1850, the church still stands.

activities.why: the sky can't ‘walk home’ — the subjects must match: Walking home, I…

activities.after: no ticket, he couldn't get in. (Have)
activities.why: reason (= because he had no ticket) → Having no ticket.

activities.after: the report, she emailed it to the board. (Write)
activities.why: a clearly earlier action → Having written the report.
result → …, forcing thousands to flee.
passive participle clause of reason/condition (= because it was left unattended).

activities.q: “___ many times before, she wasn't nervous on stage.”
why: a simple -ing clause doesn't show ‘before’.
Correct — an earlier, repeated action → Having performed.
‘to perform’ shows purpose, not a completed prior action.

activities.q: Which correctly completes “Driving to work,…”?
why: the traffic wasn't driving.
Correct — the subject who drives is ‘I’ → I got stuck in traffic.
‘my car’ shifts the subject away from the driver.

activities.prompt: Which form fits each meaning?
activities.why: active meaning → -ing/having; passive meaning → -ed/being/built.
having + past participle → “Having gone home, she made dinner.”

activities.prompt: She didn't have any cash. She paid by card.
activities.answer: not having any cash, she paid by card
alts: having no cash, she paid by card
not having any cash, she paid by card.
activities.why: negative reason → Not having any cash, …

activities.prompt: The factory closed. It left 200 people jobless.
activities.answer: the factory closed, leaving 200 people jobless
alts: the factory closed, leaving 200 people jobless.
activities.why: result → …, leaving 200 people jobless.

activities.scene: Use participle clauses correctly (mind the subject).

steps.npc: How did the presentation go?

options.t: Having rehearsed all week, I felt calm and it went smoothly.
options.why: earlier action (having rehearsed) + matching subject ‘I’.

options.t: Having rehearsed all week, the presentation felt calm to me.
options.why: the presentation didn't rehearse — keep the subject ‘I’: Having rehearsed…, I felt calm.

steps.npc: Any surprises?

options.t: Not expecting questions, I was thrown at first, but I recovered.
options.why: reason clause (Not expecting…) with subject ‘I’.

options.t: Not expecting questions, the audience threw me.
options.why: the audience did expect questions — the one not expecting is ‘I’: Not expecting questions, I was thrown.

activities.why: Use participle clauses to link events (time, reason, result, condition) concisely — -ing/having for active, -ed/being for passive — always making sure the participle's doer matches the main subject.

learn.title: Fronting a negative for emphasis
learn.html:
When a negative or restrictive adverbial is moved to the front of a sentence for emphasis, the subject and (auxiliary) verb invert — as in a question. This is a hallmark of formal, dramatic, or literary English.

I have never seen such a mess.→ Never have I seen such a mess.
She had hardly sat down when it rang.→ Hardly had she sat down when it rang.

learn.title: The trigger words
learn.html:
• Never, rarely, seldom, little: Never had he felt so alone. · Little did she know…
  • Hardly/Scarcely … when; No sooner … than: No sooner had we left than it rained.
  • Not only … (but also): Not only did they win, but they broke the record.
  • Not until / Only after / Only then / Only by: Only then did I understand.
  • Under no circumstances / At no time / On no account: Under no circumstances should you reply.

learn.title: How the inversion works
learn.html:
Use the question form: put the auxiliary before the subject. If there's no auxiliary, add do/does/did: Rarely does he complain. · Not until later did we realise.

With be, invert be directly: Seldom was he seen in public.

learn.title: Only + time/manner phrases
learn.html: After Only + (after/when/if/by/later/then), the inversion happens in the main clause, not the ‘only’ clause: Only when the guests had left did she relax. (invert in the second half). Only by working together can we succeed.

learn.title: Register & effect
learn.html: These structures are emphatic and formal — used in speeches, journalism, literature and formal writing to add weight or drama. In casual speech they can sound overly grand, so use them deliberately, not by default.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Never I have seen this. → ✓ Never have I seen this. (invert!)

✗ No sooner we arrived than it started. → ✓ No sooner had we arrived than it started.

✗ Hardly she had spoken when… → ✓ Hardly had she spoken when…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Front a negative/restrictive adverbial → invert subject & auxiliary (question form).
  • Triggers: never, rarely, seldom, hardly … when, no sooner … than, not only, not until, only then, under no circumstances.
  • Add do/does/did if there's no other auxiliary.
  • Formal & emphatic — use deliberately.

ru.title: Вынос отрицания вперёд для акцента
ru.html: Когда отрицательное или ограничительное наречие выносится в начало для акцента, подлежащее и вспомогательный глагол меняются местами — как в вопросе. Это признак формального, драматичного, литературного английского. I have never seen such a mess → Never have I seen such a mess.

kk.title: Екпін үшін болымсызды алға шығару
kk.html: Болымсыз немесе шектеуші үстеу екпін үшін сөйлемнің басына шыққанда, бастауыш пен көмекші етістік орын алмастырады — сұрақ сияқты. Бұл — ресми, драмалық, әдеби ағылшынның белгісі. I have never seen such a mess → Never have I seen such a mess.

ru.title: Слова-триггеры
ru.html: Never, rarely, seldom, little: Never had he felt so alone. Little did she know… Hardly/Scarcely … when; No sooner … than: No sooner had we left than it rained. Not only … (but also): Not only did they win, but… Not until / Only after / Only then / Only by. Under no circumstances / At no time / On no account.

kk.title: Триггер сөздер
kk.html: Never, rarely, seldom, little: Never had he felt so alone. Little did she know… Hardly/Scarcely … when; No sooner … than: No sooner had we left than it rained. Not only … (but also): Not only did they win, but… Not until / Only after / Only then / Only by. Under no circumstances / At no time / On no account.

ru.title: Как работает инверсия
ru.html: Используйте вопросительную форму: вспомогательный перед подлежащим. Если его нет, добавьте do/does/did: Rarely does he complain. Not until later did we realise. С be — прямая инверсия: Seldom was he seen in public.

kk.title: Инверсия қалай жасалады
kk.html: Сұрақ формасын қолданыңыз: көмекшіні бастауыштан бұрын қойыңыз. Көмекші жоқ болса, do/does/did қосыңыз: Rarely does he complain. Not until later did we realise. be-мен тікелей: Seldom was he seen in public.

ru.title: Only + фразы времени/образа
ru.html: После Only + (after/when/if/by/later/then) инверсия — в главном предложении, а не в ‘only’-части: Only when the guests had left did she relax. Only by working together can we succeed.

kk.title: Only + уақыт/амал тіркестері
kk.html: Only + (after/when/if/by/later/then)-нан кейін инверсия басты сөйлемде болады, ‘only’ бөлігінде емес: Only when the guests had left did she relax. Only by working together can we succeed.

ru.title: Регистр и эффект
ru.html: Эти структуры эмфатичны и формальны — в речах, журналистике, литературе, формальном письме для веса или драмы. В обычной речи звучат слишком торжественно, поэтому используйте намеренно.

kk.title: Регистр және әсер
kk.html: Бұл құрылымдар екпінді әрі ресми — сөйленіс, журналистика, әдебиет, ресми жазуда салмақ не драма қосу үшін. Кәдімгі сөйлеуде тым салтанатты естілуі мүмкін, сондықтан әдейі қолданыңыз.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ Never I have seen this → ✓ Never have I seen this. ✗ No sooner we arrived than… → ✓ No sooner had we arrived than… ✗ Hardly she had spoken when… → ✓ Hardly had she spoken when…

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ Never I have seen this → ✓ Never have I seen this. ✗ No sooner we arrived than… → ✓ No sooner had we arrived than… ✗ Hardly she had spoken when… → ✓ Hardly had she spoken when…

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Вынеси отрицательное/ограничительное наречие → инвертируй подлежащее и вспомогательный (вопросительная форма).
  • Триггеры: never, rarely, hardly … when, no sooner … than, not only, not until, only then, under no circumstances.
  • Если нет другого вспомогательного — добавь do/does/did.
  • Формально и эмфатично — используй намеренно.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Болымсыз/шектеуші үстеуді алға шығар → бастауыш пен көмекшіні орын алмастыр (сұрақ формасы).
  • Триггерлер: never, rarely, hardly … when, no sooner … than, not only, not until, only then, under no circumstances.
  • Басқа көмекші жоқ болса do/does/did қос.
  • Ресми әрі екпінді — әдейі қолдан.

activities.prompt: Sort by whether fronting it forces subject–auxiliary inversion.
activities.why: Negative/restrictive adverbials (never, no sooner, not only) trigger inversion; ordinary frequency adverbs don't.

items.s: Never have I seen such courage.
Rarely does she make mistakes.
Never I have seen such courage.
No sooner we had left than it rained.

activities.why: invert the auxiliary: Never have I…; No sooner had we left…

activities.after: such talent in a beginner. (do — I / see, present)
activities.why: no other auxiliary → add ‘do’ and invert → do I see.

activities.after: the door than the phone rang. (I / close)
activities.why: ‘No sooner’ + past perfect inversion → had I closed … than…

activities.after: the exam, but she came top. (she / pass)
activities.why: ‘Not only’ + inversion (add ‘did’) → did she pass.
‘Little’ (negative) + inversion → did they know.

activities.q: Rewrite “She relaxed only after the guests had left.”
options: Only after had the guests left she relaxed.
Only after the guests had left did she relax.
Only after the guests had left she relaxed.
why: the ‘only after’ clause keeps normal order; inversion is in the main clause.
Correct — invert in the main clause → …did she relax.
the main clause still needs inversion → did she relax.

activities.q: Emphatic version of “You must not tell anyone under any circumstances”:
options: Under no circumstances you must tell anyone.
Under no circumstances must you tell anyone.
Under no circumstances you tell anyone must.
why: you must still invert after the fronted negative.
Correct — Under no circumstances must you tell anyone.
word order is broken.

activities.prompt: For each, what comes right after the fronted adverbial?
activities.why: Use the tense's own auxiliary; add do/does/did only for the present/past simple.
‘Hardly’ triggers inversion → “Hardly had she finished when…”

activities.prompt: I have never been so embarrassed.
activities.answer: never have i been so embarrassed
alts: never have i been so embarrassed.
activities.why: Never + inversion → Never have I been…

activities.prompt: I only realised the truth when I read the letter.
activities.answer: not until i read the letter did i realise the truth
alts: not until i read the letter did i realise the truth.
activities.why: Not until … + inversion in the main clause → did I realise.

activities.scene: Choose the emphatic inverted form where it fits.

steps.npc: So how bad was the storm?

options.t: Never had I seen anything like it — no sooner had we docked than the waves crashed over the pier.
options.why: Never + inversion; No sooner … than + past perfect inversion.

options.t: Never I had seen anything — no sooner we docked than the waves crashed.
options.why: invert: Never had I seen…; No sooner had we docked than…

steps.npc: Did people panic?

options.t: Not only did the lights fail, but the radio went dead too.
options.why: Not only + inversion (did the lights fail).

options.t: Not only the lights failed, but the radio went dead.
options.why: Not only + inversion → Not only did the lights fail…

activities.why: When a negative or restrictive adverbial is fronted (never, rarely, hardly … when, no sooner … than, not only, not until, only then, under no circumstances), invert the subject and auxiliary — adding do/does/did if needed — for a formal, emphatic effect.

learn.title: Splitting a sentence to focus
learn.html:
A cleft sentence (‘cleft’ = split) divides one idea into two clauses to put emphasis on a particular element. English has two main types: it-clefts and what-clefts.

Sam paid the bill.neutral
It was Sam who paid the bill.focus on Sam (not someone else)
What Sam paid was the bill.focus on the bill

learn.title: it-clefts
learn.html:
It + be + focus + who/that + clause. You can highlight almost any element — subject, object, time, place, reason:

  • It was in 2020 that we met. (time)
  • It was the noise that woke me. (thing)
  • It's you (who/that) I want to thank. (person, object)

Often used to correct or contrast: It wasn't me who broke it.

learn.title: what-clefts (pseudo-clefts)
learn.html:
What + clause + be + focus. The ‘what’ clause sets up, then the key point lands at the end (the natural stress position):

  • What I need is a holiday.
  • What surprised me was his honesty.
  • What we did was (to) rebook the flight. (focus on an action)

learn.title: Other cleft-like patterns
learn.html:
The thing/reason/place that…: The reason (why) I called was to apologise. · The place where it happened was the car park.

All (that)…: All I want is some peace. (= the only thing). It + be + not until … that: It wasn't until midnight that they arrived.

learn.title: Why writers use clefts
learn.html: Clefts manage information flow: they mark what's new or contrastive, correct a wrong assumption, or build up to a point. They're common in speech (for emphasis) and writing (for structure). The verb be agrees with the focus/tense: It was they who…

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ What I need is to a holiday. → ✓ What I need is a holiday.

✗ It is me who am late. → ✓ It's me who's late. / It is I who am late. (formal)

✗ Is the noise that woke me. → ✓ It was the noise that woke me.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Clefts split a sentence to focus on one element.
  • it-cleft: It + be + focus + who/that + clause (any element, often contrast).
  • what-cleft: What + clause + be + focus (key point at the end).
  • Also: The reason/thing … was; All (that) … is; It wasn't until … that.

ru.title: Разбиваем предложение для фокуса
ru.html: Расщеплённое (cleft) предложение делит одну мысль на две клаузы, чтобы поставить акцент на элементе. Два основных типа: it-cleft и what-cleft. Sam paid the bill → It was Sam who paid the bill (фокус на Sam) / What Sam paid was the bill (фокус на bill).

kk.title: Назар аудару үшін сөйлемді бөлу
kk.html: Cleft сөйлем (‘cleft’ = бөлінген) бір ойды екі сөйлемге бөліп, белгілі элементке екпін қояды. Екі негізгі түрі: it-cleft және what-cleft. Sam paid the bill → It was Sam who paid the bill (Sam-ға назар) / What Sam paid was the bill (bill-ге назар).

ru.title: it-clefts
ru.html: It + be + фокус + who/that + клауза. Можно выделить почти любой элемент: It was in 2020 that we met (время). It was the noise that woke me (вещь). It's you (who/that) I want to thank (лицо, дополнение). Часто для исправления/контраста: It wasn't me who broke it.

kk.title: it-clefts
kk.html: It + be + фокус + who/that + сөйлем. Кез келген элементті бөлектеуге болады: It was in 2020 that we met (уақыт). It was the noise that woke me (зат). It's you (who/that) I want to thank (адам, толықтауыш). Жиі түзету/қарсы қою үшін: It wasn't me who broke it.

ru.title: what-clefts
ru.html: What + клауза + be + фокус. ‘what’-часть готовит, ключевое — в конце (естественное ударение): What I need is a holiday. What surprised me was his honesty. What we did was (to) rebook the flight (фокус на действии).

kk.title: what-clefts
kk.html: What + сөйлем + be + фокус. ‘what’ бөлігі дайындайды, негізгі ой соңында (табиғи екпін): What I need is a holiday. What surprised me was his honesty. What we did was (to) rebook the flight (әрекетке назар).

ru.title: Другие cleft-подобные модели
ru.html: The thing/reason/place that…: The reason (why) I called was to apologise. The place where it happened was the car park. All (that)…: All I want is some peace. It + be + not until … that: It wasn't until midnight that they arrived.

kk.title: Басқа cleft-тәрізді үлгілер
kk.html: The thing/reason/place that…: The reason (why) I called was to apologise. The place where it happened was the car park. All (that)…: All I want is some peace. It + be + not until … that: It wasn't until midnight that they arrived.

ru.title: Зачем их используют
ru.html: Клефты управляют потоком информации: маркируют новое/контрастное, исправляют неверное предположение, подводят к мысли. Часты в речи (акцент) и письме (структура). be согласуется с фокусом/временем: It was they who…

kk.title: Жазушылар неге қолданады
kk.html: Cleft-тер ақпарат ағынын басқарады: жаңа/қарсы нәрсені белгілейді, қате болжамды түзейді, ойды шыңдайды. Сөйлеуде (екпін) және жазуда (құрылым) жиі. be фокус/шақпен үйлеседі: It was they who…

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ What I need is to a holiday → ✓ What I need is a holiday. ✗ It is me who am late → ✓ It's me who's late / It is I who am late (формально). ✗ Is the noise that woke me → ✓ It was the noise that woke me.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ What I need is to a holiday → ✓ What I need is a holiday. ✗ It is me who am late → ✓ It's me who's late / It is I who am late (ресми). ✗ Is the noise that woke me → ✓ It was the noise that woke me.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Клефт делит предложение, чтобы выделить элемент.
  • it-cleft: It + be + фокус + who/that + клауза (любой элемент, часто контраст).
  • what-cleft: What + клауза + be + фокус (ключевое в конце).
  • Также: The reason/thing … was; All (that) … is; It wasn't until … that.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Cleft бір элементке назар аудару үшін сөйлемді бөледі.
  • it-cleft: It + be + фокус + who/that + сөйлем (кез келген элемент, жиі қарсылық).
  • what-cleft: What + сөйлем + be + фокус (негізгі ой соңында).
  • Тағы: The reason/thing … was; All (that) … is; It wasn't until … that.

activities.prompt: Sort each emphatic sentence by its type.

items.t: What I love is the quiet.
It's the ending that ruins it.
What we need is more time.
It was in Rome that we met.

activities.why: it-clefts begin ‘It + be + focus + that/who’; what-clefts begin ‘What + clause + be + focus’.

items.s: It was John who broke the window.
What matters is the result.
What I need is to a break.
Is the traffic that delays us.

activities.why: drop the extra ‘to’ (what I need is a break); it-clefts need ‘It’ (It's the traffic that…).

activities.after: the manager who made the decision. (It / be — past)
activities.why: it-cleft → It was the manager who…

activities.after: annoys me is people being late. (What)
activities.why: what-cleft → What annoys me is…
It wasn't until … that …
what-cleft with the focus at the end.

activities.q: To stress that it was the WEATHER (not the food) that spoiled the trip:
options: What spoiled the trip was the weather.
It was the weather that spoiled the trip.
why: this is valid…
…and so is this.
Correct — both a what-cleft and an it-cleft can foreground ‘the weather’.

activities.q: Complete: “It's the children who ___ making the noise.”
why: the verb in the relative clause agrees with ‘the children’.
Correct — plural focus → are making the noise.
‘am’ is only for ‘I’.

activities.prompt: In each it-cleft, what's being emphasised?

items.t: It was last night that it happened.
It was the smell that gave it away.
It was in Paris that they married.

activities.why: The element between ‘It was’ and ‘that/who’ is the focus.
a what-cleft needs a noun/clause as focus → “What I admire is the way he never gives up” (or ‘…is that he never gives up’).

activities.prompt: My sister sent the letter.
activities.answer: it was my sister who sent the letter
alts: it was my sister who sent the letter.
it was my sister that sent the letter
activities.why: it-cleft → It was my sister who sent the letter.

activities.prompt: He needs a fresh start.
activities.answer: what he needs is a fresh start
alts: what he needs is a fresh start.
activities.why: what-cleft → What he needs is a fresh start.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: setting the record straight
activities.scene: Use clefts to correct and emphasise.

steps.npc: So you missed the deadline?

options.t: Actually, it wasn't me who missed it — it was the courier that was late.
options.why: it-clefts to correct and contrast.

options.t: Actually, wasn't me missed it — was the courier late.
options.why: it-cleft needs ‘It’: It wasn't me who missed it; it was the courier that was late.

steps.npc: What would help you most now?

options.t: What I really need is a clear brief and a realistic deadline.
options.why: what-cleft foregrounds the key request.

options.t: What I really need is to a clear brief.
options.why: drop the extra ‘to’: What I really need is a clear brief.

activities.why: Use it-clefts (It + be + focus + who/that) to highlight or contrast a single element, and what-clefts (What + clause + be + focus) to build up to the key point — managing emphasis and information flow.

learn.title: Moving words for effect & flow
learn.html: Skilled writers don't always keep subject–verb–object order. Fronting moves an element to the start for emphasis or to link smoothly with what came before. Discourse markers organise ideas and signal the writer's stance.

learn.title: Fronting for emphasis
learn.html:
Move an object, complement or adverbial to the front to highlight it (usually no inversion with these, unlike negatives):

  • That film I really enjoyed. (object fronted)
  • Very strange it was, too. (complement)
  • In the corner stood an old clock. (place → inversion with be/intransitives)
  • Down came the rain. (adverb + inversion, literary)

learn.title: Emphasis with do & other devices
learn.html:
Emphatic do/does/did stresses a positive verb: I do like it! · She did warn you.

Other emphasisers: the very / the only / at all / whatsoever: It was the very thing I needed. · No help whatsoever. And reflexives for emphasis: I made it myself.

learn.title: Discourse markers — organising ideas
learn.html:
addingmoreover, furthermore, in addition, what's more
contrastinghowever, nevertheless, on the other hand, then again
resulttherefore, consequently, as a result, hence
attitude / focusfrankly, admittedly, as for…, when it comes to…

learn.title: Spoken discourse markers
learn.html: In speech, markers manage the conversation: well, anyway, actually, mind you, by the way, as I was saying, I mean, you know. They signal a change of topic, a return, a clarification or a softening. Written and spoken markers differ in register — don't mix them carelessly.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ However I disagree. (comma splice) → ✓ … . However, I disagree.

✗ In the corner an old clock stood. (unemphatic) — fine, but for effect: In the corner stood an old clock.

✗ I do liked it. → ✓ I did like it. (do + base verb)

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Front objects/adverbials for emphasis or cohesion (In the corner stood…).
  • Emphatic do/does/did stresses a positive verb (I do care).
  • Discourse markers organise ideas (moreover, however, therefore) and stance (frankly, admittedly).
  • Punctuate markers correctly and match register (spoken vs written).

ru.title: Перемещение слов для эффекта и связности
