ru.html: Умелые авторы не всегда держат порядок подлежащее–сказуемое–дополнение. Fronting перемещает элемент в начало для акцента или связи с предыдущим. Дискурсивные маркеры организуют мысли и выражают позицию.

kk.title: Әсер мен ағын үшін сөздерді жылжыту
kk.html: Шебер жазушылар әрдайым бастауыш–баяндауыш–толықтауыш ретін сақтамайды. Fronting элементті екпін үшін немесе алдыңғымен жалғастыру үшін басына жылжытады. Дискурс маркерлері ойды ұйымдастырады әрі көзқарасты білдіреді.

ru.title: Fronting для акцента
ru.html: Вынос дополнения, именной части или обстоятельства в начало для выделения (обычно без инверсии, в отличие от отрицаний): That film I really enjoyed. Very strange it was, too. In the corner stood an old clock (место → инверсия с be/непереходными). Down came the rain (литературно).

kk.title: Екпін үшін fronting
kk.html: Толықтауышты, баяндауыш толықтауышын не үстеуді басына шығарып, бөлектеу (мұнда әдетте инверсия жоқ, болымсыздардан бөлек): That film I really enjoyed. Very strange it was, too. In the corner stood an old clock (орын → be/сабақсызбен инверсия). Down came the rain (әдеби).

ru.title: Акцент через do и другие средства
ru.html: Эмфатическое do/does/did усиливает положительный глагол: I do like it! She did warn you. Другие усилители: the very / the only / at all / whatsoever: the very thing I needed. No help whatsoever. Возвратные для акцента: I made it myself.

kk.title: do және басқа құралдармен екпін
kk.html: Екпінді do/does/did болымды етістікті күшейтеді: I do like it! She did warn you. Басқа күшейткіштер: the very / the only / at all / whatsoever: the very thing I needed. No help whatsoever. Өздік есімдіктер: I made it myself.

ru.title: Дискурсивные маркеры — упорядочивание
ru.html: Добавление: moreover, furthermore, in addition, what's more. Контраст: however, nevertheless, on the other hand. Результат: therefore, consequently, as a result, hence. Отношение/фокус: frankly, admittedly, as for…, when it comes to…

kk.title: Дискурс маркерлері — ойды реттеу
kk.html: Қосу: moreover, furthermore, in addition, what's more. Қарсы қою: however, nevertheless, on the other hand. Нәтиже: therefore, consequently, as a result, hence. Көзқарас/фокус: frankly, admittedly, as for…, when it comes to…

ru.title: Устные дискурсивные маркеры
ru.html: В речи маркеры управляют беседой: well, anyway, actually, mind you, by the way, as I was saying, I mean, you know. Они сигналят смену темы, возврат, уточнение или смягчение. Письменные и устные различаются по регистру — не смешивайте.

kk.title: Ауызша дискурс маркерлері
kk.html: Сөйлеуде маркерлер әңгімені басқарады: well, anyway, actually, mind you, by the way, as I was saying, I mean, you know. Олар тақырып ауысуын, оралуды, нақтылауды не жұмсартуды білдіреді. Жазба мен ауызша регистрі бөлек — араластырмаңыз.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ However I disagree (ошибка запятой) → ✓ … . However, I disagree. Для эффекта: In the corner stood an old clock. ✗ I do liked it → ✓ I did like it (do + база).

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ However I disagree (үтір қатесі) → ✓ … . However, I disagree. Орын үшін: In the corner stood an old clock. ✗ I do liked it → ✓ I did like it (do + негізгі етістік).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Выносите дополнения/обстоятельства для акцента или связности.
  • Эмфатическое do/does/did усиливает положительный глагол.
  • Дискурсивные маркеры организуют мысли (moreover, however, therefore) и позицию (frankly).
  • Правильно пунктуируйте маркеры и соблюдайте регистр.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Толықтауыш/үстеуді екпін не байланыс үшін алға шығарыңыз.
  • Екпінді do/does/did болымды етістікті күшейтеді.
  • Дискурс маркерлері ойды (moreover, however, therefore) және көзқарасты (frankly) реттейді.
  • Маркерлерді дұрыс белгілеп, регистрге сай қолданыңыз.

activities.typeLabel: What does the discourse marker do?
activities.prompt: Sort each marker by its function.
activities.why: moreover/in addition add; however/nevertheless contrast; consequently shows result; frankly signals attitude.

items.s: I do appreciate your help.
In came the dog, soaking wet.
However I disagree with you.
I do liked the film.

activities.why: ‘However,’ needs a comma (and a full stop/semicolon before it); do + base verb (do like).

activities.before: I know you doubt me, but I
activities.why: emphatic ‘do’ + base verb → do remember.

activities.after: , the company raised its prices. (result marker)
activities.why: a result → Consequently/Therefore/As a result.
place-fronting with an intransitive → inversion → stood a ruined castle.
object fronting for emphasis (no inversion).

activities.q: “The plan is risky. ___, it could transform the business.”
why: ‘therefore’ shows result, not contrast.
Correct — a contrast despite the risk → Nevertheless.
‘as a result’ shows consequence, not contrast.

activities.q: Which fits a formal report best?
options: You know, the results were good.
why: ‘anyway’ is conversational.
Correct — ‘Furthermore’ is a formal, written additive marker.
‘you know’ is a spoken filler.

activities.prompt: Sort each marker by typical register.
activities.why: mind you / by the way are conversational; nevertheless / consequently are formal.
‘however’ can't join two clauses with a comma → “The offer is tempting. However, I need time.”

activities.prompt: I appreciate everything you've done.
activities.answer: i do appreciate everything you've done
alts: i do appreciate everything you have done
activities.why: emphatic do + base verb → I do appreciate…

activities.prompt: I can't stand his attitude.
activities.why: object fronting for emphasis → His attitude I can't stand.

activities.scene: Use emphasis and discourse markers appropriately.

steps.npc: So, should we take the contract?

options.t: It's demanding. However, it would raise our profile; moreover, the fee is generous.
options.why: contrast (However) + addition (moreover), correctly punctuated.

options.t: It's demanding, however it would raise our profile, moreover the fee is generous.
options.why: markers need proper punctuation: … . However, …; … . Moreover, …

steps.npc: You sound keen.

options.t: I am — I do think the benefits outweigh the risks.
options.why: emphatic ‘do’ stresses the verb.

options.t: I am — I do thinking the benefits outweigh the risks.
options.why: emphatic do + base verb: I do think.

activities.why: Front elements (objects, place adverbials) for emphasis or cohesion, stress positive verbs with do/does/did, and use discourse markers to organise ideas and signal stance — punctuating them correctly and matching register.

learn.title: Saying more with less
learn.html:
To avoid clumsy repetition, English lets you leave words out (ellipsis) or replace them with short words (substitution). This is essential for natural, cohesive English.

She can sing, and he can sing too.→ She can sing, and he can too. (ellipsis)
Do you think it'll rain? — I think it'll rain.→ … — I think so. (substitution)

learn.title: Ellipsis — leaving words out
learn.html:
Omit repeated words when the meaning is recoverable, especially after auxiliaries and to:

  • I haven't finished, but she has. (has finished)
  • I'd love to come but I can't (come).
  • He said he would help, and he did.
  • Would you like to? — I'd love to. (to = to come)

learn.title: so & neither/nor — agreeing
learn.html:
To say ‘the same is true of X’, use so + auxiliary + subject (positive) and neither/nor + auxiliary + subject (negative), with inversion:

I'm tired. — So am I.agreement, positive
I can't swim. — Neither can I / Nor can I.agreement, negative

To agree differently: I love jazz. — I don't.

learn.title: do so, one/ones, so & not
learn.html:
• do so / do it / do that replace a whole verb phrase (formal ↓ informal): Please sign here. — I've already done so.
  • one/ones replace countable nouns: the red one, the small ones.
  • so after think/hope/believe/expect/suppose: I think so. · I hope so.
  • not for the negative: I hope not. · I'm afraid not. (Also: I don't think so.)

learn.title: Register & clarity
learn.html: Ellipsis and substitution keep language economical and cohesive. Overusing full repetition sounds childish; but leaving out too much can be unclear. In formal writing, do so is preferred over do it; in speech, shorter forms dominate.

learn.title: Typical learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I'm hungry. — So I am. (means ‘indeed I am’!) → ✓ — So am I. (= me too)

✗ I think yes. → ✓ I think so.

✗ I hope don't. → ✓ I hope not.

✗ Me neither can. → ✓ Neither can I / Me neither.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Ellipsis omits recoverable words (she has __, I can't __, I'd love to __).
  • Agree with so/neither + auxiliary + subject (So do I, Neither can I).
  • Substitute with do so, one/ones, and so/not (I think so, I hope not).
  • ‘So am I’ = me too; ‘So I am’ = indeed I am — different!

ru.title: Сказать больше меньшим
ru.html: Чтобы избежать неуклюжего повтора, английский позволяет опускать слова (эллипсис) или заменять их короткими словами (субституция). Это важно для естественного, связного английского. She can sing, and he can too. Do you think it'll rain? — I think so.

kk.title: Азбен көпті айту
kk.html: Ебедейсіз қайталаудан аулақ болу үшін ағылшын сөздерді түсіруге (эллипсис) немесе қысқа сөзбен алмастыруға (субституция) мүмкіндік береді. Бұл — табиғи, байланысты ағылшын үшін маңызды. She can sing, and he can too. Do you think it'll rain? — I think so.

ru.title: Эллипсис — опущение слов
ru.html: Опускайте повторяющиеся слова, если смысл восстановим, особенно после вспомогательных и to: I haven't finished, but she has. I'd love to come but I can't. He said he would help, and he did. Would you like to? — I'd love to.

kk.title: Эллипсис — сөздерді түсіру
kk.html: Мағына қалпына келсе, қайталанатын сөздерді түсіріңіз, әсіресе көмекшілер мен to-дан кейін: I haven't finished, but she has. I'd love to come but I can't. He said he would help, and he did. Would you like to? — I'd love to.

ru.title: so & neither/nor — согласие
ru.html: Чтобы сказать «то же верно для X»: so + вспомог. + подлежащее (положит.), neither/nor + вспомог. + подлежащее (отриц.), с инверсией: I'm tired. — So am I. I can't swim. — Neither can I / Nor can I. Иначе: I love jazz. — I don't.

kk.title: so & neither/nor — келісу
kk.html: ‘X-те де солай’ деу үшін: so + көмекші + бастауыш (болымды), neither/nor + көмекші + бастауыш (болымсыз), инверсиямен: I'm tired. — So am I. I can't swim. — Neither can I / Nor can I. Басқаша келісу: I love jazz. — I don't.

ru.title: do so, one/ones, so & not
ru.html: do so / do it / do that заменяют всю глагольную группу: I've already done so. one/ones — исчисляемые: the red one, the small ones. После think/hope/believe/expect/suppose — so: I think so. I hope so. Для отрицания — not: I hope not. I'm afraid not.

kk.title: do so, one/ones, so & not
kk.html: do so / do it / do that бүкіл етістік тобын алмастырады: I've already done so. one/ones саналатын зат есімді: the red one, the small ones. think/hope/believe/expect/suppose-тан кейін so: I think so. I hope so. Болымсызға not: I hope not. I'm afraid not.

ru.title: Регистр и ясность
ru.html: Эллипсис и субституция делают речь экономной и связной. Полный повтор звучит по-детски; но слишком много опущений — неясно. В формальном письме предпочтителен do so (не do it); в речи преобладают короткие формы.

kk.title: Регистр және айқындық
kk.html: Эллипсис пен субституция тілді үнемді әрі байланысты етеді. Толық қайталау балаңдай естіледі; бірақ тым көп түсіру түсініксіз. Ресми жазуда do so (do it емес); сөйлеуде қысқа формалар басым.

ru.title: Типичные ошибки
ru.html: ✗ I'm hungry. — So I am (значит «и вправду голоден»!) → ✓ — So am I (я тоже). ✗ I think yes → ✓ I think so. ✗ I hope don't → ✓ I hope not. ✗ Me neither can → ✓ Neither can I / Me neither.

kk.title: Жиі қателер
kk.html: ✗ I'm hungry. — So I am (‘шынымен де ашпын’ дегені!) → ✓ — So am I (мен де). ✗ I think yes → ✓ I think so. ✗ I hope don't → ✓ I hope not. ✗ Me neither can → ✓ Neither can I / Me neither.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Эллипсис опускает восстановимые слова (she has __, I can't __, I'd love to __).
  • Согласие: so/neither + вспомог. + подлежащее (So do I, Neither can I).
  • Субституция: do so, one/ones, so/not (I think so, I hope not).
  • ‘So am I’ = я тоже; ‘So I am’ = и вправду — разное!

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Эллипсис қалпына келетін сөздерді түсіреді (she has __, I can't __, I'd love to __).
  • Келісу: so/neither + көмекші + бастауыш (So do I, Neither can I).
  • Субституция: do so, one/ones, so/not (I think so, I hope not).
  • ‘So am I’ = мен де; ‘So I am’ = шынымен де — бөлек!

activities.prompt: Sort each device.

items.t: He said he would, and he did __.

activities.why: Ellipsis omits recoverable words; substitution replaces them with so/not/one/do so, etc.

items.s: “I'm exhausted.” “So am I.”
“I can't drive.” “Neither can I.”
“I love it.” “So I do.”
“Will it rain?” “I think yes.”

activities.why: agreement → ‘So do I’; and ‘I think so’, not ‘I think yes’.
agreement with a ‘be’ statement → So am I.
negative agreement → Neither/Nor did I.
a negative reply after ‘afraid’ → not.
ellipsis after ‘has’ (= has seen it).

activities.typeLabel: So am I vs So I am
activities.q: “I'm getting hungry.” Which reply means ‘me too’?
why: ‘So I am’ = ‘indeed I am’ (self-agreement), not ‘me too’.
Correct — inversion (So am I) = ‘me too’.
‘I am so’ isn't a natural agreement form.

activities.q: “You should apologise.” “I've already ___.”
why: Correct — ‘done so’ replaces the whole verb phrase (formal/neutral).
‘done it so’ is not a phrase.
‘made so’ is wrong.

activities.prompt: Choose the natural substitute for each.
activities.why: so/not after reporting verbs; one/ones for nouns; did/do so for verb phrases.
to agree with a negative → “Neither do I” (‘So do I’ agrees with a positive).

activities.instruction: Agree with: “I've never been to Japan.”
activities.why: negative agreement → Neither/Nor have I.

activities.instruction: Reply hopefully to: “Do you think we'll make it in time?”
activities.why: positive hope after a yes/no question → I hope so.

activities.scene: Use natural ellipsis and substitution.

steps.npc: I'm not convinced by the new policy.

options.t: Neither am I, to be honest — though management seems to be.
options.why: negative agreement (Neither am I) + substitution (seems to be).

options.t: So am I not, to be honest — though management seems so.
options.why: negative agreement → Neither am I; and ‘seems to be’, not ‘seems so’.

steps.npc: Do you think they'll change it?

options.t: I doubt it, but I hope so.
options.why: substitution: ‘doubt it’ + ‘hope so’.

options.t: I doubt so, but I hope it.
options.why: ‘I doubt it’ (not ‘doubt so’) and ‘I hope so’ (not ‘hope it’).

activities.why: Use ellipsis to omit recoverable words and substitution (so/not, one/ones, do so, so + auxiliary + subject) to avoid repetition — and don't confuse ‘So am I’ (me too) with ‘So I am’ (indeed I am).
